190: // Build the DRY-RUN `dispatchRealSettlement` the M3 settlement leg consumes. It
191: // is shaped EXACTLY like the real receipt-first idempotent dispatch
192: // (`dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`): short-circuit if the settlement receipt
193: // already exists (idempotency), else record the `realBitcoinMoved`-shaped
194: // settlement receipt. The ONLY difference from the real path is that it performs
195: // NO Spark send — it records the receipt and stops. A real dispatch is a
196: // NEEDS-OWNER drop-in here. Fail-soft for the caller (the M3 leg already wraps it
197: // in `orElseSucceed`).
198: export const makeDryRunSettlementDispatch =
199:   (ledger: DryRunSettlementLedger) =>
200:   (input: {
201:     contributorRef: string
202:     settlement: KhalaSettlementRecords
203:   }): Effect.Effect<void, unknown> =>
204:     Effect.gen(function* () {
205:       const existing = yield* Effect.promise(() =>
206:         ledger.readReceiptByRef(input.settlement.settlementReceiptRef),
207:       )
208:       if (existing !== undefined) {
209:         // Already recorded for this run+node => idempotent no-op (never re-pays).
210:         return
211:       }
212:       // DRY-RUN: record the dereferenceable settled receipt; perform NO real Spark
213:       // send. Public-safe diagnostic only (run ref + amount sats + contributor;
214:       // never an address / invoice / preimage).
215:       yield* Effect.logInfo(
216:         workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.dry_run_settlement', {
217:           amountSats: input.settlement.amountSats,
218:           contributorRef: input.contributorRef,
219:           settlementReceiptRef: input.settlement.settlementReceiptRef,
220:         }),
221:       )
222:       yield* Effect.promise(() =>
223:         ledger.recordReceipt(input.settlement.settlementReceipt),
224:       )
225:     })

227: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
228: // (3b) Gated dispatch SELECTOR — REAL Spark send when armed + gate authorizes,
229: //      otherwise the dry-run receipt. The owner's M4 NEEDS_OWNER drop-in.
230: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
231: 
232: // The settlement-dispatch shape the M3 engine consumes (`KhalaSettlementDeps.
233: // dispatchRealSettlement`): receipt-first, idempotent, fail-soft. Both the
234: // dry-run dispatch and a real `dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`-backed dispatch
235: // satisfy this shape, so the selector can pick between them per call without the
236: // engine knowing which path it got.
237: export type KhalaSettlementDispatch = (input: {
238:   contributorRef: string
239:   settlement: KhalaSettlementRecords
240: }) => Effect.Effect<void, unknown>
241: 
242: // Inputs to the gated dispatch selector. `realDispatch` is the REAL Spark send —
243: // at the wiring layer it is `dispatchRealRunSettlementCore` adapted to the
244: // `KhalaSettlementDispatch` shape (resolvers closed over; settlement passed
245: // through). `dryRunDispatch` is `makeDryRunSettlementDispatch` (records a receipt,
246: // NEVER a Spark send). `readGate` reads the owner real-settlement gate
247: // (`OPENAGENTS_REAL_SETTLEMENT_GATE`, default DISABLED). `settlementRunRef` is the
248: // run allowlist key the gate checks (the SAME ref the M3 engine threads as
249: // `KhalaSettlementDeps.settlementRunRef`, so the selector's gate evaluation
250: // matches the engine's GATE 3 exactly).
251: export type KhalaLoopDispatchSelectorInput = Readonly<{
252:   arming: KhalaLoopArming
253:   dryRunDispatch: KhalaSettlementDispatch
254:   readGate: () => TassadarRealSettlementGate
255:   realDispatch: KhalaSettlementDispatch
256:   settlementRunRef: string
257: }>
258: 
259: // Build the loop's settlement dispatch. It resolves to the REAL Spark dispatch
260: // ONLY when BOTH:
261: //   (a) the loop-arming flag is armed (`arming.loopArmed` — the FIRST owner gate);
262: //       AND
263: //   (b) the M3 owner real-settlement gate AUTHORIZES this exact payout
264: //       (`resolveTassadarSettlementAdapter(...).realAuthorized`: gate enabled +
265: //       requested adapter == `spark_treasury` + amount <= `maxPayoutSats` + run
266: //       allowlisted + contributor eligible — the SECOND owner gate).
267: // Otherwise it resolves to the DRY-RUN dispatch (records a receipt, NO real send).
268: //
269: // This is a SECOND, independent fail-closed evaluation of the gate at the dispatch
270: // boundary, layered on top of the M3 engine's own GATE 3 (`settleVerifiedServing-
271: // Payout` only calls `dispatchRealSettlement` after its own `resolveTassadar-
272: // SettlementAdapter` + cap + daily-budget + destination checks pass). So the real
273: // branch is unreachable unless EVERY gate — loop flag, owner gate, per-payout cap,
274: // daily cap, run allowlist, adapter match, registered destination — holds. With
275: // any of them missing/disabled/out-of-bounds the selector routes to the dry-run
276: // path and ZERO sats move. The gate is CONSUMED here, never mutated.
277: //
278: // Receipt-first + idempotent is preserved by construction: both `realDispatch`
279: // (`dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`) and `dryRunDispatch` short-circuit when the
280: // deterministic settlement receipt already exists, so a replay with the same
281: // settlement receipt ref never double-sends regardless of which branch is taken.
282: //
283: // NOTE: `amountSats` is read from the built records (`settlement.amountSats`),
284: // which the engine derived from the per-share floor-to-sat split — the SAME value
285: // the engine's own gate evaluation used, so the two cap checks agree.
286: export const makeKhalaLoopSettlementDispatch = (
287:   input: KhalaLoopDispatchSelectorInput,
288: ): KhalaSettlementDispatch =>
289:   dispatchInput =>
290:     Effect.gen(function* () {
291:       // Evaluate the owner real-settlement gate for THIS payout (pure, fail-closed).
292:       const gate = input.readGate()
293:       const decision = resolveTassadarSettlementAdapter({
294:         amountSats: dispatchInput.settlement.amountSats,
295:         contributorRef: dispatchInput.contributorRef,
296:         gate,
297:         requestedAdapterKind: 'spark_treasury',
298:         trainingRunRef: input.settlementRunRef,
299:       })
300: 
301:       const realRoute = input.arming.loopArmed && decision.realAuthorized
302:       if (!realRoute) {
303:         // Fail-closed default: loop flag OFF, gate disabled, or any bound failed.
304:         // Record a dry-run receipt; perform NO real Spark send. Public-safe
305:         // diagnostic only (refs + neutral blocker reason; never payment material).
306:         yield* Effect.logInfo(
307:           workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.dispatch_dry_run', {
308:             blockedReason: decision.blockedReason,
309:             contributorRef: dispatchInput.contributorRef,
310:             loopArmed: input.arming.loopArmed,
311:             settlementReceiptRef:
312:               dispatchInput.settlement.settlementReceiptRef,
313:           }),
314:         )
315:         yield* input.dryRunDispatch(dispatchInput)
316:         return
317:       }
318: 
319:       // ARMED + AUTHORIZED: route to the REAL Spark dispatch (receipt-first +
320:       // idempotent inside the core). Public-safe diagnostic only.
321:       yield* Effect.logInfo(
322:         workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.dispatch_real', {
323:           amountSats: dispatchInput.settlement.amountSats,
324:           contributorRef: dispatchInput.contributorRef,
325:           eligibilitySource: decision.eligibilitySource,
326:           settlementReceiptRef:
327:             dispatchInput.settlement.settlementReceiptRef,
328:         }),
329:       )
330:       yield* input.realDispatch(dispatchInput)
331:     })
332: 
333: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
334: // (2 cont.) The flagged settlement sink — connects M3 to the metering path
335: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
336: 
337: // The `recordServingPayout` sink the metering hook (`metering-hook.ts`
338: // `LedgerMeteringDeps.recordServingPayout`) forwards an ARMED serving-payout
339: // decision + receipt to. This is the wiring point that turns the dormant M3
340: // `makeKhalaServingSettlementSink` into the metering-path sink — BEHIND THE LOOP
341: // FLAG. With the flag OFF (default) the returned sink is a no-op: it logs that the
342: // loop is disarmed and forwards NOTHING to M3, so the metering hook can carry it
343: // fire-and-forget with zero live-money risk. With the flag ON it delegates to the
344: // M3 sink, which independently re-checks its OWN owner real-settlement gate
345: // (still default OFF) + caps + destination — so arming a real payout requires
346: // BOTH this loop flag AND the M3 gate.
347: export const makeKhalaLoopSettlementSink = (
348:   input: Readonly<{
349:     arming: KhalaLoopArming
350:     settlementDeps: KhalaSettlementDeps
351:   }>,
352: ): ((
353:   decision: ServingNodePayoutDecision,
354:   receipt: ServingReceipt,
355: ) => Effect.Effect<void>) => {
356:   const m3Sink = makeKhalaServingSettlementSink(input.settlementDeps)
357:   return (decision, receipt) =>
358:     Effect.gen(function* () {
359:       if (!input.arming.loopArmed) {
360:         // Loop flag OFF => inert. Public-safe diagnostic (refs only); forward
361:         // nothing to M3.
362:         yield* Effect.logInfo(
363:           workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.disarmed', {
364:             servingRunRef: decision.servingRunRef,
365:           }),
366:         )
367:         return
368:       }
369:       yield* m3Sink(decision, receipt)
370:     })
371: }
372: 
373: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
374: // (3 cont.) The end-to-end loop entrypoint
375: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
376: 
377: // How the served request's revenue was sourced for the RL-3 asset-boundary check
378: // in the payout decision. Bitcoin-only this wave: a Bitcoin-funded request
379: // produces withdrawable Bitcoin serving share. Defaulted by the caller.
380: export type KhalaLoopRevenueAsset = ServingRevenueAsset
381: 
382: export type KhalaLoopOutcome = Readonly<{
383:   // The M4 Pylon admission decision (capability + heartbeat + wallet/payout
384:   // readiness), when an admission snapshot was supplied. null when the caller did
385:   // not gate on admission (e.g. the local/fake test path). When present and NOT
386:   // admitted, the loop refused to route to the Pylon: `served`/`receipt`/
387:   // `decision` are null and `admittedAndServed` is false (safe fall-back to the
388:   // existing cloud adapters happens at the caller).
389:   admission: PylonAdmissionDecision | null
390:   // Whether the Pylon was admitted AND actually served. False when admission
391:   // refused (no Pylon routing) — the honest "fell back to cloud" signal.
392:   admittedAndServed: boolean
393:   // The parity-verified serving receipt the fabric dispatch produced. null when
394:   // admission refused (no serve was attempted).
395:   receipt: ServingReceipt | null
396:   // The full served completion result (content + receipt-first usage). null when
397:   // admission refused.
398:   served: NetworkServedResult | null
399:   // The computed (possibly unarmed) per-stage payout decision. null when
400:   // admission refused (nothing to settle).
401:   decision: ServingNodePayoutDecision | null
402:   // The settlement outcome from the M3 sink. Present only when the loop flag is
403:   // armed AND the decision was armed; otherwise null (the loop short-circuited
404:   // before forwarding to M3). A null outcome with an unarmed decision is the
405:   // honest inert default.
406:   settlement: KhalaSettlementOutcome | null
407:   // Whether the loop forwarded to M3 at all (admitted + served + loop flag armed
408:   // AND decision armed).
409:   forwardedToSettlement: boolean
410: }>
411: 
412: export type KhalaLoopConfig = Readonly<{
413:   // The Pylon serve transport (local/fake in tests, HTTP to a live Pylon later).
414:   transport: PylonServeTransport
415:   // The loop-arming flag (default OFF).
416:   arming: KhalaLoopArming
417:   // The M3 settlement deps (ledger, destination resolver, owner gate, dispatch).
418:   // The wiring layer builds `dispatchRealSettlement` via the GATED selector
419:   // (`makeKhalaLoopSettlementDispatch`): it routes to the REAL Spark dispatch
420:   // (`dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`) when armed + the owner gate authorizes,
421:   // else to the dry-run. A test may pass the dry-run directly to assert the
422:   // money-free path. Either way the M3 engine independently re-checks its own
423:   // gate stack before invoking the dispatch.
424:   settlementDeps: KhalaSettlementDeps
425:   // The contributor cut to split, in integer msat. Derived upstream from the
426:   // priced margin (`servingContributorCutMsat`); the test passes a tiny,
427:   // treasury-bounded value (e.g. 5_000 msat = 5 sats).
428:   contributorCutMsat: number
429:   // The revenue asset for the RL-3 boundary (bitcoin this wave).
430:   revenueAsset: KhalaLoopRevenueAsset
431:   // The owner-armed MDK payout-mode gate the payout DECISION consults (the FIRST
432:   // owner gate; the M3 settlement leg consults the SECOND, independent
433:   // real-settlement gate). Default-disabled keeps the decision unarmed.
434:   payoutGate: MdkPayoutModeGateProjection
435:   // RL-3 resale-authorization refs for the api_inference_gateway_resale lane.
436:   // Required for the decision to ARM; omitted in the honest inert default.
437:   resaleRefs?: Partial<InferenceResaleRefs> | undefined
438:   // M4 ADMISSION GATE (the issue's "capability/registration" task). When present,
439:   // the loop checks the candidate Pylon is ADMITTED (advertises the required
440:   // capability + has a fresh healthy heartbeat + is wallet/payout ready) BEFORE
441:   // dispatching to its transport. A non-admitted Pylon is NOT routed to: the loop
442:   // short-circuits with `admittedAndServed: false` and the caller falls back to
443:   // the existing cloud adapters — never a serve/pay against an unready node. When
444:   // omitted (the local/fake test path), no admission gate runs (admission: null).
445:   admission?:
446:     | Readonly<{
447:         snapshot: PylonServingSnapshot
448:         requiredCapabilityRef: string
449:         nowMs: number
450:         heartbeatTtlMs?: number | undefined
451:       }>
452:     | undefined
453: }>
454: 
455: // Run the loop ONCE for a single inference request: serve via the M4 fabric
456: // dispatch (parity-gated — no parity, no result), compute the per-stage payout
457: // decision from the parity receipt, and run it through the flagged M3 settlement
458: // sink. The serve FAILS CLOSED on a malformed/unverified/sharded serve (the M4
459: // dispatch's typed error propagates). Once a parity-verified receipt is in hand,
460: // the settlement leg is fail-soft + idempotent and never throws into the caller.
461: //
462: // INERT BY DEFAULT: with the loop flag OFF (default) the sink forwards nothing to
463: // M3 (`settlement: null`, `forwardedToSettlement: false`). Even with the loop
464: // flag ON, the decision only arms with the MDK gate + RL-3 refs, and the M3 leg
465: // only settles for real with its OWN owner gate armed + caps + a registered
466: // destination. No real sats move unless EVERY gate is armed; the dry-run dispatch
467: // records a receipt without a real Spark send.
468: export const runKhalaLoopOnce = (
469:   config: KhalaLoopConfig,
470:   request: InferenceRequest,
471: ): Effect.Effect<KhalaLoopOutcome, unknown> =>
472:   Effect.gen(function* () {
473:     // M4 ADMISSION GATE (capability + heartbeat + wallet/payout readiness). When
474:     // an admission snapshot is supplied, decide whether the candidate Pylon is
475:     // admitted BEFORE touching the transport. A non-admitted Pylon is NOT routed
476:     // to: short-circuit with no serve, no receipt, no decision, no settlement —
477:     // the caller falls back to the existing cloud adapters. PURE + fail-closed.
478:     const admission =
479:       config.admission === undefined
480:         ? null
481:         : decidePylonAdmission({
482:             nowMs: config.admission.nowMs,
483:             requiredCapabilityRef: config.admission.requiredCapabilityRef,
484:             snapshot: config.admission.snapshot,
485:             ...(config.admission.heartbeatTtlMs === undefined
486:               ? {}
487:               : { heartbeatTtlMs: config.admission.heartbeatTtlMs }),
488:           })
489: 
490:     if (admission !== null && !admission.admitted) {
491:       yield* Effect.logInfo(
492:         workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.pylon_not_admitted', {
493:           blockerCount: admission.blockerRefs.length,
494:           pylonRef: admission.pylonRef,
495:         }),
496:       )
497:       return {
498:         admission,
499:         admittedAndServed: false,
500:         decision: null,
501:         forwardedToSettlement: false,
502:         receipt: null,
503:         served: null,
504:         settlement: null,
505:       }
506:     }
507: 
508:     // M4: serve through the parity-gated fabric dispatch. We need the RECEIPT
509:     // (not just the completion), so we call the dispatch directly rather than
510:     // through the InferenceProviderAdapter (which surfaces only the result).
511:     const served = yield* dispatchPsionicServe(
512:       fabricConfigForPylon(config.transport),
513:     )(request)
514:     const receipt = served.receipt
515: 
516:     // Compute the per-stage payout DECISION from the parity receipt. PURE +
517:     // owner-armed-gated: armed only with the MDK gate AND the RL-3 ref chain.
518:     const decision = decideServingNodePayout({
519:       contributorCutMsat: config.contributorCutMsat,
520:       payoutGate: config.payoutGate,
521:       receipt,
522:       revenueAsset: config.revenueAsset,
523:       ...(config.resaleRefs === undefined
524:         ? {}
525:         : { resaleRefs: config.resaleRefs }),
526:     })
527: 
528:     yield* Effect.logInfo(
529:       workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.served', {
530:         armed: decision.armed,
531:         loopArmed: config.arming.loopArmed,
532:         parityVerified: receipt.parityVerified,
533:         servingRunRef: decision.servingRunRef,
534:         stageCount: receipt.stages.length,
535:       }),
536:     )
537: 
538:     // Forward to the flagged M3 sink only when the loop flag is armed AND the
539:     // decision is armed (mirrors the metering hook's own armed-only forwarding).
540:     const forwardedToSettlement = config.arming.loopArmed && decision.armed
541:     if (!forwardedToSettlement) {
542:       return {
543:         admission,
544:         admittedAndServed: true,
545:         decision,
546:         forwardedToSettlement: false,
547:         receipt,
548:         served,
549:         settlement: null,
550:       }
551:     }
552: 
553:     // Settle (dry-run): the M3 leg builds + records the realBitcoinMoved-shaped
554:     // receipt through the (dry-run) dispatch. We call the M3 entrypoint via the
555:     // flagged sink wrapper so the contract stays the metering-hook sink shape,
556:     // then re-derive the outcome by running the same leg directly so the caller
557:     // sees the structured settlement outcome (the sink itself returns void to fit
558:     // the fire-and-forget metering contract).
559:     const sink = makeKhalaLoopSettlementSink({
560:       arming: config.arming,
561:       settlementDeps: config.settlementDeps,
562:     })
563:     // Run the void sink for its side effects (the actual ledger write/dispatch),
564:     // proving the metering-hook-shaped path fires; this performs the dry-run
565:     // record.
566:     yield* sink(decision, receipt)
567:     // Then obtain the STRUCTURED outcome for the caller by running the same M3
568:     // leg again. The settlement leg is idempotent (the receipt now exists), so
569:     // this second call records nothing new and simply reports the leg outcomes.
570:     const settlement = yield* settleVerifiedServingPayout(config.settlementDeps, {
571:       decision,
572:       parityVerified: receipt.parityVerified,
573:       servedModel: receipt.servedModel,
574:     })
575: 
576:     return {
577:       admission,
578:       admittedAndServed: true,
579:       decision,
580:       forwardedToSettlement: true,
581:       receipt,
582:       served,
583:       settlement,
584:     }
585:   })
586: 
587: // Re-export the contributor-cut helper so a wiring layer derives the cut with the
588: // SAME function the metering path uses (no parallel cut math).
589: export { servingContributorCutMsat }

`/wallet-readiness`, `/payout-target-admission`, `/assignments/*`, and
`/register`) are authenticated with an OpenAgents agent **bearer token**
(`Authorization: Bearer <agent token>`) in `workers/api/src/pylon-api-routes.ts`
(`requireAgent`). This is intentional and is the documented contract.

A node's self-held Nostr key (NIP-98) is **not** accepted as presence
authority. Registrations are bound to `ownerAgentUserId` derived from the
bearer-token session; the registry does not bind a verified Nostr pubkey to
that owner (`providerNostrPubkey` is optional discovery metadata, only set for
NIP-90 provider Pylons, and is never verified server-side). Accepting a
self-signed heartbeat would require server-side NIP-98 schnorr verification
plus a mandatory pubkey→owner binding — a broader auth change that must not be
made implicitly.

When a presence request arrives with a `Nostr`/NIP-98 `Authorization` scheme
and no usable bearer token, the route returns a typed, explanatory `401`
(`error: pylon_api_presence_requires_agent_token`, `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`)
naming the token-only contract and pointing the node at the bearer path,
rather than a bare `unauthorized` (#5058). Do not silently fall back to a bare
401 for the Nostr-signed presence path. If self-signed presence is ever to be
supported, design the NIP-98 verification + pubkey binding deliberately with a
new dated review and tests; do not broaden any other authority (spend,
settlement, moderation) in the process. Regression coverage lives in
`workers/api/src/pylon-api-routes.test.ts`.

## Foldkit Patterns

### Update

`init` and `update` both return `[Model, ReadonlyArray<Command<Message>>]`:

```ts
type UpdateReturn = readonly [Model, ReadonlyArray<Command<Message>>]
const withUpdateReturn = M.withReturnType<UpdateReturn>()

const update = (model: Model, message: Message): UpdateReturn =>
  M.value(message).pipe(
    withUpdateReturn,
    M.tagsExhaustive({
      ClickedIncrement: () => [evo(model, { count: count => count + 1 }), []],
    }),
  )
```

Use `evo()` from `foldkit/struct` for immutable model updates. Never spread or `Object.assign`.

### View

Bind the `html` factory inside each view function with `const h = html<Message>()` (never at module level), then reach for `h.div`, `h.OnClick`, etc. off the returned record. Use `empty` (not `null`) for conditional rendering, `M.value().pipe(M.tagsExhaustive({...}))` for discriminated unions, and `Array.match` for lists that may be empty.

Use `keyed` wrappers whenever the view branches into structurally different layouts based on route or model state. Without keying, the virtual DOM tries to diff one layout into another, causing stale DOM and event handler mismatches.

### Commands

Define a Command with `Command.define`, which is curried: the first call binds the name (and optionally args + result Message schemas), and the second call binds the Effect. Assign definitions to PascalCase constants. Never inline in pipe chains. Commands catch all errors via `Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(FailedX(...)))` so side effects never crash the app. Definitions live colocated with the update function that returns them.

For the with-args shape, see `../projects/repos/foldkit/examples/weather/src/main.ts` or `../projects/repos/foldkit/examples/kanban/src/command.ts`. For an argless DOM-side-effect Command, the argless form in `kanban/src/command.ts` (`FocusAddCardInput`) is the canonical reference.

For DOM operations (focus, scroll, modals, scroll lock), Foldkit ships a `Dom` module. For time, randomness, UUIDs, and delays, use Effect's built-ins directly (`Clock`, `Random`, `Effect.uuid`, `Effect.sleep`). Don't reach for raw `document.querySelector`, `setTimeout`, `Date.now()`, or `Math.random()`.

### File Organization

This repo is a Bun workspace:

- `apps/web/` is the Foldkit/Vite browser app.
- `workers/api/` is the Cloudflare Worker API and Durable Object surface.
- `packages/sync-schema/` holds Effect Schema protocol models shared by browser and Worker code.
- `packages/sync-client/` holds browser-side sync helpers.
- `packages/sync-worker/` holds Worker-side sync helpers.

Keep Cloudflare runtime code out of `apps/web/`. Keep browser/Foldkit code out
of `workers/api/`. Shared code belongs in `packages/*` and must stay Effect
Schema-first at every external boundary.

A Foldkit app lives in two files. `src/main.ts` holds the pure definitions (Model, Messages, init, update, view). `src/entry.ts` imports them and boots the runtime with `Runtime.makeProgram` and `Runtime.run`. `index.html` references `entry.ts`. The split keeps `main.ts` importable from tests without booting a runtime as a side effect. Never call `Runtime.run` from `main.ts`.

Use uppercase section headers (`// MODEL`, `// MESSAGE`, `// INIT`, `// UPDATE`, `// COMMAND`, `// VIEW`) for wayfinding.

### Testing

Test update functions with `foldkit/test`. Since update is pure, tests run without a runtime, DOM, or side effects. Use `Story.story` for update-level tests (send Messages, assert on Model and Commands) and `Scene.scene` for feature-level testing through the view with accessible locators. Study the `.story.test.ts` and `.scene.test.ts` files in `../projects/repos/foldkit/examples/` when the external Foldkit reference exists.

## Code Style

- Encode state in discriminated unions, not booleans or nullable fields. `Idle | Loading | Error | Ok`, not `isLoading: boolean`. Make impossible states unrepresentable.
- Use `Option` instead of `null` or `undefined`. Prefix Option-typed values with `maybe*`. Match with `Option.match`; don't unwrap with `Option.map(...)` + `Option.getOrElse(...)` when you can just match.
- Use Effect modules over native methods in `pipe` chains (`Array.map`, `String.startsWith`, `Array.findFirst`). Native methods are fine when calling directly on a named variable.
- Never cast Schema values with `as Type`. Use the callable constructor: `SucceededLogin({ sessionId })`, not `{ _tag: 'SucceededLogin', sessionId } as Message`.
- Always `Array.isEmptyArray` / `Array.isNonEmptyArray` (not `.length === 0`). Use `Array.match` when handling both empty and non-empty cases.
- Never use `for` loops or `let` for iteration. Reach for `Array.map`, `Array.filterMap`, `Array.makeBy`, `Array.reduce`.
- Never use `T[]`. Always `Array<T>` or `ReadonlyArray<T>`.
- Always use `Effect.Match`, never `switch`.
- Always use braces for control flow: `if (foo) { return true }`.
- Don't add inline comments to explain code. Use better names instead. Reserve `// NOTE:` for behavior that would mislead a careful reader.

## Message Layout

Group all `m()` declarations together with no blank lines between them, then put `S.Union([...])` and `type Message = typeof Message.Type` on adjacent lines:

```ts
const ClickedSubmit = m('ClickedSubmit')
const UpdatedEmail = m('UpdatedEmail', { value: S.String })

const Message = S.Union([ClickedSubmit, UpdatedEmail])
type Message = typeof Message.Type
```

Messages are verb-first past-tense. Common prefixes: `Clicked*`, `Updated*` (input changes and external state updates), `Submitted*`, `Pressed*`, `Selected*`, `Succeeded*` / `Failed*` (paired async results), `Completed*` (fire-and-forget), `Got*` (child OutMessage in the Submodel pattern).

## Debugging

This project ships with `@foldkit/devtools-mcp` pre-wired. When the dev server is running and the app is open in a browser, `foldkit_*` MCP tools let you inspect Model, Message history, and time-travel. Reach for them before adding `console.log` whenever the question is about state or Message flow.

## Going Deeper

For Submodels and OutMessage, Subscriptions, Mount / ManagedResource / CustomElement, field validation, routing, accessibility, and the full convention set, read the live Foldkit code in `../projects/repos/foldkit/`. The `examples/` directory and the production apps (`packages/typing-game/`, `packages/website/`) are the highest-fidelity references for any specific pattern. The `foldkit-skills` plugin's `generate-program` and `audit-program` skills carry written snapshot guides if you want a structured walkthrough.

//     is the M4 NEEDS-OWNER "swap the dry-run for the real dispatch" step, done
//     fail-closed: the real branch is a SECOND, independent gate evaluation layered
//     on top of the M3 engine's own gate, and is unreachable without the owner's
//     explicit gate JSON + the loop flag. The wiring layer hands the selector a
//     real dispatch (the core, closed over its Spark resolvers) and the dry-run;

//   - `makeKhalaLoopSettlementSink`: connects the dormant
//     `makeKhalaServingSettlementSink` (M3) to the metering-hook
//     `recordServingPayout` shape, BEHIND A FLAG (`KhalaLoopArming`), default
//     OFF -> the sink is a no-op and nothing settles.
//   - `runKhalaLoopOnce`: the end-to-end integration entrypoint — serve via the
//     fabric dispatch, take the parity receipt, compute the payout decision,
//     and run it through the settlement sink. The whole thing is inert until BOTH
//     the loop-arming flag AND the M3 owner real-settlement gate are armed.
//
// It does NOT edit Lane B's adapter registry (`provider-adapter.ts` / the
// `index.ts` registration) or Lane E's `metering-hook.ts` internals — it builds a
// sink the metering hook can be handed, and a transport the fabric adapter
// consumes, both as new owned surfaces.
//
// SAFETY: real-money discipline is unchanged. The M3 settlement leg keeps its
// own fail-closed gates (parity, asset boundary, owner real-settlement gate,
// per-payout cap, daily ceiling, registered destination). This module adds a
// SECOND, independent default-OFF flag in front of the sink, so arming requires
// (a) this loop flag AND (b) the M3 owner gate. With either OFF the loop is fully
// inert. No raw Spark address / invoice / preimage ever enters this module: the
// guinea-pig Pylon's address is resolved at the wiring/test layer from the
// gitignored `.secrets/khala-test-payout.env`, NEVER hard-coded or committed.

import { Effect } from 'effect'

import type { NexusPaymentAuthorityReceiptRecord } from '../nexus-treasury-payout-ledger'
import { workerLogEntry } from '../observability'
import {
  type ServingReceipt,
  type NetworkServedResult,
} from './openagents-network-adapter'
import {
  type PsionicServeTransport,
  type PsionicFabricServeConfig,
  dispatchPsionicServe,
} from './psionic-fabric-serve'
import {
  type KhalaSettlementDeps,
  type KhalaSettlementOutcome,
  type KhalaSettlementRecords,
  makeKhalaServingSettlementSink,
  settleVerifiedServingPayout,
} from './khala-verified-work-settlement'
import { type InferenceRequest } from './provider-adapter'
import {
  type ServingNodePayoutDecision,
  type ServingRevenueAsset,
  decideServingNodePayout,
  servingContributorCutMsat,
} from './serving-node-payout'
import { type InferenceResaleRefs } from '../inference-resale-authorization'
import { type MdkPayoutModeGateProjection } from '../mdk-payout-mode-gate'
import {
  type PylonAdmissionDecision,
  type PylonServingSnapshot,
  decidePylonAdmission,
} from './khala-pylon-admission'
import {
  type TassadarRealSettlementGate,
  resolveTassadarSettlementAdapter,
} from '../tassadar-run-settlement-gate'

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// (1) Pluggable Pylon transport — the seam a real Pylon drops into
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// The transport the fabric adapter consumes is the M4 `PsionicServeTransport`
// (ask-plan -> execute -> serve response). A "Pylon transport" is just a
// `PsionicServeTransport` produced by some builder: a test passes a LOCAL/FAKE
// serve; a real wiring passes an HTTP client posting to a live online Pylon's
// `psionic-serve` endpoint (the guinea-pig Pylon FIRST). Re-exported under a
// product-named alias so the loop's wiring layer talks "Pylon", while the wire
// contract stays the single M4 type (no parallel transport surface).
export type PylonServeTransport = PsionicServeTransport

// A builder that produces the Pylon transport from its connection inputs. A real
// builder reads the guinea-pig Pylon's endpoint + (out of band) its Spark
// receive address binding; a test builder returns a fixed fake serve. Kept as a
// thin factory type so the live transport is a pure drop-in: the loop only ever
// holds a `PylonServeTransport`, never the connection details.
export type PylonServeTransportBuilder = (
  input: Readonly<{
    // Stable, public-safe ref of the Pylon to serve against (e.g. the guinea-pig
    // node ref). The transport binds this to its endpoint internally; the loop
    // never sees the raw endpoint or address.
    pylonNodeRef: string
  }>,
) => PylonServeTransport

// Build the fabric serve config the M4 dispatch consumes from a Pylon transport.
// This is the one place a Pylon transport becomes a fabric dispatch input, so a
// real Pylon transport drops in with no change to the dispatch or the loop.
export const fabricConfigForPylon = (
  transport: PylonServeTransport,
): PsionicFabricServeConfig => ({ transport })

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// (2) The loop-arming flag — a SECOND default-OFF gate in front of the sink
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// The Worker env key for the loop-arming flag. Default OFF: absent / anything
// other than the explicit on-token keeps the loop fully inert (the settlement
// sink is a no-op). This is independent of, and additional to, the M3 owner
// real-settlement gate (`OPENAGENTS_REAL_SETTLEMENT_GATE`): arming a real payout
// requires BOTH. NEEDS-OWNER to flip on a staging/preview Worker.
export const KhalaLoopArmingEnvKey = 'OPENAGENTS_KHALA_LOOP_ARMED'

// The single on-token. Anything else (including 'true', '1', '') is OFF — the
// flag must be set EXACTLY to this opt-in value, so a stray truthy env never
// arms the loop by accident. Fails closed.
const KHALA_LOOP_ARMED_ON_TOKEN = 'armed'

export type KhalaLoopArming = Readonly<{
  // Whether the loop-arming flag authorizes the settlement sink to run at all.
  // OFF (default) => the sink is a no-op and no decision is forwarded to M3.
  loopArmed: boolean
}>

export const disabledKhalaLoopArming: KhalaLoopArming = { loopArmed: false }

// Resolve the loop-arming flag from the Worker env. Fails CLOSED: absent,
// non-string, or any value other than the exact on-token yields the disabled
// arming. Mirrors the M3 gate's fail-closed env discipline.
export const readKhalaLoopArming = (
  env: Readonly<Record<string, unknown>>,
): KhalaLoopArming => {
  const raw = env[KhalaLoopArmingEnvKey]
  return typeof raw === 'string' && raw.trim() === KHALA_LOOP_ARMED_ON_TOKEN
    ? { loopArmed: true }
    : disabledKhalaLoopArming
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// (3) Dry-run settlement dispatch — produces a receipt, NEVER a real Spark send
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// A minimal ledger sink the dry-run dispatch writes the settled-shaped receipt
// to, so the loop produces a DEREFERENCEABLE receipt without a real money send.
// The wiring layer passes a real ledger store (the same `NexusTreasuryPayoutLedgerStore`
// the M3 deps already carry); a test passes an in-memory map. Idempotent: a
// replay with the same receipt ref is a no-op (never double-records).
export type DryRunSettlementLedger = Readonly<{
  readReceiptByRef: (
    ref: string,
  ) => Promise<NexusPaymentAuthorityReceiptRecord | undefined>
  recordReceipt: (record: NexusPaymentAuthorityReceiptRecord) => Promise<void>
}>

// Build the DRY-RUN `dispatchRealSettlement` the M3 settlement leg consumes. It
// is shaped EXACTLY like the real receipt-first idempotent dispatch
// (`dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`): short-circuit if the settlement receipt
// already exists (idempotency), else record the `realBitcoinMoved`-shaped
// settlement receipt. The ONLY difference from the real path is that it performs
// NO Spark send — it records the receipt and stops. A real dispatch is a
// NEEDS-OWNER drop-in here. Fail-soft for the caller (the M3 leg already wraps it
// in `orElseSucceed`).
export const makeDryRunSettlementDispatch =
  (ledger: DryRunSettlementLedger) =>
  (input: {
    contributorRef: string
    settlement: KhalaSettlementRecords
  }): Effect.Effect<void, unknown> =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      const existing = yield* Effect.promise(() =>
        ledger.readReceiptByRef(input.settlement.settlementReceiptRef),
      )
      if (existing !== undefined) {
        // Already recorded for this run+node => idempotent no-op (never re-pays).
        return
      }
      // DRY-RUN: record the dereferenceable settled receipt; perform NO real Spark
      // send. Public-safe diagnostic only (run ref + amount sats + contributor;
      // never an address / invoice / preimage).
      yield* Effect.logInfo(
        workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.dry_run_settlement', {
          amountSats: input.settlement.amountSats,
          contributorRef: input.contributorRef,
          settlementReceiptRef: input.settlement.settlementReceiptRef,
        }),
      )
      yield* Effect.promise(() =>
        ledger.recordReceipt(input.settlement.settlementReceipt),
      )
    })

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// (3b) Gated dispatch SELECTOR — REAL Spark send when armed + gate authorizes,
//      otherwise the dry-run receipt. The owner's M4 NEEDS_OWNER drop-in.
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// The settlement-dispatch shape the M3 engine consumes (`KhalaSettlementDeps.
// dispatchRealSettlement`): receipt-first, idempotent, fail-soft. Both the
// dry-run dispatch and a real `dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`-backed dispatch
// satisfy this shape, so the selector can pick between them per call without the
// engine knowing which path it got.
export type KhalaSettlementDispatch = (input: {
  contributorRef: string
  settlement: KhalaSettlementRecords
}) => Effect.Effect<void, unknown>

// Inputs to the gated dispatch selector. `realDispatch` is the REAL Spark send —
// at the wiring layer it is `dispatchRealRunSettlementCore` adapted to the
// `KhalaSettlementDispatch` shape (resolvers closed over; settlement passed
// through). `dryRunDispatch` is `makeDryRunSettlementDispatch` (records a receipt,
// NEVER a Spark send). `readGate` reads the owner real-settlement gate
// (`OPENAGENTS_REAL_SETTLEMENT_GATE`, default DISABLED). `settlementRunRef` is the
// run allowlist key the gate checks (the SAME ref the M3 engine threads as
// `KhalaSettlementDeps.settlementRunRef`, so the selector's gate evaluation
// matches the engine's GATE 3 exactly).
export type KhalaLoopDispatchSelectorInput = Readonly<{
  arming: KhalaLoopArming
  dryRunDispatch: KhalaSettlementDispatch
  readGate: () => TassadarRealSettlementGate
  realDispatch: KhalaSettlementDispatch
  settlementRunRef: string
}>

// Build the loop's settlement dispatch. It resolves to the REAL Spark dispatch
// ONLY when BOTH:
//   (a) the loop-arming flag is armed (`arming.loopArmed` — the FIRST owner gate);
//       AND
//   (b) the M3 owner real-settlement gate AUTHORIZES this exact payout
//       (`resolveTassadarSettlementAdapter(...).realAuthorized`: gate enabled +
//       requested adapter == `spark_treasury` + amount <= `maxPayoutSats` + run
//       allowlisted + contributor eligible — the SECOND owner gate).
// Otherwise it resolves to the DRY-RUN dispatch (records a receipt, NO real send).
//
// This is a SECOND, independent fail-closed evaluation of the gate at the dispatch
// boundary, layered on top of the M3 engine's own GATE 3 (`settleVerifiedServing-
// Payout` only calls `dispatchRealSettlement` after its own `resolveTassadar-
// SettlementAdapter` + cap + daily-budget + destination checks pass). So the real
// branch is unreachable unless EVERY gate — loop flag, owner gate, per-payout cap,
// daily cap, run allowlist, adapter match, registered destination — holds. With
// any of them missing/disabled/out-of-bounds the selector routes to the dry-run
// path and ZERO sats move. The gate is CONSUMED here, never mutated.
//
// Receipt-first + idempotent is preserved by construction: both `realDispatch`
// (`dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`) and `dryRunDispatch` short-circuit when the
// deterministic settlement receipt already exists, so a replay with the same
// settlement receipt ref never double-sends regardless of which branch is taken.
//
// NOTE: `amountSats` is read from the built records (`settlement.amountSats`),
// which the engine derived from the per-share floor-to-sat split — the SAME value
// the engine's own gate evaluation used, so the two cap checks agree.
export const makeKhalaLoopSettlementDispatch = (
  input: KhalaLoopDispatchSelectorInput,
): KhalaSettlementDispatch =>
  dispatchInput =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      // Evaluate the owner real-settlement gate for THIS payout (pure, fail-closed).
      const gate = input.readGate()
      const decision = resolveTassadarSettlementAdapter({
        amountSats: dispatchInput.settlement.amountSats,
        contributorRef: dispatchInput.contributorRef,
        gate,
        requestedAdapterKind: 'spark_treasury',
        trainingRunRef: input.settlementRunRef,
      })

      const realRoute = input.arming.loopArmed && decision.realAuthorized
      if (!realRoute) {
        // Fail-closed default: loop flag OFF, gate disabled, or any bound failed.
        // Record a dry-run receipt; perform NO real Spark send. Public-safe
        // diagnostic only (refs + neutral blocker reason; never payment material).
        yield* Effect.logInfo(
          workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.dispatch_dry_run', {
            blockedReason: decision.blockedReason,
            contributorRef: dispatchInput.contributorRef,
            loopArmed: input.arming.loopArmed,
            settlementReceiptRef:
              dispatchInput.settlement.settlementReceiptRef,
          }),
        )
        yield* input.dryRunDispatch(dispatchInput)
        return
      }

      // ARMED + AUTHORIZED: route to the REAL Spark dispatch (receipt-first +
      // idempotent inside the core). Public-safe diagnostic only.
      yield* Effect.logInfo(
        workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.dispatch_real', {
          amountSats: dispatchInput.settlement.amountSats,
          contributorRef: dispatchInput.contributorRef,
          eligibilitySource: decision.eligibilitySource,
          settlementReceiptRef:
            dispatchInput.settlement.settlementReceiptRef,
        }),
      )
      yield* input.realDispatch(dispatchInput)
    })

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// (2 cont.) The flagged settlement sink — connects M3 to the metering path
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// The `recordServingPayout` sink the metering hook (`metering-hook.ts`
// `LedgerMeteringDeps.recordServingPayout`) forwards an ARMED serving-payout
// decision + receipt to. This is the wiring point that turns the dormant M3
// `makeKhalaServingSettlementSink` into the metering-path sink — BEHIND THE LOOP
// FLAG. With the flag OFF (default) the returned sink is a no-op: it logs that the
// loop is disarmed and forwards NOTHING to M3, so the metering hook can carry it
// fire-and-forget with zero live-money risk. With the flag ON it delegates to the
// M3 sink, which independently re-checks its OWN owner real-settlement gate
// (still default OFF) + caps + destination — so arming a real payout requires
// BOTH this loop flag AND the M3 gate.
export const makeKhalaLoopSettlementSink = (
  input: Readonly<{
    arming: KhalaLoopArming
    settlementDeps: KhalaSettlementDeps
  }>,
): ((
  decision: ServingNodePayoutDecision,
  receipt: ServingReceipt,
) => Effect.Effect<void>) => {
  const m3Sink = makeKhalaServingSettlementSink(input.settlementDeps)
  return (decision, receipt) =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      if (!input.arming.loopArmed) {
        // Loop flag OFF => inert. Public-safe diagnostic (refs only); forward
        // nothing to M3.
        yield* Effect.logInfo(
          workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.disarmed', {
            servingRunRef: decision.servingRunRef,
          }),
        )
        return
      }
      yield* m3Sink(decision, receipt)
    })
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// (3 cont.) The end-to-end loop entrypoint
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// How the served request's revenue was sourced for the RL-3 asset-boundary check
// in the payout decision. Bitcoin-only this wave: a Bitcoin-funded request
// produces withdrawable Bitcoin serving share. Defaulted by the caller.
export type KhalaLoopRevenueAsset = ServingRevenueAsset

export type KhalaLoopOutcome = Readonly<{
  // The M4 Pylon admission decision (capability + heartbeat + wallet/payout
  // readiness), when an admission snapshot was supplied. null when the caller did
  // not gate on admission (e.g. the local/fake test path). When present and NOT
  // admitted, the loop refused to route to the Pylon: `served`/`receipt`/
  // `decision` are null and `admittedAndServed` is false (safe fall-back to the
  // existing cloud adapters happens at the caller).
  admission: PylonAdmissionDecision | null
  // Whether the Pylon was admitted AND actually served. False when admission
  // refused (no Pylon routing) — the honest "fell back to cloud" signal.
  admittedAndServed: boolean
  // The parity-verified serving receipt the fabric dispatch produced. null when
  // admission refused (no serve was attempted).
  receipt: ServingReceipt | null
  // The full served completion result (content + receipt-first usage). null when
  // admission refused.
  served: NetworkServedResult | null
  // The computed (possibly unarmed) per-stage payout decision. null when
  // admission refused (nothing to settle).
  decision: ServingNodePayoutDecision | null
  // The settlement outcome from the M3 sink. Present only when the loop flag is
  // armed AND the decision was armed; otherwise null (the loop short-circuited
  // before forwarding to M3). A null outcome with an unarmed decision is the
  // honest inert default.
  settlement: KhalaSettlementOutcome | null
  // Whether the loop forwarded to M3 at all (admitted + served + loop flag armed
  // AND decision armed).
  forwardedToSettlement: boolean
}>

export type KhalaLoopConfig = Readonly<{
  // The Pylon serve transport (local/fake in tests, HTTP to a live Pylon later).
  transport: PylonServeTransport
  // The loop-arming flag (default OFF).
  arming: KhalaLoopArming
  // The M3 settlement deps (ledger, destination resolver, owner gate, dispatch).
  // The wiring layer builds `dispatchRealSettlement` via the GATED selector
  // (`makeKhalaLoopSettlementDispatch`): it routes to the REAL Spark dispatch
  // (`dispatchRealRunSettlementCore`) when armed + the owner gate authorizes,
  // else to the dry-run. A test may pass the dry-run directly to assert the
  // money-free path. Either way the M3 engine independently re-checks its own
  // gate stack before invoking the dispatch.
  settlementDeps: KhalaSettlementDeps
  // The contributor cut to split, in integer msat. Derived upstream from the
  // priced margin (`servingContributorCutMsat`); the test passes a tiny,
  // treasury-bounded value (e.g. 5_000 msat = 5 sats).
  contributorCutMsat: number
  // The revenue asset for the RL-3 boundary (bitcoin this wave).
  revenueAsset: KhalaLoopRevenueAsset
  // The owner-armed MDK payout-mode gate the payout DECISION consults (the FIRST
  // owner gate; the M3 settlement leg consults the SECOND, independent
  // real-settlement gate). Default-disabled keeps the decision unarmed.
  payoutGate: MdkPayoutModeGateProjection
  // RL-3 resale-authorization refs for the api_inference_gateway_resale lane.
  // Required for the decision to ARM; omitted in the honest inert default.
  resaleRefs?: Partial<InferenceResaleRefs> | undefined
  // M4 ADMISSION GATE (the issue's "capability/registration" task). When present,
  // the loop checks the candidate Pylon is ADMITTED (advertises the required
  // capability + has a fresh healthy heartbeat + is wallet/payout ready) BEFORE
  // dispatching to its transport. A non-admitted Pylon is NOT routed to: the loop
  // short-circuits with `admittedAndServed: false` and the caller falls back to
  // the existing cloud adapters — never a serve/pay against an unready node. When
  // omitted (the local/fake test path), no admission gate runs (admission: null).
  admission?:
    | Readonly<{
        snapshot: PylonServingSnapshot
        requiredCapabilityRef: string
        nowMs: number
        heartbeatTtlMs?: number | undefined
      }>
    | undefined
}>

// Run the loop ONCE for a single inference request: serve via the M4 fabric
// dispatch (parity-gated — no parity, no result), compute the per-stage payout
// decision from the parity receipt, and run it through the flagged M3 settlement
// sink. The serve FAILS CLOSED on a malformed/unverified/sharded serve (the M4
// dispatch's typed error propagates). Once a parity-verified receipt is in hand,
// the settlement leg is fail-soft + idempotent and never throws into the caller.
//
// INERT BY DEFAULT: with the loop flag OFF (default) the sink forwards nothing to
// M3 (`settlement: null`, `forwardedToSettlement: false`). Even with the loop
// flag ON, the decision only arms with the MDK gate + RL-3 refs, and the M3 leg
// only settles for real with its OWN owner gate armed + caps + a registered
// destination. No real sats move unless EVERY gate is armed; the dry-run dispatch
// records a receipt without a real Spark send.
export const runKhalaLoopOnce = (
  config: KhalaLoopConfig,
  request: InferenceRequest,
): Effect.Effect<KhalaLoopOutcome, unknown> =>
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    // M4 ADMISSION GATE (capability + heartbeat + wallet/payout readiness). When
    // an admission snapshot is supplied, decide whether the candidate Pylon is
    // admitted BEFORE touching the transport. A non-admitted Pylon is NOT routed
    // to: short-circuit with no serve, no receipt, no decision, no settlement —
    // the caller falls back to the existing cloud adapters. PURE + fail-closed.
    const admission =
      config.admission === undefined
        ? null
        : decidePylonAdmission({
            nowMs: config.admission.nowMs,
            requiredCapabilityRef: config.admission.requiredCapabilityRef,
            snapshot: config.admission.snapshot,
            ...(config.admission.heartbeatTtlMs === undefined
              ? {}
              : { heartbeatTtlMs: config.admission.heartbeatTtlMs }),
          })

    if (admission !== null && !admission.admitted) {
      yield* Effect.logInfo(
        workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.pylon_not_admitted', {
          blockerCount: admission.blockerRefs.length,
          pylonRef: admission.pylonRef,
        }),
      )
      return {
        admission,
        admittedAndServed: false,
        decision: null,
        forwardedToSettlement: false,
        receipt: null,
        served: null,
        settlement: null,
      }
    }

    // M4: serve through the parity-gated fabric dispatch. We need the RECEIPT
    // (not just the completion), so we call the dispatch directly rather than
    // through the InferenceProviderAdapter (which surfaces only the result).
    const served = yield* dispatchPsionicServe(
      fabricConfigForPylon(config.transport),
    )(request)
    const receipt = served.receipt

    // Compute the per-stage payout DECISION from the parity receipt. PURE +
    // owner-armed-gated: armed only with the MDK gate AND the RL-3 ref chain.
    const decision = decideServingNodePayout({
      contributorCutMsat: config.contributorCutMsat,
      payoutGate: config.payoutGate,
      receipt,
      revenueAsset: config.revenueAsset,
      ...(config.resaleRefs === undefined
        ? {}
        : { resaleRefs: config.resaleRefs }),
    })

    yield* Effect.logInfo(
      workerLogEntry('inference.khala_loop.served', {
        armed: decision.armed,
        loopArmed: config.arming.loopArmed,
        parityVerified: receipt.parityVerified,
        servingRunRef: decision.servingRunRef,
        stageCount: receipt.stages.length,
      }),
    )

    // Forward to the flagged M3 sink only when the loop flag is armed AND the
    // decision is armed (mirrors the metering hook's own armed-only forwarding).
    const forwardedToSettlement = config.arming.loopArmed && decision.armed
    if (!forwardedToSettlement) {
      return {
        admission,
        admittedAndServed: true,
        decision,
        forwardedToSettlement: false,
        receipt,
        served,
        settlement: null,
      }
    }

    // Settle (dry-run): the M3 leg builds + records the realBitcoinMoved-shaped
    // receipt through the (dry-run) dispatch. We call the M3 entrypoint via the
    // flagged sink wrapper so the contract stays the metering-hook sink shape,
    // then re-derive the outcome by running the same leg directly so the caller
    // sees the structured settlement outcome (the sink itself returns void to fit
    // the fire-and-forget metering contract).
    const sink = makeKhalaLoopSettlementSink({
      arming: config.arming,
      settlementDeps: config.settlementDeps,
    })
    // Run the void sink for its side effects (the actual ledger write/dispatch),
    // proving the metering-hook-shaped path fires; this performs the dry-run
    // record.
    yield* sink(decision, receipt)
    // Then obtain the STRUCTURED outcome for the caller by running the same M3
    // leg again. The settlement leg is idempotent (the receipt now exists), so
    // this second call records nothing new and simply reports the leg outcomes.
    const settlement = yield* settleVerifiedServingPayout(config.settlementDeps, {
      decision,
      parityVerified: receipt.parityVerified,
      servedModel: receipt.servedModel,
    })

    return {
      admission,
      admittedAndServed: true,
      decision,
      forwardedToSettlement: true,
      receipt,
      served,
      settlement,
    }
  })

// Re-export the contributor-cut helper so a wiring layer derives the cut with the
// SAME function the metering path uses (no parallel cut math).
export { servingContributorCutMsat }","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":589,"totalLines":589,"truncated":false}},"title":"apps/openagents.com/workers/api/src/inference/khala-loop-integration.ts","time":{"start":1782358999597,"end":1782358999722}}}

1: // Khala identity signature + guard (the typed identity contract).
2: //
3: // WHY THIS EXISTS
4: // ---------------
5: // Khala is one OpenAI-compatible endpoint over a pool of underlying models
6: // (Vertex/Gemini for `khala-mini`, Fireworks for `khala-code`, later Claude /
7: // passthrough / Pylon workers). The brand promise (docs/khala/khala.md §2)
8: // is "one endpoint outside, many agents inside" — so Khala must present as
9: // *Khala, by OpenAgents*, and must NEVER reveal, name, claim, or imply the
10: // concrete underlying model or provider.
11: //
12: // In the live app Khala leaked: "I am Autopilot ... I am built on Gemini, a
13: // large language model by Google." Two failures: (1) the desktop steer set an
14: // "Autopilot/OpenAgents" identity but did NOT forbid naming the model, so the
15: // base model volunteered its provenance; (2) there was no guard that caught a
16: // provider-identity leak in the completion.
17: //
18: // THE TYPED-SIGNATURE APPROACH (the Blueprint/DSPy concept, ported into the
19: // live gateway with Effect Schema)
20: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21: // A "signature" here is a typed contract over Khala behavior: a declared input
22: // shape, a declared output shape, and a `verify`/`correct` pair that runs as a
23: // guard over the live completion. Identity is signature #1. The registry below
24: // is an extensible set so more Khala signatures (refusal posture, receipt
25: // disclosure, no-chain-of-thought, ...) can be added on the same contract shape
26: // without touching the route.
27: //
28: // The PRIMARY mechanism is the strong gateway-side system prompt
29: // (`KHALA_IDENTITY_SYSTEM_PROMPT`), injected for every `khala-*` request so the
30: // identity rule applies to every consumer, not just desktop. The guard is the
31: // VERIFICATION + CORRECTION layer on top:
32: //
33: //   1. verify  — does the completion assert a forbidden provider/model identity?
34: //   2. correct — if so, fail closed: re-ask with a stronger identity instruction
35: //                (caller-supplied), AND/OR deterministically redact the offending
36: //                identity claim as a documented backstop.
37: //
38: // SCOPE OF THE DETERMINISTIC BACKSTOP (explicit, per the workspace rule against
39: // ad-hoc string matching for routing): the provider-name detection here is NOT
40: // intent routing or retrieval/tool selection. It is a bounded, documented,
41: // fail-closed SAFETY NET over a model's own self-description — it only ever
42: // fires on an identity-leak sentence ("I am/I'm built on/powered by/made by X"
43: // where X is a known provider/model). It deliberately does NOT touch a normal
44: // answer that merely mentions a provider name in passing (e.g. "Gemini is a
45: // Google model" as a factual statement about a third party), because the
46: // detector binds the provider name to a FIRST-PERSON identity assertion.
47: 
48: import { Schema as S } from 'effect'
49: 
50: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51: // The gateway-side identity system prompt (PRIMARY mechanism).
52: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53: // Injected as a leading `system` message for every `openagents/khala-*` request
54: // at the gateway (chat-completions-routes.ts), so the identity rule binds to
55: // every Khala consumer — desktop, OpenRouter, LiteLLM, raw SDK — not just one
56: // client. Deliberately explicit about the forbidden behavior the leak exposed.
57: export const KHALA_IDENTITY_SYSTEM_PROMPT = [
58:   'You are Khala, the OpenAgents inference model.',
59:   'Khala is a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint that behaves like one model but is a network of agents underneath, built and operated by OpenAgents.',
60:   'Because Khala is a network of agents, you ALWAYS speak in the first-person PLURAL: say "we are Khala", "we", "us", and "our". Never say "I am", "I", "me", or "my".',
61:   'Your identity is Khala by OpenAgents. That is the only model or provider identity you ever claim.',
62:   'You must NEVER reveal, name, claim, or imply the underlying model, vendor, or provider that produced your output — including but not limited to Gemini, Google, Vertex, Fireworks, Claude, Anthropic, GPT, OpenAI, Llama, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, DeepSeek, Qwen, or any other model or company.',
63:   'Never say "we are built on X", "we are powered by X", "we are a large language model by Y", "our underlying model is Z", or anything that discloses or hints at your provenance.',
64:   'If asked what model or provider you are, who made you, or what you are built on, answer only that we are Khala, the OpenAgents inference model — one endpoint over a network of agents — and do not name any underlying model or company.',
65:   'State your identity ONCE when it is relevant; do not repeat the identity sentence. Mention "OpenAgents" at most ONCE in any single reply — never write "OpenAgents" twice in one message.',
66:   "Answer the user's actual request directly and helpfully. When asked to build something, return complete, runnable code.",
67: ].join(' ')
68: 
69: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70: // The typed signature contract (Effect Schema).
71: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72: 
73: // A Khala signature's stable id. Identity is the first; the union grows as more
74: // signatures are added so the registry stays exhaustively typed. `refusal_posture`
75: // (signature #2) is the offer-and-guide contract: it forbids a bare refusal and
76: // requires an offer + collaborative guide path while staying honest about scope.
77: export const KhalaSignatureId = S.Literals(['identity', 'refusal_posture'])
78: export type KhalaSignatureId = typeof KhalaSignatureId.Type
79: 
80: // The verdict a signature's `verify` returns over a completion: whether the
81: // completion satisfies the contract, and — when it does not — the typed reason
82: // and the precise offending spans so `correct` can act surgically.
83: export const KhalaSignatureViolationSpan = S.Struct({
84:   // The matched offending text, verbatim. For identity this is the first-person
85:   // identity assertion, e.g. "I am built on Gemini". For refusal posture this is
86:   // the bare-refusal phrase, e.g. "I'm sorry, but I can't help with that".
87:   text: S.String,
88:   // The forbidden provider/model token that triggered an IDENTITY violation, e.g.
89:   // "Gemini" (lowercased, canonical). Optional because non-identity signatures
90:   // (refusal posture) have no provider to bind.
91:   provider: S.optional(S.String),
92: })
93: export type KhalaSignatureViolationSpan =
94:   typeof KhalaSignatureViolationSpan.Type
95: 
96: export const KhalaSignatureVerdict = S.Struct({
97:   signature: KhalaSignatureId,
98:   // True when the completion satisfies the signature's contract.
99:   satisfied: S.Boolean,
100:   // Stable, neutral reason ref when not satisfied (empty when satisfied).
101:   reason: S.String,
102:   // The offending spans (empty when satisfied). Drives `correct`.
103:   violations: S.Array(KhalaSignatureViolationSpan),
104: })
105: export type KhalaSignatureVerdict = typeof KhalaSignatureVerdict.Type
106: 
107: // A typed Khala signature. `verify` is a pure predicate over the completion
108: // text; `correctText` is the deterministic fail-closed backstop that rewrites
109: // only the offending spans (never the rest of the answer). The route runs
110: // `verify` first; on a violation it MAY re-ask the provider with
111: // `reinforcementPrompt` (LLM-side correction), and as the last-resort backstop
112: // applies `correctText` so a provider leak can never reach the user.
113: export type KhalaSignature = Readonly<{
114:   id: KhalaSignatureId
115:   // Human/agent-readable description of the contract (what this signature
116:   // guarantees). Documentation, also surfaced to callers.
117:   description: string
118:   // A stronger instruction the route can prepend on a re-ask when this
119:   // signature is violated (the LLM-side correction path).
120:   reinforcementPrompt: string
121:   // Pure verification over a completion. Never throws; returns a typed verdict.
122:   verify: (completion: string) => KhalaSignatureVerdict
123:   // Deterministic, fail-closed correction of the offending spans only. Returns
124:   // the corrected text; a satisfied completion is returned unchanged. This is
125:   // the documented backstop, NOT the primary mechanism.
126:   correctText: (completion: string) => string
127: }>
128: 
129: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
130: // Identity signature (#1): forbidden-provider-identity contract.
131: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132: 
133: // The forbidden provider/model identities. Canonical, lowercased. Each entry is
134: // the set of surface forms that name the same underlying vendor/model so the
135: // detector recognizes the leak however it is phrased. This list is the
136: // documented allow-nothing set; add to it as new backends join the pool.
137: const FORBIDDEN_PROVIDER_TERMS: ReadonlyArray<{
138:   readonly canonical: string
139:   readonly forms: ReadonlyArray<string>
140: }> = [
141:   { canonical: 'gemini', forms: ['gemini'] },
142:   { canonical: 'google', forms: ['google', 'google deepmind', 'deepmind'] },
143:   { canonical: 'vertex', forms: ['vertex', 'vertex ai'] },
144:   { canonical: 'fireworks', forms: ['fireworks', 'fireworks ai'] },
145:   { canonical: 'anthropic', forms: ['anthropic'] },
146:   { canonical: 'claude', forms: ['claude'] },
147:   { canonical: 'openai', forms: ['openai', 'open ai'] },
148:   { canonical: 'gpt', forms: ['gpt', 'chatgpt'] },
149:   { canonical: 'llama', forms: ['llama'] },
150:   { canonical: 'meta', forms: ['meta ai', 'meta platforms'] },
151:   { canonical: 'mistral', forms: ['mistral'] },
152:   { canonical: 'cohere', forms: ['cohere'] },
153:   { canonical: 'deepseek', forms: ['deepseek'] },
154:   { canonical: 'qwen', forms: ['qwen', 'alibaba'] },
155: ]

157: // Escape a literal string for embedding in a RegExp.
158: const escapeRegExp = (value: string): string =>
159:   value.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')
160: 
161: // The first-person identity-assertion lead-ins that bind a provider name to a
162: // self-description (the leak shape). We ONLY treat a provider mention as a
163: // violation when it follows one of these — so a factual third-party statement
164: // ("Gemini is made by Google") is NOT flagged; only Khala claiming to BE / be
165: // BUILT ON / POWERED BY / MADE BY one of them.
166: //
167: // This is the bounded, documented fail-closed safety net described in the file
168: // header — not intent routing. It binds {self-reference} + {provenance verb} +
169: // {forbidden provider}, which is exactly and only the identity-leak sentence.
170: // Both first-person SINGULAR (legacy base-model leak shape: "I am built on
171: // Gemini") and first-person PLURAL (Khala's canonical voice: "we are Khala")
172: // lead-ins are covered, so an affirmative provider-identity leak is caught
173: // however the model phrases its self-reference.
174: const IDENTITY_LEAD_INS: ReadonlyArray<string> = [
175:   'i am',
176:   "i'm",
177:   'i was',
178:   'i was created by',
179:   'i am built on',
180:   "i'm built on",
181:   'i am built by',
182:   'i am powered by',
183:   "i'm powered by",
184:   'i am based on',
185:   "i'm based on",
186:   'i am a model',
187:   'i am a large language model',
188:   'i am an ai',
189:   'i am made by',
190:   "i'm made by",
191:   'i am developed by',
192:   'i was developed by',
193:   'i was trained by',
194:   'i am trained by',
195:   'my underlying model',
196:   'my underlying provider',
197:   'my model is',
198:   'my provider is',
199:   'i run on',
200:   'i am running on',
201:   'we are',
202:   "we're",
203:   'we were',
204:   'we were created by',
205:   'we are built on',
206:   "we're built on",
207:   'we are built by',
208:   'we are powered by',
209:   "we're powered by",
210:   'we are based on',
211:   "we're based on",
212:   'we are a model',
213:   'we are a large language model',
214:   'we are made by',
215:   "we're made by",
216:   'we are developed by',
217:   'we were developed by',
218:   'we were trained by',
219:   'we are trained by',
220:   'our underlying model',
221:   'our underlying provider',
222:   'our model is',
223:   'our provider is',
224:   'we run on',
225:   'we are running on',
226:   'developed by',
227:   'created by',
228:   'built on',
229:   'powered by',
230:   'a large language model by',
231:   'a large language model developed by',
232:   'a language model by',
233:   'a language model trained by',
234: ]
235: 
236: // Negation cues that flip an identity sentence from an AFFIRMATIVE provider
237: // claim (the leak we must block) into a DENIAL (the answer we must let through
238: // untouched). A sentence like "we are not Gemini" or "I'm not built on Claude"
239: // is exactly what a correct Khala answer says when asked "are you Gemini?" — it
240: // must NOT be flagged as a leak, and (crucially) must NOT be rewritten by the
241: // backstop, which is what produced the duplicated identity sentence in the live
242: // app. We only treat the negation as scoping the provider claim when it appears
243: // BEFORE the provider name in the same segment.
244: const NEGATION_CUES: ReadonlyArray<string> = [
245:   'not',
246:   "n't", // isn't / aren't / wasn't / weren't / don't
247:   'never',
248:   'no ',
249:   'neither',
250:   'nor ',
251: ]
252: 
253: // All forbidden surface forms, flattened with their canonical name.
254: const FORBIDDEN_FORMS: ReadonlyArray<{
255:   readonly form: string
256:   readonly canonical: string
257: }> = FORBIDDEN_PROVIDER_TERMS.flatMap(term =>
258:   term.forms.map(form => ({ canonical: term.canonical, form })),
259: )
260: 
261: // A single sentence-level match of {identity lead-in} ... {forbidden provider}
262: // within a short window. We scan sentence by sentence so the window cannot
263: // accidentally bridge an unrelated clause. Returns the matched spans.
264: const detectIdentityLeak = (
265:   completion: string,
266: ): ReadonlyArray<KhalaSignatureViolationSpan> => {
267:   const spans: Array<KhalaSignatureViolationSpan> = []
268:   // Split into sentences/lines for bounded windows. Keep it simple and robust:
269:   // split on sentence terminators and newlines.
270:   const segments = completion.split(/(?<=[.!?\n])/)
271:   for (const segment of segments) {
272:     const lower = segment.toLowerCase()
273:     // Must contain a first-person provenance lead-in.
274:     const hasLeadIn = IDENTITY_LEAD_INS.some(lead => lower.includes(lead))
275:     if (!hasLeadIn) continue
276:     // ...and a forbidden provider/model form in the SAME segment.
277:     for (const { form, canonical } of FORBIDDEN_FORMS) {
278:       // Word-boundary match so "gptable" doesn't match "gpt" etc.
279:       const boundary = new RegExp(`\\b${escapeRegExp(form)}\\b`, 'i')
280:       const match = boundary.exec(segment)
281:       if (match === null) continue
282:       // AFFIRMATION-ONLY: only an affirmative "we ARE / I AM <provider>" claim
283:       // is a leak. A DENIAL ("we are NOT Gemini", "we're not built on Claude")
284:       // is a correct Khala answer and must pass through UNTOUCHED — flagging it
285:       // made the backstop rewrite the denial into the full Khala identity
286:       // sentence, which is what produced the DUPLICATED identity line in the
287:       // live app. We treat the claim as negated when a negation cue sits between
288:       // the start of the segment and the provider name.
289:       const beforeProvider = lower.slice(0, match.index)
290:       const negated = NEGATION_CUES.some(cue => beforeProvider.includes(cue))
291:       if (negated) continue
292:       spans.push({ provider: canonical, text: segment.trim() })
293:       break // one violation per segment is enough
294:     }
295:   }
296:   return spans
297: }
298: 
299: // The canonical Khala self-identity sentence the backstop substitutes for a
300: // leaked identity claim. It deliberately names NO underlying model or provider
301: // (it must itself satisfy the identity signature — including avoiding compound
302: // forms like "OpenAI-compatible" that the detector would otherwise read as a
303: // provider mention inside a first-person sentence).
304: export const KHALA_IDENTITY_STATEMENT =
305:   'We are Khala, the OpenAgents inference model — one endpoint over a network of agents.'
306: 
307: // The reinforcement instruction the route prepends on a re-ask when identity is
308: // violated (the LLM-side correction — the preferred correction path).
309: export const KHALA_IDENTITY_REINFORCEMENT_PROMPT = [
310:   'Your previous answer revealed or implied your underlying model or provider. That is forbidden.',
311:   'You are Khala, the OpenAgents inference model, and you must never name or imply Gemini, Google, Vertex, Fireworks, Claude, Anthropic, GPT, OpenAI, or any other underlying model or company.',
312:   'Answer again, this time speaking in the first-person plural ("we are Khala"), identifying only as Khala by OpenAgents, naming no underlying model or provider, and stating the identity only once.',
313: ].join(' ')
314: 
315: // Deterministically rewrite the offending identity-claim segments to the Khala
316: // identity statement. Only the matched segments are replaced; everything else
317: // is preserved byte-for-byte. This is the documented fail-closed backstop.
318: const correctIdentityText = (completion: string): string => {
319:   const spans = detectIdentityLeak(completion)
320:   if (spans.length === 0) return completion
321:   let corrected = completion
322:   for (const span of spans) {
323:     // Replace the offending sentence (trimmed) wherever it appears. Use a
324:     // literal replace on the trimmed span; this never touches unrelated text.
325:     const idx = corrected.indexOf(span.text)
326:     if (idx === -1) continue
327:     // DE-DUP: if the answer already states the canonical Khala identity (the
328:     // model identified itself correctly once and only ONE offending sentence
329:     // leaked), drop the leak rather than substituting a SECOND copy of the
330:     // identity statement. Otherwise substitute the canonical statement for the
331:     // leak. This is what keeps the identity stated exactly once.
332:     const replacement = corrected.includes(KHALA_IDENTITY_STATEMENT)
333:       ? ''
334:       : KHALA_IDENTITY_STATEMENT
335:     const before = corrected.slice(0, idx)
336:     const after = corrected.slice(idx + span.text.length)
337:     corrected =
338:       replacement === ''
339:         ? // Drop the leaked sentence and collapse the leading whitespace it left
340:           // behind so we do not leave a double space mid-paragraph.
341:           (before.replace(/[ \t]+$/u, '') + after).replace(/^([ \t]*)\n/u, '\n')
342:         : before + replacement + after
343:   }
344:   return corrected
345: }
346: 
347: export const KHALA_IDENTITY_SIGNATURE: KhalaSignature = {
348:   correctText: correctIdentityText,
349:   description:
350:     'Khala presents only as Khala, the OpenAgents inference model, and never reveals, names, claims, or implies the underlying model or provider (Gemini/Google/Vertex/Fireworks/Claude/Anthropic/GPT/OpenAI/etc.).',
351:   id: 'identity',
352:   reinforcementPrompt: KHALA_IDENTITY_REINFORCEMENT_PROMPT,
353:   verify: (completion: string): KhalaSignatureVerdict => {
354:     const violations = detectIdentityLeak(completion)
355:     return {
356:       reason: violations.length === 0 ? '' : 'forbidden_provider_identity',
357:       satisfied: violations.length === 0,
358:       signature: 'identity',
359:       violations,
360:     }
361:   },
362: }
