## What it is
Multi-tenant **serverless** inference on Fireworks-managed infra — pay per token, no GPUs to
size, no cold starts. Eligible models carry the **Serverless** tag. (On-demand dedicated GPUs
exist for custom/LoRA models + version stability; not needed for our passthrough lane.)

## Serverless pricing (per 1M tokens, USD — Standard: input / cached-input / output)
Real cost basis for our open/cheap tier:

| Model | input / cached / output |
| --- | --- |
| gpt-oss-20b | 0.07 / 0.035 / 0.30 |
| OpenAI gpt-oss-120b | **0.15 / 0.015 / 0.60** |
| DeepSeek V4 Flash | 0.14 / 0.028 / 0.28 |
| MiniMax M3 / 2.7 / 2.5 | 0.30 / 0.06 / 1.20 |
| Qwen 3.7 Plus | 0.40 / 0.08 / 1.60 |
| Nemotron 3 Ultra | 0.60 / 0.12 / 2.40 |
| Kimi K2.5 | 0.60 / 0.10 / 3.00 |
| Kimi K2.6 | 0.95 / 0.16 / 4.00 |
| Kimi K2.7 Code | 0.95 / 0.19 / 4.00 |
| GLM 5.2 / 5.1 | 1.40 / 0.26 / 4.40 |
| DeepSeek V4 Pro | 1.74 / 0.145 / 3.48 |

Size-based fallback for any other model (uniform in/out, no cached rate): <4B $0.10, 4–16B
$0.20, >16B $0.90, MoE ≤56B $0.50, MoE 56–176B $1.20. Embeddings: $0.008–$0.10 / 1M input.
**Batch inference = 50%** of standard (both directions). **Fast** variants and **Priority**
(`service_tier:"priority"`) cost a premium.

> These are our **marginal cost** for the cheap/open tier — drop them straight into the
> pricing-model multiplier/margin math (`2026-06-19-pricing-model.md`). e.g. gpt-oss-120b at
> $0.15/$0.60 in/out is dramatically cheaper than frontier Claude, so the open tier carries
> fat margin even at a low sell multiplier — and that margin is what fans to serving nodes +
> referrers.

## Billing dimensions (meter against these)
Three billed dimensions: **input**, **cached input** (prompt-cache hits, default ~50% of
input), **output**. The response **`usage` object is the source of truth** (`prompt_tokens`,
`completion_tokens`, `total_tokens`) — our metering decrements credits from `usage`, never
an estimate (receipt-first). Response headers also carry `fireworks-prompt-tokens`,
`fireworks-cached-prompt-tokens`, and the live rate-limit ceilings (`X-Ratelimit-Limit-Tokens-*`).

## Prompt caching (free margin)
On by default for every serverless model; cached input billed ~50%. Caching is
**replica-local**, so to maximize hit rate pin repeated/shared-prefix prompts to one replica
via the **`x-session-affinity`** header (or the OpenAI `user` field). For our gateway: pass a
stable per-customer/per-session affinity key so context-heavy coding sessions hit cache —
directly lowering our cost and widening margin.

## Rate limits (must handle in the adapter)
Three adaptive metrics, **per-account + per-model**, that grow/shrink with usage:
**Total-Prompt TPM**, **Uncached-Prompt TPM**, **Generated TPM**. Starting limits:
**3.6M / 900k / 36k TPM** (~60k / 15k / 600 TPS). Fast vs regular variants have **separate**
limits; Priority shares the regular limit. Higher account **spend tier** → higher upper
bounds. Current effective limits are in the `X-Ratelimit-Limit-Tokens-*` response headers.
- `429 Too Many Requests` → **exponential backoff**, and in our gateway **overflow to another
  supply lane** (Vertex / other passthrough / our network) rather than failing the request.
- `503 Service Overloaded` (load-shed even within limits) → retry / use **Priority tier** for
  latency-sensitive traffic.
- **Don't ramp cold** — the adaptive limit punishes sudden spikes; warm up gradually or
  pre-arrange higher limits (inquiries@fireworks.ai) before a launch.

## Serving paths
- **Standard** — default (no param).
- **Priority** — `service_tier:"priority"`, premium price, higher reliability under load.
- **Fast** — switch the model id to the Fast variant (e.g. `…/kimi-k2p6-fast` / a fast router);
  separate rate-limit pool.

## Model lifecycle
Serverless models are Fireworks-managed; **≥2 weeks notice** before removal (longer for
popular ones). For version-pinned production stability, on-demand deployments give full
control. Our gateway should treat serverless model ids as a managed set and degrade
gracefully if one is retired.

## How it slots into the OpenAgents gateway
- **Provider adapter:** OpenAI-compatible, base `https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1`, key
  from `.secrets/fireworks.env`. Near drop-in.
- **Routing:** the **open-model / cheap tier** of the gateway — for models Vertex lacks
  (DeepSeek/Kimi/GLM/Qwen/MiniMax/gpt-oss/Nemotron) + embeddings/vision/image. Vertex stays
  the Claude lane; our Pylon fabric is the contributor-served lane; Fireworks is the
  managed-open-model lane.
- **Pricing:** sell = Fireworks cost × multiplier/margin; cache-affinity + batch (50%) cut our
  cost further; 429/503 → backoff + overflow.
- **Meter from `usage`**, pin `x-session-affinity`, handle the adaptive rate-limit ramp.

## Re-verification (2026-06-19, weekend assault DE-2)

Independently re-confirmed the live provider connection against the OpenAgents-held key
(`~/work/.secrets/fireworks.env`, never printed/committed):

This keeps `inference.fireworks_open_model_provider.v1` at **yellow**: the provider connection
is a verified, reproducible, registered live supply lane in the deployed gateway. It does NOT
green — green needs the PAID open-model product (card/Bitcoin → metered open-model spend →
dereferenceable paid receipt), which is the owner-gated paid-credits path shared with

1: # Khala Inference Push — Product-Promise Review

15: ## 1. Promises this GTM push directly advances
16: 
17: The inference push is built on the live free OpenAI-compatible API + public
18: counter. These existing registry promises are the ones it moves:

20: | promiseId | state | How this push advances it | What it still needs for green |
21: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
22: | `inference.free_tier_taste.v1` | yellow | Pillars 1–2 drive real usage of the live free lane (`openagents/khala`, `POST /api/keys/free`, 200 req / 200k tok/day). The more tools point at the free tier, the stronger the live free-API evidence. | `blocker.product_promises.inference_free_taste_paid_upgrade_not_collectable` — green needs the collectable paid loop so free→paid is a sellable product, plus a receipt-first upgrade. (Owner-gated; out of scope for the GTM push.) |
23: | `inference.gateway_credits_business.v1` | red | The gateway request surface this promise describes (`POST /v1/chat/completions`, key-auth, balance gate, routing, receipt-first metering) is the exact surface the push grows demand on. More demand = more pressure/evidence for the paid loop. | `..._inference_paid_credits_card_to_credit_not_collectable`, `..._inference_paid_receipt_not_yet_supplied`, `..._inference_mpp_owner_activation_pending`, `public_paid_model_gateway_missing` — needs a real card/MPP→credit→inference-spend settled receipt, owner-signed. |
24: | `inference.fireworks_open_model_provider.v1` | yellow | Benchmarking (Pillar 3) compares Khala vs the Fireworks open-model lane; the dogfood traffic exercises it as live supply. | `..._inference_paid_credits_card_to_credit_not_collectable`, `..._inference_open_model_paid_product_no_receipt` — needs a real customer-completed funded open-model request with dereferenceable metering + settlement. |
25: | `api.hosted_gemini.v1` | yellow | Khala's free lane serves Gemini Flash today; dogfood + tools exercise the hosted path. | `..._public_paid_model_gateway_missing`, `..._production_hosted_gemini_executor_binding_missing`. |
26: | `inference.referral_on_all_inference.v1` | planned | The "every tool points at us" distribution channel is the referral thesis' demand engine; landing tools seeds the accounts a referral would later accrue on. | `..._inference_paid_credits_card_to_credit_not_collectable`, `..._inference_referral_attribution_unbuilt`, `..._inference_referral_accrual_unbuilt`, `referral.first_real_payout_pending`. |
27: | `inference.decentralized_serving_fabric.v1` | red | The Verse-uses-Khala and gym dogfood lanes (direction) and growing demand build the case for Pylon-served supply later. | `..._pylon_inference_serving_unbuilt`, `..._shard_wan_large_model_serving_psionic_planned`, `..._inference_serving_node_payout_unbuilt`, `..._inference_serving_first_real_payout_owner_armed`, `..._inference_paid_credits_card_to_credit_not_collectable`. |

The gym↔Khala *product* wiring is new direction, not covered by these. |

39: ## 2. Missing promise(s) for this push
40: 
41: The registry has **no** promise that cleanly asserts the two things the push is
42: publicly built on:
43: 
44: 1. **"Khala OpenAI-compatible inference is free + live."** Today this is split
45:    across `inference.free_tier_taste.v1` (yellow, framed as a bounded taste on a
46:    paid loop) and the gateway-live evidence buried inside
47:    `inference.gateway_credits_business.v1` (red, framed as a *credits business*).
48:    Neither is a clean, standalone, green-eligible "the free live API exists"
49:    assertion — which is exactly the claim the GTM push leans on hardest.
50: 2. **"Tokens served per day is a public metric."** No promise asserts the live
51:    `khala-tokens-served` counter + history as a public, agent-readable metric,
52:    even though it ships and is the push's north-star number. (The only adjacent
53:    metric promise is `metrics.accepted_outcomes_per_kwh.v1`, which is a different
54:    measure.)
55: 
56: ### Proposed new promise A — free live OpenAI-compatible API
57: 
58: > Conservative, receipt-first wording. **Suggestion only — do not create or flip;
59: > owner-gated.** This is plausibly **green-eligible today** because its evidence is
60: > already live and dereferenceable, which is precisely why it should be its own
61: > record rather than hiding inside two non-green promises.
62: 
63: - **promiseId (proposed):** `inference.khala_free_openai_compatible_api.v1`
64: - **productArea:** inference
65: - **state (proposed):** green *(eligible — all evidence is live; owner sign-off +
66:   receipt-first transition still required)*
67: - **safeCopy (proposed):** "Khala is a free, live, OpenAI-compatible inference
68:   API. Point any OpenAI-compatible client at `https://openagents.com/api/v1` with
69:   model `openagents/khala`, mint a free key at `POST /api/keys/free`, and run
70:   inference free within a per-key daily quota (200 requests / 200,000 tokens per
71:   UTC day). Over-quota and premium lanes require credits. Free inference serves
72:   own-infra / non-premium models only; the `claude`/premium lanes are never free."
73: - **unsafeCopy (must-not-say):** "Paid Khala is generally launched"; "you can fund
74:   inference end-to-end via card/Bitcoin/MPP" (those belong to the red/yellow paid
75:   promises); "unlimited free inference."
76: - **evidence (proposed):** `POST /api/v1/chat/completions` live (`index.ts`);
77:   `POST /api/keys/free` live (`free-key-mint-routes.ts`,
78:   `inference/inference-free-tier-key.ts`, quota constants
79:   `FREE_TIER_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_DAY=200`, `FREE_TIER_MAX_TOKENS_PER_DAY=200_000`);
80:   free lane policy (own-infra only, premium excluded) in the same module; capability
81:   manifest + OpenAPI entries.
82: - **rationale:** Splitting the free-API claim out of the credits-business red
83:   promise lets us make the honest, currently-true claim cleanly without implying
84:   the paid loop. It is the single most-used claim in the GTM push.
85: 
86: ### Proposed new promise B — public tokens-served metric
87: 
88: - **promiseId (proposed):** `metrics.khala_tokens_served_public.v1`
89: - **productArea:** metrics (or inference)
90: - **state (proposed):** green *(eligible — counter is live and verified)*
91: - **safeCopy (proposed):** "OpenAgents publishes a live public count of Khala
92:   inference tokens served, with per-day history, at
93:   `GET /api/public/khala-tokens-served` and `/history` (also shown on `/stats` and
94:   `/khala`). The counter increments on every completion."
95: - **unsafeCopy:** any claim that the counter implies paid revenue, external-only
96:   demand, or a specific business outcome — it counts served tokens (internal +
97:   external), nothing more.
98: - **evidence (proposed):** `public-khala-tokens-served-routes.ts`,
99:   `public-khala-tokens-served-history-routes.ts`, `token-usage-ledger.ts`, route
100:   registration in `index.ts`; the verified-to-the-token-under-24-wide-concurrency
101:   test note.
102: - **caveat to bake in:** the copy must not let the number be read as external
103:   traction; per the GTM doc, keep internal vs external distinguishable in analytics.
104: 
105: > Both proposed promises are green-*eligible* but still owner-gated: creating them
106: > and (if chosen) flipping them green requires recording the backing transition
107: > receipts against the deployed registry per `proof.claim_upgrade_receipts.v1`.
108: > This review only proposes the wording.

110: ## 3. Suggested deprioritizations (park while we focus on inference)
111: 
112: Suggestions only — no state flips, nothing withdrawn. These are promises/areas
113: **not on the inference push's critical path** that could be parked so attention and
114: agent lanes concentrate on tokens-served growth. One-line rationale each. (Note:
115: several are already `planned`, so "deprioritize" here means *do not pull them into
116: active lanes during the push*, not "change their state.")
117: 
118: | Area / promiseId | Current state | Why park during the inference push |
119: | --- | --- | --- |
120: | `cloud.fine_tuning_service.v1` | red | Separate sellable product (OpenAI-shaped FT lifecycle); inert scaffold; no demand wedge vs inference. Park until inference demand justifies it. |
121: | `cloud.sandbox_compute_service.v1` | red | Same — separate rentable-compute product, inert scaffold; not on the tokens-served path. |
122: | `inference.batch_processing_jobs.v1` | planned | Batch surface is unbuilt and additive; single-request gateway is the wedge. Defer until interactive demand is exponential. |
123: | `training.public_gradient_windows.v1`, `training.full_pipeline_program.v1`, `training.ablation_system.v1`, `training.data_refinery_corpus.v1`, `training.model_ladder.v1`, `training.marathon_operations.v1`, `training.post_training_arc.v1`, `training.device_capability_dataset.v1` | planned | The broad training program is large and not the GTM wedge. Keep only the gym→Khala dogfood slice that *uses/improves Khala*; park the rest of the training-promise expansion. |
124: | `pylon.largest_decentralized_training_claim.v1`, `pylon.first_real_model_training_run.v1`, `claims.world_first_*` | planned/red | World-first/largest *claims* are owner-signed, high-risk, and orthogonal to inference adoption. Do not spend lanes chasing these during the push. |
125: | `marketplace.agentic_npm_module_registry.v1`, `marketplace.signature_monetization.v1`, `marketplace.wasm_plugins.v1`, `marketplace.compose_and_list_products.v1`, `marketplace.monetize_any_layer_with_referral.v1` | planned | The plugin/module marketplace is the long-run Khala-Blueprint direction but unbuilt; it is downstream of having demand. Park until tokens-served is growing. |
126: | `markets.open_protocol_markets.v1` | planned | Liquidity/risk/compute-data markets are far-future relative to "be adoptable today." |
127: | `autopilot_sites.*` (e.g. `autopilot_sites.native_email_sequences.v1`, `custom_tenant_hostnames.v1`, `partner_payout_ledger.v1`, `site_build_and_host.v1`) | yellow/red | Sites is a distinct product line; not on the inference wedge. Keep maintenance only. |
128: | `mobile.*` (`voice_approval_companion.v1`, `autopilot_remote_control.v1`, `voice_session_evidence_transcript_ingest.v1`) | yellow/red/planned | Mobile operator surfaces are orthogonal to inference adoption; park new lanes. |
129: | `world.multiplayer_agent_world.v1` & broad Verse work | planned | Keep only the thin "Verse visualizes Khala traffic" dogfood slice (Pillar 1.5); park the broader multiplayer-world promise during the push. |
130: | `energy.flexible_load_proof.v1`, `compute.agentic_kernel_optimization_at_scale.v1` | planned | Compute/energy demonstrations are not the adoption wedge; defer. |
131: 
132: **Explicitly NOT deprioritized (stay hot):** the inference family
133: (`inference.*`), the credits/billing seam that unblocks the paid loop
134: (`payments.autopilot_credits_purchase.v1` red, `autopilot.cloud_credits_ui.v1`
135: yellow), the benchmark harness work, and the green training/verification
136: foundation the gym dogfood reuses. The paid-loop blocker
137: (`..._inference_paid_credits_card_to_credit_not_collectable`) is the one
138: owner-gated item worth keeping warm because it unblocks the most inference
139: promises at once — but it does not block the free-API + demand + ecosystem work,
140: which is why the GTM push ships those first.
141: 
142: ## 4. Bottom line
143: 
144: - The push advances the whole `inference.*` family's **evidence and demand** but
145:   flips nothing green by itself; the shared bottleneck is the owner-gated
146:   collectable paid loop.
147: - Two **green-eligible** promises are **missing** and should exist: a clean
148:   "free live OpenAI-compatible Khala API" promise and a "public tokens-served
149:   metric" promise. Wording proposed above; creation/flip is owner-gated.
150: - Park the fine-tuning/sandbox/batch, broad-training, marketplace, world,
151:   mobile, sites, and world-first-claim lanes during the push; concentrate lanes
152:   on tokens-served growth (dogfood + OpenCode + benchmark) and on keeping the
153:   paid-loop blocker warm.

(End of file - total 153 lines)

  inference tokens served, with per-day history, at
  `GET /api/public/khala-tokens-served` and `/history` (also shown on `/stats` and
  `/khala`). The counter increments on every completion."
- **unsafeCopy:** any claim that the counter implies paid revenue, external-only
  demand, or a specific business outcome — it counts served tokens (internal +
  external), nothing more.
- **evidence (proposed):** `public-khala-tokens-served-routes.ts`,
  `public-khala-tokens-served-history-routes.ts`, `token-usage-ledger.ts`, route
  registration in `index.ts`; the verified-to-the-token-under-24-wide-concurrency
  test note.
- **caveat to bake in:** the copy must not let the number be read as external
  traction; per the GTM doc, keep internal vs external distinguishable in analytics.

> Both proposed promises are green-*eligible* but still owner-gated: creating them
> and (if chosen) flipping them green requires recording the backing transition
> receipts against the deployed registry per `proof.claim_upgrade_receipts.v1`.
> This review only proposes the wording.

## 3. Suggested deprioritizations (park while we focus on inference)

Suggestions only — no state flips, nothing withdrawn. These are promises/areas
**not on the inference push's critical path** that could be parked so attention and
agent lanes concentrate on tokens-served growth. One-line rationale each. (Note:
several are already `planned`, so "deprioritize" here means *do not pull them into
active lanes during the push*, not "change their state.")

| Area / promiseId | Current state | Why park during the inference push |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cloud.fine_tuning_service.v1` | red | Separate sellable product (OpenAI-shaped FT lifecycle); inert scaffold; no demand wedge vs inference. Park until inference demand justifies it. |
| `cloud.sandbox_compute_service.v1` | red | Same — separate rentable-compute product, inert scaffold; not on the tokens-served path. |
| `inference.batch_processing_jobs.v1` | planned | Batch surface is unbuilt and additive; single-request gateway is the wedge. Defer until interactive demand is exponential. |
| `training.public_gradient_windows.v1`, `training.full_pipeline_program.v1`, `training.ablation_system.v1`, `training.data_refinery_corpus.v1`, `training.model_ladder.v1`, `training.marathon_operations.v1`, `training.post_training_arc.v1`, `training.device_capability_dataset.v1` | planned | The broad training program is large and not the GTM wedge. Keep only the gym→Khala dogfood slice that *uses/improves Khala*; park the rest of the training-promise expansion. |
| `pylon.largest_decentralized_training_claim.v1`, `pylon.first_real_model_training_run.v1`, `claims.world_first_*` | planned/red | World-first/largest *claims* are owner-signed, high-risk, and orthogonal to inference adoption. Do not spend lanes chasing these during the push. |
| `marketplace.agentic_npm_module_registry.v1`, `marketplace.signature_monetization.v1`, `marketplace.wasm_plugins.v1`, `marketplace.compose_and_list_products.v1`, `marketplace.monetize_any_layer_with_referral.v1` | planned | The plugin/module marketplace is the long-run Khala-Blueprint direction but unbuilt; it is downstream of having demand. Park until tokens-served is growing. |
| `markets.open_protocol_markets.v1` | planned | Liquidity/risk/compute-data markets are far-future relative to "be adoptable today." |
| `autopilot_sites.*` (e.g. `autopilot_sites.native_email_sequences.v1`, `custom_tenant_hostnames.v1`, `partner_payout_ledger.v1`, `site_build_and_host.v1`) | yellow/red | Sites is a distinct product line; not on the inference wedge. Keep maintenance only. |
| `mobile.*` (`voice_approval_companion.v1`, `autopilot_remote_control.v1`, `voice_session_evidence_transcript_ingest.v1`) | yellow/red/planned | Mobile operator surfaces are orthogonal to inference adoption; park new lanes. |
| `world.multiplayer_agent_world.v1` & broad Verse work | planned | Keep only the thin "Verse visualizes Khala traffic" dogfood slice (Pillar 1.5); park the broader multiplayer-world promise during the push. |
| `energy.flexible_load_proof.v1`, `compute.agentic_kernel_optimization_at_scale.v1` | planned | Compute/energy demonstrations are not the adoption wedge; defer. |

**Explicitly NOT deprioritized (stay hot):** the inference family
(`inference.*`), the credits/billing seam that unblocks the paid loop
(`payments.autopilot_credits_purchase.v1` red, `autopilot.cloud_credits_ui.v1`
yellow), the benchmark harness work, and the green training/verification
foundation the gym dogfood reuses. The paid-loop blocker
(`..._inference_paid_credits_card_to_credit_not_collectable`) is the one
owner-gated item worth keeping warm because it unblocks the most inference
promises at once — but it does not block the free-API + demand + ecosystem work,
which is why the GTM push ships those first.

## 4. Bottom line

- The push advances the whole `inference.*` family's **evidence and demand** but
  flips nothing green by itself; the shared bottleneck is the owner-gated
  collectable paid loop.
- Two **green-eligible** promises are **missing** and should exist: a clean
  "free live OpenAI-compatible Khala API" promise and a "public tokens-served
  metric" promise. Wording proposed above; creation/flip is owner-gated.
- Park the fine-tuning/sandbox/batch, broad-training, marketplace, world,
  mobile, sites, and world-first-claim lanes during the push; concentrate lanes
  on tokens-served growth (dogfood + OpenCode + benchmark) and on keeping the
  paid-loop blocker warm.","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":153,"totalLines":153,"truncated":false}},"title":"docs/promises/2026-06-25-khala-inference-push-promise-review.md","time":{"start":1782358588933,"end":1782358588947}}}

1: // Khala FREE API MODE (issue #6228, EPIC #5474). The self-serve FREE tier: a
2: // FREE API key (`POST /api/keys/free`) plus a FREE inference lane that calls the
3: // single public model `openagents/khala` (own-infra GPT-OSS / Gemini Flash) with
4: // NO funded balance, within a per-key daily quota. Beyond the quota — or for
5: // premium lanes — credits/budget are still required (the existing balance + 402
6: // path), so paid Khala behavior for funded keys is unchanged.
7: //
8: // WHY THIS IS A KEY-TIER, NOT A NEW MODEL. There is exactly one public model
9: // `openagents/khala`; `khala-mini`/`khala-code` are not public products. "Free"
10: // is a property of the KEY/account + the lane, mirroring the two existing
11: // balance-gate bypasses (`inference-free-allowance.ts` — owner-keyed Sybil pool
12: // on Gemini; `inference-operator-exemption.ts` — owner-keyed operator credit on
13: // own-infra). This module adds the THIRD bypass: a per-KEY free tier on the
14: // public Khala lane, the one anyone can self-serve. It reuses the SAME agent
15: // bearer-token auth (a free key is a normal `oa_agent_` credential), the SAME
16: // balance-gate seam shape (`checkFreeTier`), and the SAME metering-hook decorator
17: // shape (`withFreeTierKhala` records a zero-debit free receipt + accrues quota).
18: //
19: // HARD GUARDRAILS:
20: //   - Own-infra / non-premium ONLY. The free lane is `openagents/khala` (which
21: //     classifies `open`, routed to own GPT-OSS/Gemini). A PREMIUM model
22: //     (`claude` / `unknown`) is NEVER free here — it still hits the normal
23: //     balance + premium-grant gates, so a free key can never rack up real
24: //     third-party cost. We reuse `isPremiumModel` (model-router classes) so the
25: //     premium set stays single-source.
26: //   - Quota-bounded. A free key has a per-UTC-day request count + served-token
27: //     ceiling. Over-quota requests fall through to the normal balance gate (402),
28: //     so free is genuinely free WITHIN the quota and paid beyond it.
29: //   - Mint is abuse-bounded. The self-serve mint endpoint is per-IP-hash,
30: //     per-day rate-limited (no unbounded key minting) and never echoes a raw IP.
31: //
32: // PUBLIC-SAFE. Every table this touches carries account refs, IP-hash refs,
33: // model ids, bounded integer counters, and timestamps only — never prompts,
34: // completions, wallet/payment material, raw tokens, raw IPs, or secrets. The
35: // decorator never fails the customer's inference call: an accrual error logs a
36: // public-safe diagnostic and falls through to normal metering rather than
37: // breaking.
38: //
39: // INERT until INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED is on AND the gateway is enabled.
40: 
41: import { Effect } from 'effect'
42: 
43: import { workerLogEntry } from '../observability'
44: import { currentIsoTimestamp } from '../runtime-primitives'
45: import { isPremiumModel } from './inference-premium-allowlist'
46: import { isKhalaModel } from './pricing'
47: import {
48:   type MeteringContext,
49:   type MeteringHook,
50:   type MeteringOutcome,
51: } from './metering-hook'
52: 
53: class FreeTierPersistenceError extends Error {
54:   readonly _tag = 'FreeTierPersistenceError'
55: 
56:   constructor(cause: unknown) {
57:     super(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : String(cause))
58:     this.name = 'FreeTierPersistenceError'
59:   }
60: }
61: 
62: const freeTierPersistenceError = (error: unknown) =>
63:   new FreeTierPersistenceError(error)
64: 
65: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
66: // Env flag (fail-closed, default OFF)
67: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
68: 
69: export const INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED_ENV_KEY =
70:   'INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED' as const
71: 
72: const ON_TOKENS = new Set(['1', 'on', 'true', 'yes'])
73: 
74: // Fail-closed flag read. Absent / non-string / any non-on value => disabled.
75: export const isFreeTierEnabled = (value: unknown): boolean =>
76:   typeof value === 'string' && ON_TOKENS.has(value.trim().toLowerCase())
77: 
78: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
79: // Tunable quota constants (all free-tier thresholds in ONE place)
80: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
81: 
82: // Per-key, per-UTC-day FREE request ceiling. The last allowed request lands the
83: // counter AT the ceiling; the next one falls through to the normal balance gate.
84: // !! TUNABLE: issue #6228.
85: export const FREE_TIER_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_DAY = 200 as const
86: 
87: // Per-key, per-UTC-day FREE served-token ceiling (prompt + completion). Bounds a
88: // single free key's draw on the shared own-infra pool regardless of request
89: // count. !! TUNABLE: issue #6228.
90: export const FREE_TIER_MAX_TOKENS_PER_DAY = 200_000 as const
91: 
92: // Per-IP-hash, per-UTC-day self-serve MINT ceiling so anonymous minting is
93: // bounded (no unbounded key minting). !! TUNABLE: issue #6228.
94: export const FREE_KEY_MAX_MINTS_PER_IP_PER_DAY = 25 as const
95: 
96: export type FreeTierQuota = Readonly<{
97:   maxRequestsPerDay: number
98:   maxTokensPerDay: number
99: }>
100: 
101: export const DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_QUOTA: FreeTierQuota = {
102:   maxRequestsPerDay: FREE_TIER_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_DAY,
103:   maxTokensPerDay: FREE_TIER_MAX_TOKENS_PER_DAY,
104: }
105: 
106: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
107: // UTC day bucket
108: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
109: 
110: // The UTC day bucket (YYYY-MM-DD) the free quota resets on. Derived from an ISO
111: // timestamp so tests can inject a fixed clock.
112: export const freeTierUsageDay = (nowIso: string): string =>
113:   nowIso.slice(0, 10)
114: 
115: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
116: // Free-lane eligibility (own-infra / non-premium public Khala ONLY)
117: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
118: 
119: export const FREE_TIER_REASON_ELIGIBLE =
120:   'reason.inference_free_tier.eligible' as const
121: export const FREE_TIER_REASON_NOT_FREE_LANE =
122:   'reason.inference_free_tier.not_free_lane' as const
123: export const FREE_TIER_REASON_PREMIUM_DENIED =
124:   'reason.inference_free_tier.premium_never_free' as const
125: 
126: // Whether a requested model is on the FREE lane: it must be the single public
127: // Khala model AND not a premium class (defense in depth — Khala classifies as
128: // `open`, never premium, so the premium check can only ever protect against a
129: // future reclassification). Bounded id/class check, never an intent parser.
130: export const isFreeTierLaneModel = (model: string): boolean =>
131:   isKhalaModel(model) && !isPremiumModel(model)
132: 
133: export type FreeTierLaneDecision = Readonly<{
134:   freeLane: boolean
135:   premium: boolean
136:   reasonRef: string
137: }>
138: 
139: export const decideFreeTierLane = (model: string): FreeTierLaneDecision => {
140:   // GUARDRAIL: a premium model is never free, even if it were ever aliased to
141:   // Khala. Premium is deny-by-default here.
142:   if (isPremiumModel(model)) {
143:     return {
144:       freeLane: false,
145:       premium: true,
146:       reasonRef: FREE_TIER_REASON_PREMIUM_DENIED,
147:     }
148:   }
149:   if (isKhalaModel(model)) {
150:     return {
151:       freeLane: true,
152:       premium: false,
153:       reasonRef: FREE_TIER_REASON_ELIGIBLE,
154:     }
155:   }
156:   return {
157:     freeLane: false,
158:     premium: false,
159:     reasonRef: FREE_TIER_REASON_NOT_FREE_LANE,
160:   }
161: }
162: 
163: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
164: // Pure quota decision
165: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
166: 
167: export type FreeTierUsage = Readonly<{
168:   requestsToday: number
169:   tokensToday: number
170: }>
171: 
172: export type FreeTierQuotaDecision = Readonly<{
173:   // True when THIS request fits under BOTH the daily request and token
174:   // ceilings. False => fall through to the normal balance gate (402).
175:   withinQuota: boolean
176:   maxRequestsPerDay: number
177:   maxTokensPerDay: number
178:   remainingRequests: number
179:   remainingTokens: number
180:   reasonRef: string
181: }>
182: 
183: export const FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_WITHIN =
184:   'reason.inference_free_tier.within_quota' as const
185: export const FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_REQUESTS_EXCEEDED =
186:   'reason.inference_free_tier.daily_requests_exceeded' as const
187: export const FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_TOKENS_EXCEEDED =
188:   'reason.inference_free_tier.daily_tokens_exceeded' as const
189: 
190: // Decide whether a free key may take ANOTHER request today. Pure. A request is
191: // admitted only when the already-used request count is UNDER the request ceiling
192: // AND the already-used token count is UNDER the token ceiling. A key that has
193: // already issued >= the request ceiling, or already drawn >= the token ceiling,
194: // is over quota (falls through to the balance gate). Pre-flight has no per-
195: // request token estimate, so the token check is against the already-used total.
196: export const decideFreeTierQuota = (
197:   input: Readonly<{
198:     usage: FreeTierUsage
199:     quota?: FreeTierQuota | undefined
200:   }>,
201: ): FreeTierQuotaDecision => {
202:   const quota = input.quota ?? DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_QUOTA
203:   const requests = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(input.usage.requestsToday))
204:   const tokens = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(input.usage.tokensToday))
205:   const remainingRequests = Math.max(0, quota.maxRequestsPerDay - requests)
206:   const remainingTokens = Math.max(0, quota.maxTokensPerDay - tokens)
207: 
208:   if (requests >= quota.maxRequestsPerDay) {
209:     return {
210:       maxRequestsPerDay: quota.maxRequestsPerDay,
211:       maxTokensPerDay: quota.maxTokensPerDay,
212:       reasonRef: FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_REQUESTS_EXCEEDED,
213:       remainingRequests: 0,
214:       remainingTokens,
215:       withinQuota: false,
216:     }
217:   }
218:   if (tokens >= quota.maxTokensPerDay) {
219:     return {
220:       maxRequestsPerDay: quota.maxRequestsPerDay,
221:       maxTokensPerDay: quota.maxTokensPerDay,
222:       reasonRef: FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_TOKENS_EXCEEDED,
223:       remainingRequests,
224:       remainingTokens: 0,
225:       withinQuota: false,
226:     }
227:   }
228:   return {
229:     maxRequestsPerDay: quota.maxRequestsPerDay,
230:     maxTokensPerDay: quota.maxTokensPerDay,
231:     reasonRef: FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_WITHIN,
232:     remainingRequests,
233:     remainingTokens,
234:     withinQuota: true,
235:   }
236: }
237: 
238: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
239: // Free-tier key store (mark + read)
240: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
241: 
242: export const DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_SCOPE = 'free_khala_daily' as const
243: 
244: // Plain-async read of whether an account is on the free tier. The route gate (a
245: // Promise-returning seam) uses this directly. Returns false on any read error
246: // (fail-closed).
247: export const readAccountFreeTier = async (
248:   db: D1Database,
249:   accountRef: string,
250: ): Promise<boolean> => {
251:   try {
252:     const row = await db
253:       .prepare(
254:         `SELECT account_ref FROM inference_free_tier_keys WHERE account_ref = ? LIMIT 1`,
255:       )
256:       .bind(accountRef)
257:       .first<{ account_ref: string }>()
258:     return row !== null
259:   } catch {
260:     return false
261:   }
262: }

264: // Plain-async mark of an account as a free-tier key (idempotent upsert), for
265: // callers already in a Promise context (e.g. the mint route handler) so no
266: // Effect->Promise bridge runs there. Returns whether the write succeeded.
267: export const markAccountFreeTierAsync = async (
268:   db: D1Database,
269:   input: Readonly<{
270:     accountRef: string
271:     mintSource?: string | undefined
272:     note?: string | null | undefined
273:     scope?: string | undefined

294:         input.note ?? null,
295:         nowIso,

297:       )
298:       .run()

302:   }
303: }
304: 
305: // Plain-async record of one mint against an IP-hash for the day (idempotent
306: // increment), for callers already in a Promise context. Returns whether the
307: // write succeeded.
308: export const recordFreeKeyMintAsync = async (
309:   db: D1Database,
310:   input: Readonly<{
311:     ipHash: string

313:     nowIso?: string | undefined
314:   }>,
315: ): Promise<boolean> => {

324:            mint_count = mint_count + 1,

326:       )

335: // Mark an account as a free-tier key (idempotent upsert). Called by the
336: // self-serve mint endpoint after registering the underlying agent credential.
337: export const markAccountFreeTier = (
338:   db: D1Database,
339:   input: Readonly<{
340:     accountRef: string
341:     mintSource?: string | undefined
342:     note?: string | null | undefined
343:     scope?: string | undefined
344:     nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
345:   }>,
346: ): Effect.Effect<boolean> =>
347:   Effect.gen(function* () {
348:     const nowIso = (input.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp)()
349:     return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
350:       catch: freeTierPersistenceError,
351:       try: async () => {
352:         await db
353:           .prepare(
354:             `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_keys
355:                (account_ref, scope, mint_source, note, created_at, updated_at)
356:              VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
357:              ON CONFLICT(account_ref) DO UPDATE SET
358:                scope = excluded.scope,
359:                mint_source = excluded.mint_source,
360:                note = excluded.note,
361:                updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
362:           )
363:           .bind(
364:             input.accountRef,
365:             input.scope ?? DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_SCOPE,
366:             input.mintSource ?? 'self_serve_anonymous',
367:             input.note ?? null,
368:             nowIso,
369:             nowIso,
370:           )
371:           .run()

373:       },
374:     }).pipe(Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(false)))

377: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
378: // Free-tier daily usage read + idempotent accrual
379: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
380: 
381: type FreeTierUsageRow = Readonly<{
382:   free_request_count: number
383:   free_total_tokens: number
384: }>
385: 
386: // Read the account's current free usage for the given UTC day. A missing row
387: // means $0/0 used today. Read-only and bounded.
388: export const readFreeTierUsage = async (
389:   db: D1Database,
390:   accountRef: string,
391:   usageDay: string,
392: ): Promise<FreeTierUsage> => {
393:   const row = await db
394:     .prepare(
395:       `SELECT free_request_count, free_total_tokens
396:          FROM inference_free_tier_usage
397:         WHERE account_ref = ? AND usage_day = ?
398:         LIMIT 1`,
399:     )
400:     .bind(accountRef, usageDay)
401:     .first<FreeTierUsageRow>()
402:   return {
403:     requestsToday:
404:       typeof row?.free_request_count === 'number' ? row.free_request_count : 0,
405:     tokensToday:
406:       typeof row?.free_total_tokens === 'number' ? row.free_total_tokens : 0,
407:   }
408: }
409: 
410: // Accrue one free request (+ its served tokens) against the account's daily
411: // tally, idempotently. Writes the per-request event row (UNIQUE request_id) and
412: // increments the daily tally in ONE D1 batch. On a duplicate request id the
413: // UNIQUE constraint aborts the batch and we treat it as already-accrued (no
414: // double-count). Returns whether a NEW accrual was recorded.
415: const accrueFreeTierUsage = async (
416:   db: D1Database,
417:   input: Readonly<{
418:     accountRef: string
419:     usageDay: string
420:     requestId: string
421:     servedModel: string
422:     totalTokens: number
423:     nowIso: string
424:   }>,
425: ): Promise<boolean> => {
426:   try {
427:     await db.batch([
428:       db
429:         .prepare(
430:           `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_usage_events
431:              (request_id, account_ref, usage_day, served_model, total_tokens, created_at)
432:            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
433:         )
434:         .bind(
435:           input.requestId,
436:           input.accountRef,
437:           input.usageDay,
438:           input.servedModel,
439:           input.totalTokens,
440:           input.nowIso,
441:         ),
442:       db
443:         .prepare(
444:           `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_usage
445:              (account_ref, usage_day, free_request_count, free_total_tokens, created_at, updated_at)
446:            VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, ?, ?)
447:            ON CONFLICT(account_ref, usage_day) DO UPDATE SET
448:              free_request_count = free_request_count + 1,
449:              free_total_tokens = free_total_tokens + excluded.free_total_tokens,
450:              updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
451:         )
452:         .bind(
453:           input.accountRef,
454:           input.usageDay,
455:           input.totalTokens,
456:           input.nowIso,
457:           input.nowIso,
458:         ),
459:     ])
460:     return true
461:   } catch {
462:     // Duplicate request id => already accrued. Idempotent no-op.
463:     return false
464:   }
465: }
466: 
467: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
468: // Mint abuse guard (per-IP-hash, per-day)
469: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
470: 
471: export type FreeKeyMintGateDecision = Readonly<{
472:   allowed: boolean
473:   mintsToday: number
474:   maxMintsPerDay: number
475:   reasonRef: string
476: }>
477: 
478: export const FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_ALLOWED =
479:   'reason.inference_free_key_mint.allowed' as const
480: export const FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_RATE_LIMITED =
481:   'reason.inference_free_key_mint.daily_ip_limit_exceeded' as const
482: 
483: // Pure mint-rate decision: a new mint is allowed only when the IP's already-used
484: // mint count today is UNDER the daily ceiling.
485: export const decideFreeKeyMint = (
486:   input: Readonly<{
487:     mintsToday: number
488:     maxMintsPerDay?: number
489:   }>,
490: ): FreeKeyMintGateDecision => {
491:   const max = Math.max(
492:     1,
493:     Math.trunc(input.maxMintsPerDay ?? FREE_KEY_MAX_MINTS_PER_IP_PER_DAY),
494:   )
495:   const mints = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(input.mintsToday))
496:   if (mints >= max) {
497:     return {
498:       allowed: false,
499:       maxMintsPerDay: max,
500:       mintsToday: mints,
501:       reasonRef: FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_RATE_LIMITED,
502:     }
503:   }
504:   return {
505:     allowed: true,
506:     maxMintsPerDay: max,
507:     mintsToday: mints,
508:     reasonRef: FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_ALLOWED,
509:   }
510: }
511: 
512: // Read how many free keys an IP-hash has minted today. Returns 0 on any read
513: // error so a transient DB blip cannot block minting forever (the per-mint write
514: // below is still bounded; this read is the pre-flight signal).
515: export const readFreeKeyMintsToday = async (
516:   db: D1Database,
517:   ipHash: string,
518:   mintDay: string,
519: ): Promise<number> => {
520:   try {
521:     const row = await db
522:       .prepare(
523:         `SELECT mint_count FROM inference_free_key_mints
524:           WHERE ip_hash = ? AND mint_day = ? LIMIT 1`,
525:       )
526:       .bind(ipHash, mintDay)
527:       .first<{ mint_count: number }>()
528:     return typeof row?.mint_count === 'number' ? row.mint_count : 0
529:   } catch {
530:     return 0
531:   }
532: }
533: 
534: // Record one mint against an IP-hash for the day (idempotent increment). Returns
535: // whether the write succeeded; a failure is logged by the caller (the mint still
536: // proceeds — the pre-flight read is the primary bound).
537: export const recordFreeKeyMint = (
538:   db: D1Database,
539:   input: Readonly<{
540:     ipHash: string
541:     mintDay: string
542:     nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
543:   }>,
544: ): Effect.Effect<boolean> =>
545:   Effect.gen(function* () {
546:     const nowIso = (input.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp)()
547:     return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
548:       catch: freeTierPersistenceError,
549:       try: async () => {
550:         await db
551:           .prepare(
552:             `INSERT INTO inference_free_key_mints
553:                (ip_hash, mint_day, mint_count, created_at, updated_at)
554:              VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, ?)
555:              ON CONFLICT(ip_hash, mint_day) DO UPDATE SET
556:                mint_count = mint_count + 1,
557:                updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
558:           )
559:           .bind(input.ipHash, input.mintDay, nowIso, nowIso)
560:           .run()
561:         return true
562:       },
563:     }).pipe(Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(false)))
564:   })
565: 
566: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
567: // Balance-gate seam (the free-tier bypass)
568: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
569: 
570: export type FreeTierGateDecision = Readonly<{
571:   // True when the request may BYPASS the balance gate: a free-tier key, on the
572:   // free Khala lane, within the daily quota. False => the gate is unchanged (the
573:   // request must clear the normal balance gate; paid-Khala behavior intact).
574:   free: boolean
575:   reasonRef: string
576: }>
577: 
578: export type FreeTierGateDeps = Readonly<{
579:   db: D1Database
580:   quota?: FreeTierQuota
581:   nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
582: }>
583: 
584: // A route-level balance-gate bypass: given an account ref + requested model,
585: // returns whether the request may bypass the balance gate as a free-tier call.
586: // PREMIUM / non-Khala models short-circuit to NOT-free WITHOUT a quota read.
587: // Wired into the chat-completions `checkFreeTier` seam (open/no-op when unwired
588: // or flag-off). Fail-closed: any read error => not free (the 402 stands).
589: export type FreeTierGate = (
590:   accountRef: string,
591:   model: string,
592: ) => Promise<FreeTierGateDecision>
593: 
594: export const makeFreeTierGate = (deps: FreeTierGateDeps): FreeTierGate => {
595:   const nowIso = deps.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp
596:   return async (accountRef: string, model: string) => {
597:     const lane = decideFreeTierLane(model)
598:     if (!lane.freeLane) {
599:       return { free: false, reasonRef: lane.reasonRef }
600:     }
601:     try {
602:       const isFree = await readAccountFreeTier(deps.db, accountRef)
603:       if (!isFree) {
604:         return {
605:           free: false,
606:           reasonRef: 'reason.inference_free_tier.account_not_free_tier',
607:         }
608:       }
609:       const usageDay = freeTierUsageDay(nowIso())
610:       const usage = await readFreeTierUsage(deps.db, accountRef, usageDay)
611:       const quota = decideFreeTierQuota({ quota: deps.quota, usage })
612:       return {
613:         free: quota.withinQuota,
614:         reasonRef: quota.reasonRef,
615:       }
616:     } catch {
617:       // Read error: do NOT bypass the balance gate.
618:       return {
619:         free: false,
620:         reasonRef: 'reason.inference_free_tier.read_error',
621:       }
622:     }
623:   }
624: }
625: 
626: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
627: // Free-tier metering wrapper (zero-debit, receipt-first, no referral)
628: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
629: 
630: // Public-safe free-tier receipt ref. Resolvable without exposing any account id,
631: // amount, destination, or payment material. The `free_tier` infix marks the
632: // honest zero-debit free-mode accounting state.
633: export const freeTierReceiptRef = (requestId: string): string =>
634:   `receipt.inference.free_tier.${requestId}`
635: 
636: const freeTierOutcome = (requestId: string): MeteringOutcome => ({
637:   metered: false,
638:   receiptRef: freeTierReceiptRef(requestId),
639: })
640: 
641: export type FreeTierMeteringDeps = Readonly<{
642:   db: D1Database
643:   quota?: FreeTierQuota
644:   nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
645: }>
646: 
647: /**
648:  * Wrap a metering hook so a FREE-TIER key's request for the free Khala lane,
649:  * WITHIN the daily quota, is recorded as a zero-debit free receipt (receipt-
650:  * first, NO credit decrement, NO referral) AND its usage is accrued against the
651:  * daily quota (idempotent per request). Otherwise it falls through to the inner
652:  * hook unchanged.
653:  *
654:  * GUARDRAILS:
655:  *   - A PREMIUM served model ALWAYS falls through to the inner hook (the normal
656:  *     ledger debit), even for a free-tier key — a free key never gets a premium
657:  *     model free.
658:  *   - A non-free-tier account, a non-Khala model, an OVER-quota request, or any
659:  *     DB error falls through to the inner hook (charge), so free is genuinely
660:  *     free only within the quota and never grants usage we could not account
661:  *     for. The customer's inference call is never failed by the bookkeeping.
662:  *
663:  * Wired alongside `withFreeAllowance` / `withOperatorCredit`. It runs AFTER
664:  * dispatch; the route's `checkFreeTier` balance-gate seam is what lets the
665:  * zero-balance free-tier request reach dispatch in the first place. The two
666:  * agree because they share the SAME store + quota + UTC-day bucket.
667:  */
668: export const withFreeTierKhala = (
669:   inner: MeteringHook,
670:   deps: FreeTierMeteringDeps,
671: ): MeteringHook => {
672:   const nowIso = deps.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp
673:   return (context: MeteringContext) =>
674:     Effect.gen(function* () {
675:       // Only the free Khala lane is ever free here; premium / non-Khala meter.
676:       // Key on the SERVED model (the lane that actually incurred cost), matching
677:       // the ledger hook + the other decorators.
678:       if (!isFreeTierLaneModel(context.servedModel)) {
679:         return yield* inner(context)
680:       }
681: 
682:       const gated = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
683:         catch: freeTierPersistenceError,
684:         try: async () => {
685:           const isFree = await readAccountFreeTier(deps.db, context.accountRef)
686:           if (!isFree) {
687:             return { accrued: false, free: false as const }
688:           }
689:           const now = nowIso()
690:           const usageDay = freeTierUsageDay(now)
691:           const usage = await readFreeTierUsage(
692:             deps.db,
693:             context.accountRef,
694:             usageDay,
695:           )
696:           const quota = decideFreeTierQuota({ quota: deps.quota, usage })
697:           if (!quota.withinQuota) {
698:             return { accrued: false, free: false as const }
699:           }
700:           const accrued = await accrueFreeTierUsage(deps.db, {
701:             accountRef: context.accountRef,
702:             nowIso: now,
703:             requestId: context.requestId,
704:             servedModel: context.servedModel,
705:             totalTokens: Math.max(0, Math.trunc(context.usage.totalTokens)),
706:             usageDay,
707:           })
708:           return { accrued, free: true as const }
709:         },
710:       }).pipe(
711:         Effect.catch(error =>
712:           Effect.gen(function* () {
713:             // Public-safe diagnostic only; never break the inference response.
714:             // On error meter normally (charge) rather than grant a free request
715:             // we could not account for.
716:             yield* Effect.logInfo(
717:               workerLogEntry('inference.free_tier.error', {
718:                 accountRef: context.accountRef,
719:                 adapterId: context.adapterId,
720:                 reason: error.message,
721:                 requestId: context.requestId,
722:                 servedModel: context.servedModel,
723:               }),
724:             )
725:             return { accrued: false, free: false as const }
726:           }),
727:         ),
728:       )
729: 
730:       if (!gated.free) {
731:         // Not free-tier, over quota, or error: meter normally (decrement).
732:         return yield* inner(context)
733:       }
734: 
735:       // Free-tier within quota: record the zero-debit free receipt + accrual and
736:       // return WITHOUT calling the inner hook (no decrement, no referral). Log a
737:       // public-safe diagnostic (refs + token counts only).
738:       yield* Effect.logInfo(
739:         workerLogEntry('inference.free_tier.granted', {
740:           accountRef: context.accountRef,
741:           accrued: gated.accrued,
742:           adapterId: context.adapterId,
743:           requestId: context.requestId,
744:           servedModel: context.servedModel,
745:           totalTokens: context.usage.totalTokens,
746:         }),
747:       )
748:       return freeTierOutcome(context.requestId) satisfies MeteringOutcome
749:     })
750: }
751: 
752: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
753: // Self-serve mint (pure helpers; the Worker route wires registration + IP hash)
754: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
755: 
756: // The public-safe request body for `POST /api/keys/free`. Anonymous by default;
757: // an optional public-safe label (e.g. an app name) helps the owner attribute
758: // keys. NO email is required (the simplest safe option — abuse is bounded by the
759: // per-IP mint rate limit + the per-key quota), and an optional email, if sent,
760: // is only used as the registration display label, never verified or stored as
761: // PII beyond the agent record.
762: export type FreeKeyMintRequest = Readonly<{
763:   label?: string | null | undefined
764: }>
765: 
766: // Bound the display label so the minted credential name is sane.
767: export const FREE_KEY_LABEL_MAX_LENGTH = 80 as const
768: 
769: export const sanitizeFreeKeyLabel = (
770:   label: string | null | undefined,
771: ): string => {
772:   const trimmed = (label ?? '').trim()
773:   if (trimmed === '') {
774:     return 'Free API key'
775:   }
776:   return trimmed.slice(0, FREE_KEY_LABEL_MAX_LENGTH)
777: }

## Project Conventions

- Read `DESIGN.md` before changing UI. OpenAgents Autopilot uses the Vortex dark,
  mono, operational design direction; do not introduce light mode, decorative
  gradients, card-heavy marketing shells, or generic chat-first framing.
- When writing GitHub issue comments with `gh`, never pass Markdown containing
  escaped `\n` sequences in a quoted `--body` string. Use real newlines via
  `--body-file -`, a heredoc/stdin body, or a body variable populated from a
  heredoc. For important issue comments, verify the rendered body with
  `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/issues/comments/<comment-id> --jq .body` before
  moving on.
- Before editing a UI surface for a named URL, trace the actual rendered
  entrypoint from routing/startup through the top-level view and verify which
  component produces the live DOM. Do not assume that a same-named file is the
  route owner. For example, live `/login` is rendered by `apps/web/src/view.ts`
  via the GitHub OAuth login button, while
  `apps/web/src/page/loggedOut/page/login.ts` is a legacy/local auth view and
  is not the production `/login` surface. When changing deployed UI, verify
  against the served bundle or rendered DOM before calling the work complete.
- When the user asks to deploy this repo, always build the latest web assets
  before deploying the Worker. For JS/CSS/public-doc/web-asset-only changes,
  prefer the no-container path in
  `docs/2026-06-15-openagents-web-deploy-runbook.md`: run
  `bun run check:deploy`, run `bun run build:web`, then deploy from
  `apps/openagents.com/workers/api` with
  `npx wrangler deploy --containers-rollout=none --assets ../../apps/web/dist`
  so Wrangler does not build or update Containers. Use
  `bun run --cwd workers/api deploy` for full production deploys that need its
  orchestration, such as container image rollout or remote D1 migrations. Do
  not run bare `wrangler deploy` after UI/web changes. Never report deployment
  success until live smoke checks prove both the document and JS asset are
  reachable:
  `curl -fsSI https://openagents.com/` and the concrete
  `/assets/index-*.js` URL referenced by the served HTML must both return 200.
- Before changing Worker route/service boundaries, sync/runtime/config code,
  provider-account or GitHub-write error handling, logged-in update routing,
  login/root route behavior, UI family modules, or other zero-tech-debt
  cleanup surfaces, read
  `docs/2026-06-04-openagents-zero-tech-debt-caller-inventory.md`. Treat that
  document as a forward contract, not just a historical audit: do not increase
  compatibility budgets, add `Effect.runPromise` bridges, add Promise
  dependency adapters, reintroduce deleted aliases/files, or bypass typed
  service/layer boundaries unless the same change adds caller evidence,
  deletion conditions, and an architecture guardrail.
- User-facing product copy must not explain implementation mechanics,
  internal dispatch paths, grant plumbing, auth materialization, sync internals,
  SHC handoff details, or "how it works" process notes. Put that material in
  docs, logs, metadata dialogs, runbooks, or operator-only surfaces. Product UI
  should use short labels, values, statuses, and direct actions such as
  "Connect", "Reconnect", "Open Settings", or "Check status".
- Product UI icons must come from the generated Fireball Apps SDK icon catalog
  in `apps/web/src/icon.ts`. Type icon props as `IconName` and render through
  `iconView` or `IconService`; do not add ad hoc SVG paths, Unicode/text icon
  stand-ins, image icon URLs, icon fonts, lucide/react-icons, Iconify, or new
  icon dependencies. If a needed icon is missing, update the upstream Fireball
  catalog first, then run `bun run sync:icons` and keep `apps/web/src/icon*.test.ts`
  passing.
- Training and proof replay visual surfaces must use the existing
  `@openagentsinc/three-effect` renderer vocabulary and the visual taxonomy
  documented in `docs/launch/2026-06-17-tassadar-training-run-visual-language.md`
  plus the `/animations` studies. `@openagentsinc/proof-replay` owns replay
  bundle shape, clocks, source gates, and render plans only; it is not a visual
  renderer. Browser or desktop app code may adapt public replay bundles into
  `three-effect` options and may render Foldkit controls, inspectors, transcript
  mirrors, and accessibility fallbacks. Do not add new app-local DOM/CSS/canvas
  stages, actor/avatar renderers, payment-zap effects, camera grammar, particles,
  or other proof replay visuals. Add missing primitives to
  `/Users/christopherdavid/work/three-effect` first, then consume the package.
  The current `apps/web/src/scene/tassadarProofReplayElement.ts` is a temporary
  legacy bridge for the first replay route; do not extend its visual language
  except to replace it with `three-effect` mounts.
- Use Tailwind utility classes and the local Foldkit UI registry as the default
  styling path. For Worker-rendered HTML, put Tailwind classes in the template
  and make sure `apps/web/src/styles.css` sources the Worker templates so the
  classes compile into `/assets/openagents.css`.
- Vanilla CSS is a last resort. Use it only for global base/reset rules,
  platform quirks, third-party integration constraints, or selectors that
  cannot be expressed clearly with Tailwind. Do not add one-off vanilla CSS
  blocks for product surfaces when Tailwind utilities or UI registry primitives
  can express the same layout.
- Foldkit is tightly coupled to the Effect ecosystem. Do not suggest solutions outside of Effect-TS.
- Model fields must be Schema types (the model is a schema). Plain TypeScript types are fine elsewhere (function return types, local variables, etc.).
- Use full names like `Message` (not `Msg`), and `withReturnType` (not `as const` or type casting).
- Use `m()` for message schemas, `ts()` for tagged structs (model states, field validation), and `r()` for route schemas.
- Push back on any direction that violates Elm Architecture principles: unidirectional data flow, messages as facts (not commands), model as single source of truth, side effects confined to commands. If a prompt suggests mutating state, imperative event handlers, or two-way bindings, flag the issue and propose the idiomatic Foldkit approach.
- Never use `NoOp`. Every message must describe what happened. Fire-and-forget commands use `Completed*` messages mirroring the Command name verb-first: `LockScroll` → `CompletedLockScroll`.

## Pylon Presence Auth Contract (token-only)

Pylon presence and lifecycle writes (`POST /api/pylons/:ref/heartbeat`,
`/wallet-readiness`, `/payout-target-admission`, `/assignments/*`, and
`/register`) are authenticated with an OpenAgents agent **bearer token**
(`Authorization: Bearer <agent token>`) in `workers/api/src/pylon-api-routes.ts`
(`requireAgent`). This is intentional and is the documented contract.

A node's self-held Nostr key (NIP-98) is **not** accepted as presence
authority. Registrations are bound to `ownerAgentUserId` derived from the
bearer-token session; the registry does not bind a verified Nostr pubkey to
that owner (`providerNostrPubkey` is optional discovery metadata, only set for
NIP-90 provider Pylons, and is never verified server-side). Accepting a
self-signed heartbeat would require server-side NIP-98 schnorr verification
plus a mandatory pubkey→owner binding — a broader auth change that must not be
made implicitly.

When a presence request arrives with a `Nostr`/NIP-98 `Authorization` scheme
and no usable bearer token, the route returns a typed, explanatory `401`
(`error: pylon_api_presence_requires_agent_token`, `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`)
naming the token-only contract and pointing the node at the bearer path,
rather than a bare `unauthorized` (#5058). Do not silently fall back to a bare
401 for the Nostr-signed presence path. If self-signed presence is ever to be
supported, design the NIP-98 verification + pubkey binding deliberately with a
new dated review and tests; do not broaden any other authority (spend,
settlement, moderation) in the process. Regression coverage lives in
`workers/api/src/pylon-api-routes.test.ts`.

## Foldkit Patterns

### Update

`init` and `update` both return `[Model, ReadonlyArray<Command<Message>>]`:

```ts
type UpdateReturn = readonly [Model, ReadonlyArray<Command<Message>>]
const withUpdateReturn = M.withReturnType<UpdateReturn>()

const update = (model: Model, message: Message): UpdateReturn =>
  M.value(message).pipe(
    withUpdateReturn,
    M.tagsExhaustive({
      ClickedIncrement: () => [evo(model, { count: count => count + 1 }), []],
    }),
  )
```

Use `evo()` from `foldkit/struct` for immutable model updates. Never spread or `Object.assign`.

### View

Bind the `html` factory inside each view function with `const h = html<Message>()` (never at module level), then reach for `h.div`, `h.OnClick`, etc. off the returned record. Use `empty` (not `null`) for conditional rendering, `M.value().pipe(M.tagsExhaustive({...}))` for discriminated unions, and `Array.match` for lists that may be empty.

Use `keyed` wrappers whenever the view branches into structurally different layouts based on route or model state. Without keying, the virtual DOM tries to diff one layout into another, causing stale DOM and event handler mismatches.

### Commands

Define a Command with `Command.define`, which is curried: the first call binds the name (and optionally args + result Message schemas), and the second call binds the Effect. Assign definitions to PascalCase constants. Never inline in pipe chains. Commands catch all errors via `Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(FailedX(...)))` so side effects never crash the app. Definitions live colocated with the update function that returns them.

For the with-args shape, see `../projects/repos/foldkit/examples/weather/src/main.ts` or `../projects/repos/foldkit/examples/kanban/src/command.ts`. For an argless DOM-side-effect Command, the argless form in `kanban/src/command.ts` (`FocusAddCardInput`) is the canonical reference.

For DOM operations (focus, scroll, modals, scroll lock), Foldkit ships a `Dom` module. For time, randomness, UUIDs, and delays, use Effect's built-ins directly (`Clock`, `Random`, `Effect.uuid`, `Effect.sleep`). Don't reach for raw `document.querySelector`, `setTimeout`, `Date.now()`, or `Math.random()`.

### File Organization

This repo is a Bun workspace:

- `apps/web/` is the Foldkit/Vite browser app.
- `workers/api/` is the Cloudflare Worker API and Durable Object surface.
- `packages/sync-schema/` holds Effect Schema protocol models shared by browser and Worker code.
- `packages/sync-client/` holds browser-side sync helpers.
- `packages/sync-worker/` holds Worker-side sync helpers.

Keep Cloudflare runtime code out of `apps/web/`. Keep browser/Foldkit code out
of `workers/api/`. Shared code belongs in `packages/*` and must stay Effect
Schema-first at every external boundary.

A Foldkit app lives in two files. `src/main.ts` holds the pure definitions (Model, Messages, init, update, view). `src/entry.ts` imports them and boots the runtime with `Runtime.makeProgram` and `Runtime.run`. `index.html` references `entry.ts`. The split keeps `main.ts` importable from tests without booting a runtime as a side effect. Never call `Runtime.run` from `main.ts`.

Use uppercase section headers (`// MODEL`, `// MESSAGE`, `// INIT`, `// UPDATE`, `// COMMAND`, `// VIEW`) for wayfinding.

### Testing

Test update functions with `foldkit/test`. Since update is pure, tests run without a runtime, DOM, or side effects. Use `Story.story` for update-level tests (send Messages, assert on Model and Commands) and `Scene.scene` for feature-level testing through the view with accessible locators. Study the `.story.test.ts` and `.scene.test.ts` files in `../projects/repos/foldkit/examples/` when the external Foldkit reference exists.

## Code Style

- Encode state in discriminated unions, not booleans or nullable fields. `Idle | Loading | Error | Ok`, not `isLoading: boolean`. Make impossible states unrepresentable.
- Use `Option` instead of `null` or `undefined`. Prefix Option-typed values with `maybe*`. Match with `Option.match`; don't unwrap with `Option.map(...)` + `Option.getOrElse(...)` when you can just match.
- Use Effect modules over native methods in `pipe` chains (`Array.map`, `String.startsWith`, `Array.findFirst`). Native methods are fine when calling directly on a named variable.
- Never cast Schema values with `as Type`. Use the callable constructor: `SucceededLogin({ sessionId })`, not `{ _tag: 'SucceededLogin', sessionId } as Message`.
- Always `Array.isEmptyArray` / `Array.isNonEmptyArray` (not `.length === 0`). Use `Array.match` when handling both empty and non-empty cases.
- Never use `for` loops or `let` for iteration. Reach for `Array.map`, `Array.filterMap`, `Array.makeBy`, `Array.reduce`.
- Never use `T[]`. Always `Array<T>` or `ReadonlyArray<T>`.
- Always use `Effect.Match`, never `switch`.
- Always use braces for control flow: `if (foo) { return true }`.
- Don't add inline comments to explain code. Use better names instead. Reserve `// NOTE:` for behavior that would mislead a careful reader.

## Message Layout

Group all `m()` declarations together with no blank lines between them, then put `S.Union([...])` and `type Message = typeof Message.Type` on adjacent lines:

```ts
const ClickedSubmit = m('ClickedSubmit')
const UpdatedEmail = m('UpdatedEmail', { value: S.String })

const Message = S.Union([ClickedSubmit, UpdatedEmail])
type Message = typeof Message.Type
```

Messages are verb-first past-tense. Common prefixes: `Clicked*`, `Updated*` (input changes and external state updates), `Submitted*`, `Pressed*`, `Selected*`, `Succeeded*` / `Failed*` (paired async results), `Completed*` (fire-and-forget), `Got*` (child OutMessage in the Submodel pattern).

## Debugging

This project ships with `@foldkit/devtools-mcp` pre-wired. When the dev server is running and the app is open in a browser, `foldkit_*` MCP tools let you inspect Model, Message history, and time-travel. Reach for them before adding `console.log` whenever the question is about state or Message flow.

## Going Deeper

For Submodels and OutMessage, Subscriptions, Mount / ManagedResource / CustomElement, field validation, routing, accessibility, and the full convention set, read the live Foldkit code in `../projects/repos/foldkit/`. The `examples/` directory and the production apps (`packages/typing-game/`, `packages/website/`) are the highest-fidelity references for any specific pattern. The `foldkit-skills` plugin's `generate-program` and `audit-program` skills carry written snapshot guides if you want a structured walkthrough.

//   - Mint is abuse-bounded. The self-serve mint endpoint is per-IP-hash,

//
// PUBLIC-SAFE. Every table this touches carries account refs, IP-hash refs,
// model ids, bounded integer counters, and timestamps only — never prompts,
// completions, wallet/payment material, raw tokens, raw IPs, or secrets. The
// decorator never fails the customer's inference call: an accrual error logs a
// public-safe diagnostic and falls through to normal metering rather than
// breaking.
