// INERT until INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED is on AND the gateway is enabled.

import { Effect } from 'effect'

import { workerLogEntry } from '../observability'
import { currentIsoTimestamp } from '../runtime-primitives'
import { isPremiumModel } from './inference-premium-allowlist'
import { isKhalaModel } from './pricing'
import {
  type MeteringContext,
  type MeteringHook,
  type MeteringOutcome,
} from './metering-hook'

class FreeTierPersistenceError extends Error {
  readonly _tag = 'FreeTierPersistenceError'

  constructor(cause: unknown) {
    super(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : String(cause))
    this.name = 'FreeTierPersistenceError'
  }
}

const freeTierPersistenceError = (error: unknown) =>
  new FreeTierPersistenceError(error)

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Env flag (fail-closed, default OFF)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED_ENV_KEY =
  'INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED' as const

const ON_TOKENS = new Set(['1', 'on', 'true', 'yes'])

// Fail-closed flag read. Absent / non-string / any non-on value => disabled.
export const isFreeTierEnabled = (value: unknown): boolean =>
  typeof value === 'string' && ON_TOKENS.has(value.trim().toLowerCase())

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tunable quota constants (all free-tier thresholds in ONE place)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Per-key, per-UTC-day FREE request ceiling. The last allowed request lands the
// counter AT the ceiling; the next one falls through to the normal balance gate.
// !! TUNABLE: issue #6228.
export const FREE_TIER_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_DAY = 200 as const

// Per-key, per-UTC-day FREE served-token ceiling (prompt + completion). Bounds a
// single free key's draw on the shared own-infra pool regardless of request
// count. !! TUNABLE: issue #6228.
export const FREE_TIER_MAX_TOKENS_PER_DAY = 200_000 as const

// Per-IP-hash, per-UTC-day self-serve MINT ceiling so anonymous minting is
// bounded (no unbounded key minting). !! TUNABLE: issue #6228.
export const FREE_KEY_MAX_MINTS_PER_IP_PER_DAY = 25 as const

export type FreeTierQuota = Readonly<{
  maxRequestsPerDay: number
  maxTokensPerDay: number
}>

export const DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_QUOTA: FreeTierQuota = {
  maxRequestsPerDay: FREE_TIER_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_DAY,
  maxTokensPerDay: FREE_TIER_MAX_TOKENS_PER_DAY,
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// UTC day bucket
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// The UTC day bucket (YYYY-MM-DD) the free quota resets on. Derived from an ISO
// timestamp so tests can inject a fixed clock.
export const freeTierUsageDay = (nowIso: string): string =>
  nowIso.slice(0, 10)

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Free-lane eligibility (own-infra / non-premium public Khala ONLY)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const FREE_TIER_REASON_ELIGIBLE =
  'reason.inference_free_tier.eligible' as const
export const FREE_TIER_REASON_NOT_FREE_LANE =
  'reason.inference_free_tier.not_free_lane' as const
export const FREE_TIER_REASON_PREMIUM_DENIED =
  'reason.inference_free_tier.premium_never_free' as const

// Whether a requested model is on the FREE lane: it must be the single public
// Khala model AND not a premium class (defense in depth — Khala classifies as
// `open`, never premium, so the premium check can only ever protect against a
// future reclassification). Bounded id/class check, never an intent parser.
export const isFreeTierLaneModel = (model: string): boolean =>
  isKhalaModel(model) && !isPremiumModel(model)

export type FreeTierLaneDecision = Readonly<{
  freeLane: boolean
  premium: boolean
  reasonRef: string
}>

export const decideFreeTierLane = (model: string): FreeTierLaneDecision => {
  // GUARDRAIL: a premium model is never free, even if it were ever aliased to
  // Khala. Premium is deny-by-default here.
  if (isPremiumModel(model)) {
    return {
      freeLane: false,
      premium: true,
      reasonRef: FREE_TIER_REASON_PREMIUM_DENIED,
    }
  }
  if (isKhalaModel(model)) {
    return {
      freeLane: true,
      premium: false,
      reasonRef: FREE_TIER_REASON_ELIGIBLE,
    }
  }
  return {
    freeLane: false,
    premium: false,
    reasonRef: FREE_TIER_REASON_NOT_FREE_LANE,
  }
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Pure quota decision
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export type FreeTierUsage = Readonly<{
  requestsToday: number
  tokensToday: number
}>

export type FreeTierQuotaDecision = Readonly<{
  // True when THIS request fits under BOTH the daily request and token
  // ceilings. False => fall through to the normal balance gate (402).
  withinQuota: boolean
  maxRequestsPerDay: number
  maxTokensPerDay: number
  remainingRequests: number
  remainingTokens: number
  reasonRef: string
}>

export const FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_WITHIN =
  'reason.inference_free_tier.within_quota' as const
export const FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_REQUESTS_EXCEEDED =
  'reason.inference_free_tier.daily_requests_exceeded' as const
export const FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_TOKENS_EXCEEDED =
  'reason.inference_free_tier.daily_tokens_exceeded' as const

// Decide whether a free key may take ANOTHER request today. Pure. A request is
// admitted only when the already-used request count is UNDER the request ceiling
// AND the already-used token count is UNDER the token ceiling. A key that has
// already issued >= the request ceiling, or already drawn >= the token ceiling,
// is over quota (falls through to the balance gate). Pre-flight has no per-
// request token estimate, so the token check is against the already-used total.
export const decideFreeTierQuota = (
  input: Readonly<{
    usage: FreeTierUsage
    quota?: FreeTierQuota | undefined
  }>,
): FreeTierQuotaDecision => {
  const quota = input.quota ?? DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_QUOTA
  const requests = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(input.usage.requestsToday))
  const tokens = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(input.usage.tokensToday))
  const remainingRequests = Math.max(0, quota.maxRequestsPerDay - requests)
  const remainingTokens = Math.max(0, quota.maxTokensPerDay - tokens)

  if (requests >= quota.maxRequestsPerDay) {
    return {
      maxRequestsPerDay: quota.maxRequestsPerDay,
      maxTokensPerDay: quota.maxTokensPerDay,
      reasonRef: FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_REQUESTS_EXCEEDED,
      remainingRequests: 0,
      remainingTokens,
      withinQuota: false,
    }
  }
  if (tokens >= quota.maxTokensPerDay) {
    return {
      maxRequestsPerDay: quota.maxRequestsPerDay,
      maxTokensPerDay: quota.maxTokensPerDay,
      reasonRef: FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_TOKENS_EXCEEDED,
      remainingRequests,
      remainingTokens: 0,
      withinQuota: false,
    }
  }
  return {
    maxRequestsPerDay: quota.maxRequestsPerDay,
    maxTokensPerDay: quota.maxTokensPerDay,
    reasonRef: FREE_TIER_QUOTA_REASON_WITHIN,
    remainingRequests,
    remainingTokens,
    withinQuota: true,
  }
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Free-tier key store (mark + read)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_SCOPE = 'free_khala_daily' as const

// Plain-async read of whether an account is on the free tier. The route gate (a
// Promise-returning seam) uses this directly. Returns false on any read error
// (fail-closed).
export const readAccountFreeTier = async (
  db: D1Database,
  accountRef: string,
): Promise<boolean> => {
  try {
    const row = await db
      .prepare(
        `SELECT account_ref FROM inference_free_tier_keys WHERE account_ref = ? LIMIT 1`,
      )
      .bind(accountRef)
      .first<{ account_ref: string }>()
    return row !== null
  } catch {
    return false
  }
}

// Plain-async mark of an account as a free-tier key (idempotent upsert), for
// callers already in a Promise context (e.g. the mint route handler) so no
// Effect->Promise bridge runs there. Returns whether the write succeeded.
export const markAccountFreeTierAsync = async (
  db: D1Database,
  input: Readonly<{
    accountRef: string
    mintSource?: string | undefined
    note?: string | null | undefined
    scope?: string | undefined
    nowIso?: string | undefined
  }>,
): Promise<boolean> => {
  const nowIso = input.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp()
  try {
    await db
      .prepare(
        `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_keys
           (account_ref, scope, mint_source, note, created_at, updated_at)
         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
         ON CONFLICT(account_ref) DO UPDATE SET
           scope = excluded.scope,
           mint_source = excluded.mint_source,
           note = excluded.note,
           updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
      )
      .bind(
        input.accountRef,
        input.scope ?? DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_SCOPE,
        input.mintSource ?? 'self_serve_anonymous',
        input.note ?? null,
        nowIso,
        nowIso,
      )
      .run()
    return true
  } catch {
    return false
  }
}

// Plain-async record of one mint against an IP-hash for the day (idempotent
// increment), for callers already in a Promise context. Returns whether the
// write succeeded.
export const recordFreeKeyMintAsync = async (
  db: D1Database,
  input: Readonly<{
    ipHash: string
    mintDay: string
    nowIso?: string | undefined
  }>,
): Promise<boolean> => {
  const nowIso = input.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp()
  try {
    await db
      .prepare(
        `INSERT INTO inference_free_key_mints
           (ip_hash, mint_day, mint_count, created_at, updated_at)
         VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, ?)
         ON CONFLICT(ip_hash, mint_day) DO UPDATE SET
           mint_count = mint_count + 1,
           updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
      )
      .bind(input.ipHash, input.mintDay, nowIso, nowIso)
      .run()
    return true
  } catch {
    return false
  }
}

// Mark an account as a free-tier key (idempotent upsert). Called by the
// self-serve mint endpoint after registering the underlying agent credential.
export const markAccountFreeTier = (
  db: D1Database,
  input: Readonly<{
    accountRef: string
    mintSource?: string | undefined
    note?: string | null | undefined
    scope?: string | undefined
    nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
  }>,
): Effect.Effect<boolean> =>
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    const nowIso = (input.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp)()
    return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
      catch: freeTierPersistenceError,
      try: async () => {
        await db
          .prepare(
            `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_keys
               (account_ref, scope, mint_source, note, created_at, updated_at)
             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
             ON CONFLICT(account_ref) DO UPDATE SET
               scope = excluded.scope,
               mint_source = excluded.mint_source,
               note = excluded.note,
               updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
          )
          .bind(
            input.accountRef,
            input.scope ?? DEFAULT_FREE_TIER_SCOPE,
            input.mintSource ?? 'self_serve_anonymous',
            input.note ?? null,
            nowIso,
            nowIso,
          )
          .run()
        return true
      },
    }).pipe(Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(false)))
  })

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Free-tier daily usage read + idempotent accrual
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

type FreeTierUsageRow = Readonly<{
  free_request_count: number
  free_total_tokens: number
}>

// Read the account's current free usage for the given UTC day. A missing row
// means $0/0 used today. Read-only and bounded.
export const readFreeTierUsage = async (
  db: D1Database,
  accountRef: string,
  usageDay: string,
): Promise<FreeTierUsage> => {
  const row = await db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT free_request_count, free_total_tokens
         FROM inference_free_tier_usage
        WHERE account_ref = ? AND usage_day = ?
        LIMIT 1`,
    )
    .bind(accountRef, usageDay)
    .first<FreeTierUsageRow>()
  return {
    requestsToday:
      typeof row?.free_request_count === 'number' ? row.free_request_count : 0,
    tokensToday:
      typeof row?.free_total_tokens === 'number' ? row.free_total_tokens : 0,
  }
}

// Accrue one free request (+ its served tokens) against the account's daily
// tally, idempotently. Writes the per-request event row (UNIQUE request_id) and
// increments the daily tally in ONE D1 batch. On a duplicate request id the
// UNIQUE constraint aborts the batch and we treat it as already-accrued (no
// double-count). Returns whether a NEW accrual was recorded.
const accrueFreeTierUsage = async (
  db: D1Database,
  input: Readonly<{
    accountRef: string
    usageDay: string
    requestId: string
    servedModel: string
    totalTokens: number
    nowIso: string
  }>,
): Promise<boolean> => {
  try {
    await db.batch([
      db
        .prepare(
          `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_usage_events
             (request_id, account_ref, usage_day, served_model, total_tokens, created_at)
           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
        )
        .bind(
          input.requestId,
          input.accountRef,
          input.usageDay,
          input.servedModel,
          input.totalTokens,
          input.nowIso,
        ),
      db
        .prepare(
          `INSERT INTO inference_free_tier_usage
             (account_ref, usage_day, free_request_count, free_total_tokens, created_at, updated_at)
           VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, ?, ?)
           ON CONFLICT(account_ref, usage_day) DO UPDATE SET
             free_request_count = free_request_count + 1,
             free_total_tokens = free_total_tokens + excluded.free_total_tokens,
             updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
        )
        .bind(
          input.accountRef,
          input.usageDay,
          input.totalTokens,
          input.nowIso,
          input.nowIso,
        ),
    ])
    return true
  } catch {
    // Duplicate request id => already accrued. Idempotent no-op.
    return false
  }
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Mint abuse guard (per-IP-hash, per-day)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export type FreeKeyMintGateDecision = Readonly<{
  allowed: boolean
  mintsToday: number
  maxMintsPerDay: number
  reasonRef: string
}>

export const FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_ALLOWED =
  'reason.inference_free_key_mint.allowed' as const
export const FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_RATE_LIMITED =
  'reason.inference_free_key_mint.daily_ip_limit_exceeded' as const

// Pure mint-rate decision: a new mint is allowed only when the IP's already-used
// mint count today is UNDER the daily ceiling.
export const decideFreeKeyMint = (
  input: Readonly<{
    mintsToday: number
    maxMintsPerDay?: number
  }>,
): FreeKeyMintGateDecision => {
  const max = Math.max(
    1,
    Math.trunc(input.maxMintsPerDay ?? FREE_KEY_MAX_MINTS_PER_IP_PER_DAY),
  )
  const mints = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(input.mintsToday))
  if (mints >= max) {
    return {
      allowed: false,
      maxMintsPerDay: max,
      mintsToday: mints,
      reasonRef: FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_RATE_LIMITED,
    }
  }
  return {
    allowed: true,
    maxMintsPerDay: max,
    mintsToday: mints,
    reasonRef: FREE_KEY_MINT_REASON_ALLOWED,
  }
}

// Read how many free keys an IP-hash has minted today. Returns 0 on any read
// error so a transient DB blip cannot block minting forever (the per-mint write
// below is still bounded; this read is the pre-flight signal).
export const readFreeKeyMintsToday = async (
  db: D1Database,
  ipHash: string,
  mintDay: string,
): Promise<number> => {
  try {
    const row = await db
      .prepare(
        `SELECT mint_count FROM inference_free_key_mints
          WHERE ip_hash = ? AND mint_day = ? LIMIT 1`,
      )
      .bind(ipHash, mintDay)
      .first<{ mint_count: number }>()
    return typeof row?.mint_count === 'number' ? row.mint_count : 0
  } catch {
    return 0
  }
}

// Record one mint against an IP-hash for the day (idempotent increment). Returns
// whether the write succeeded; a failure is logged by the caller (the mint still
// proceeds — the pre-flight read is the primary bound).
export const recordFreeKeyMint = (
  db: D1Database,
  input: Readonly<{
    ipHash: string
    mintDay: string
    nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
  }>,
): Effect.Effect<boolean> =>
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    const nowIso = (input.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp)()
    return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
      catch: freeTierPersistenceError,
      try: async () => {
        await db
          .prepare(
            `INSERT INTO inference_free_key_mints
               (ip_hash, mint_day, mint_count, created_at, updated_at)
             VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, ?)
             ON CONFLICT(ip_hash, mint_day) DO UPDATE SET
               mint_count = mint_count + 1,
               updated_at = excluded.updated_at`,
          )
          .bind(input.ipHash, input.mintDay, nowIso, nowIso)
          .run()
        return true
      },
    }).pipe(Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(false)))
  })

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Balance-gate seam (the free-tier bypass)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export type FreeTierGateDecision = Readonly<{
  // True when the request may BYPASS the balance gate: a free-tier key, on the
  // free Khala lane, within the daily quota. False => the gate is unchanged (the
  // request must clear the normal balance gate; paid-Khala behavior intact).
  free: boolean
  reasonRef: string
}>

export type FreeTierGateDeps = Readonly<{
  db: D1Database
  quota?: FreeTierQuota
  nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
}>

// A route-level balance-gate bypass: given an account ref + requested model,
// returns whether the request may bypass the balance gate as a free-tier call.
// PREMIUM / non-Khala models short-circuit to NOT-free WITHOUT a quota read.
// Wired into the chat-completions `checkFreeTier` seam (open/no-op when unwired
// or flag-off). Fail-closed: any read error => not free (the 402 stands).
export type FreeTierGate = (
  accountRef: string,
  model: string,
) => Promise<FreeTierGateDecision>

export const makeFreeTierGate = (deps: FreeTierGateDeps): FreeTierGate => {
  const nowIso = deps.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp
  return async (accountRef: string, model: string) => {
    const lane = decideFreeTierLane(model)
    if (!lane.freeLane) {
      return { free: false, reasonRef: lane.reasonRef }
    }
    try {
      const isFree = await readAccountFreeTier(deps.db, accountRef)
      if (!isFree) {
        return {
          free: false,
          reasonRef: 'reason.inference_free_tier.account_not_free_tier',
        }
      }
      const usageDay = freeTierUsageDay(nowIso())
      const usage = await readFreeTierUsage(deps.db, accountRef, usageDay)
      const quota = decideFreeTierQuota({ quota: deps.quota, usage })
      return {
        free: quota.withinQuota,
        reasonRef: quota.reasonRef,
      }
    } catch {
      // Read error: do NOT bypass the balance gate.
      return {
        free: false,
        reasonRef: 'reason.inference_free_tier.read_error',
      }
    }
  }
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Free-tier metering wrapper (zero-debit, receipt-first, no referral)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Public-safe free-tier receipt ref. Resolvable without exposing any account id,
// amount, destination, or payment material. The `free_tier` infix marks the
// honest zero-debit free-mode accounting state.
export const freeTierReceiptRef = (requestId: string): string =>
  `receipt.inference.free_tier.${requestId}`

const freeTierOutcome = (requestId: string): MeteringOutcome => ({
  metered: false,
  receiptRef: freeTierReceiptRef(requestId),
})

export type FreeTierMeteringDeps = Readonly<{
  db: D1Database
  quota?: FreeTierQuota
  nowIso?: (() => string) | undefined
}>

/**
 * Wrap a metering hook so a FREE-TIER key's request for the free Khala lane,
 * WITHIN the daily quota, is recorded as a zero-debit free receipt (receipt-
 * first, NO credit decrement, NO referral) AND its usage is accrued against the
 * daily quota (idempotent per request). Otherwise it falls through to the inner
 * hook unchanged.
 *
 * GUARDRAILS:
 *   - A PREMIUM served model ALWAYS falls through to the inner hook (the normal
 *     ledger debit), even for a free-tier key — a free key never gets a premium
 *     model free.
 *   - A non-free-tier account, a non-Khala model, an OVER-quota request, or any
 *     DB error falls through to the inner hook (charge), so free is genuinely
 *     free only within the quota and never grants usage we could not account
 *     for. The customer's inference call is never failed by the bookkeeping.
 *
 * Wired alongside `withFreeAllowance` / `withOperatorCredit`. It runs AFTER
 * dispatch; the route's `checkFreeTier` balance-gate seam is what lets the
 * zero-balance free-tier request reach dispatch in the first place. The two
 * agree because they share the SAME store + quota + UTC-day bucket.
 */
export const withFreeTierKhala = (
  inner: MeteringHook,
  deps: FreeTierMeteringDeps,
): MeteringHook => {
  const nowIso = deps.nowIso ?? currentIsoTimestamp
  return (context: MeteringContext) =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      // Only the free Khala lane is ever free here; premium / non-Khala meter.
      // Key on the SERVED model (the lane that actually incurred cost), matching
      // the ledger hook + the other decorators.
      if (!isFreeTierLaneModel(context.servedModel)) {
        return yield* inner(context)
      }

      const gated = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
        catch: freeTierPersistenceError,
        try: async () => {
          const isFree = await readAccountFreeTier(deps.db, context.accountRef)
          if (!isFree) {
            return { accrued: false, free: false as const }
          }
          const now = nowIso()
          const usageDay = freeTierUsageDay(now)
          const usage = await readFreeTierUsage(
            deps.db,
            context.accountRef,
            usageDay,
          )
          const quota = decideFreeTierQuota({ quota: deps.quota, usage })
          if (!quota.withinQuota) {
            return { accrued: false, free: false as const }
          }
          const accrued = await accrueFreeTierUsage(deps.db, {
            accountRef: context.accountRef,
            nowIso: now,
            requestId: context.requestId,
            servedModel: context.servedModel,
            totalTokens: Math.max(0, Math.trunc(context.usage.totalTokens)),
            usageDay,
          })
          return { accrued, free: true as const }
        },
      }).pipe(
        Effect.catch(error =>
          Effect.gen(function* () {
            // Public-safe diagnostic only; never break the inference response.
            // On error meter normally (charge) rather than grant a free request
            // we could not account for.
            yield* Effect.logInfo(
              workerLogEntry('inference.free_tier.error', {
                accountRef: context.accountRef,
                adapterId: context.adapterId,
                reason: error.message,
                requestId: context.requestId,
                servedModel: context.servedModel,
              }),
            )
            return { accrued: false, free: false as const }
          }),
        ),
      )

      if (!gated.free) {
        // Not free-tier, over quota, or error: meter normally (decrement).
        return yield* inner(context)
      }

      // Free-tier within quota: record the zero-debit free receipt + accrual and
      // return WITHOUT calling the inner hook (no decrement, no referral). Log a
      // public-safe diagnostic (refs + token counts only).
      yield* Effect.logInfo(
        workerLogEntry('inference.free_tier.granted', {
          accountRef: context.accountRef,
          accrued: gated.accrued,
          adapterId: context.adapterId,
          requestId: context.requestId,
          servedModel: context.servedModel,
          totalTokens: context.usage.totalTokens,
        }),
      )
      return freeTierOutcome(context.requestId) satisfies MeteringOutcome
    })
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Self-serve mint (pure helpers; the Worker route wires registration + IP hash)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// The public-safe request body for `POST /api/keys/free`. Anonymous by default;
// an optional public-safe label (e.g. an app name) helps the owner attribute
// keys. NO email is required (the simplest safe option — abuse is bounded by the
// per-IP mint rate limit + the per-key quota), and an optional email, if sent,
// is only used as the registration display label, never verified or stored as
// PII beyond the agent record.
export type FreeKeyMintRequest = Readonly<{
  label?: string | null | undefined
}>

// Bound the display label so the minted credential name is sane.
export const FREE_KEY_LABEL_MAX_LENGTH = 80 as const

export const sanitizeFreeKeyLabel = (
  label: string | null | undefined,
): string => {
  const trimmed = (label ?? '').trim()
  if (trimmed === '') {
    return 'Free API key'
  }
  return trimmed.slice(0, FREE_KEY_LABEL_MAX_LENGTH)

203: // SUPPLY-SELECTION SEAM (#5482) -------------------------------------------
204: // Resolves a requested model to an ORDERED list of candidate adapter ids
205: // (cheapest viable lane first, then overflow fallbacks). When supplied, the
206: // route dispatches across this plan with bounded-backoff overflow on retryable
207: // provider failures (429 / 503 / 5xx / transport) — see `dispatchWithOverflow`
208: // in model-router.ts. The Worker wires this to `selectAdapterPlan`; when it is
209: // absent the route falls back to the single-id `router` seam (the #5476 path).
210: export type ModelLanePlanner = (model: string) => ReadonlyArray<string>
211: 
212: // Parse the INFERENCE_GATEWAY_ENABLED flag value. Default OFF: anything other
213: // than an explicit truthy token leaves the gateway inert.
214: export const isInferenceGatewayEnabled = (
215:   value: string | undefined,
216: ): boolean => {
217:   if (value === undefined) {
218:     return false
219:   }
220:   return ['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(value.trim().toLowerCase())
221: }
222: 
223: // Parse the INFERENCE_DURABLE_STREAM_ENABLED flag (durable-stream Rank-1, #6058).
224: // Default OFF: the streaming pass-through degrades to today's behaviour (no
225: // persistence/resume) unless this is an explicit truthy token AND a durable
226: // store factory is wired. Fail-safe + inert by default.
227: export const isInferenceDurableStreamEnabled = (
228:   value: string | undefined,
229: ): boolean => {
230:   if (value === undefined) {
231:     return false
232:   }
233:   return ['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(value.trim().toLowerCase())
234: }
235: 
236: // COMPONENT-CHANNEL CONFIG (issue #6127). `enabled` is the gateway-level flag
237: // (default off). When on, the channel is STILL only activated per-request via an
238: // explicit opt-in for a Khala model. `repairReask` (optional) wires the ONE
239: // bounded repair turn over a single non-streaming Khala call; absent => an
240: // invalid card is dropped without a repair attempt (still never shipped).
241: export type ComponentChannelConfig = Readonly<{
242:   enabled: boolean
243:   repairReask?: ComponentRepairReask | undefined
244: }>
245: 
246: // Resolve whether the typed component channel is active FOR THIS REQUEST. Three
247: // AND-gated conditions, all required, so the default `/v1` shape never changes:
248: //   1. the gateway-level flag is on (`config.enabled`)
249: //   2. the request explicitly opts in (header `x-oa-component-channel: on`, or a
250: //      truthy `oa_component_channel` body field) — a deterministic parse of an
251: //      explicit caller-supplied switch, never an intent inference
252: //   3. the model is a Khala model (the channel is a Khala capability)
253: export const resolveComponentChannelActive = (
254:   input: Readonly<{
255:     config: ComponentChannelConfig | undefined
256:     request: Request
257:     rawBody: Record<string, unknown>
258:     requestedModel: string
259:     autopilotConcierge?: boolean | undefined
260:   }>,
261: ): boolean => {
262:   if (input.config?.enabled !== true) {
263:     return false
264:   }
265:   if (!isKhalaModel(input.requestedModel)) {
266:     return false
267:   }
268:   if (input.autopilotConcierge === true) {
269:     return true
270:   }
271:   const header = input.request.headers
272:     .get('x-oa-component-channel')
273:     ?.trim()
274:     .toLowerCase()
275:   const headerOptIn =
276:     header !== undefined && ['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(header)
277:   const bodyField = input.rawBody['oa_component_channel']
278:   const bodyOptIn =
279:     bodyField === true ||
280:     (typeof bodyField === 'string' &&
281:       ['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(bodyField.trim().toLowerCase()))
282:   return headerOptIn || bodyOptIn
283: }

285: export type ChatCompletionsDeps = Readonly<{
286:   // Whether the gateway is enabled. The Worker passes
287:   // env.INFERENCE_GATEWAY_ENABLED parsed as a flag; default OFF.
288:   enabled: boolean
289:   authenticate: InferenceAuth
290:   readAvailableMsat: InferenceBalanceReader
291:   registry: InferenceProviderRegistry
292:   // Single-id router seam. Defaults to the stub router (always selects the
293:   // stub/echo adapter). Used to gate `model_unavailable` and to dispatch when
294:   // no multi-lane `lanePlan` is supplied (#5476 path).
295:   router?: ModelRouter
296:   // Ordered multi-lane plan for cheapest-viable selection + overflow (#5482).
297:   // When present, the route dispatches across the plan with bounded-backoff
298:   // overflow on retryable failures. The Worker wires this to `selectAdapterPlan`.
299:   lanePlan?: ModelLanePlanner
300:   // PROVIDER SERVING POLICY (blocker.product_promises.public_paid_model_gateway_missing
301:   // on api.hosted_gemini.v1). The SAME presence-derived lane arming the public
302:   // catalog (`/v1/models`) and the pre-purchase quote (`/v1/quote`) are gated on.
303:   // When supplied, a request for a KNOWN model whose supply lane is NOT armed is
304:   // rejected with a clean `model_unavailable` (400) BEFORE any account-state gate
305:   // or provider dispatch — so the gateway serves exactly what it advertises and
306:   // quotes, instead of accepting the request and failing deep at dispatch with a
307:   // generic `provider_error` (502). An UNKNOWN model id is not gated (the
308:   // estimator prices it at the conservative fallback rate, consistent with
309:   // `/v1/quote`). Omitting it preserves the prior serve-everything behaviour.
310:   // Presence-only; no secret value is read here.
311:   laneArming?: SupplyLaneArming
312:   // Routing overflow knobs (backoff + injected sleep) forwarded to
313:   // `dispatchWithOverflow`. Tests inject `sleep: () => Effect.void` so overflow
314:   // never waits. Ignored unless `lanePlan` is supplied.
315:   dispatch?: Omit<DispatchDeps, 'registry' | 'plan'>
316:   // Defaults to the no-op/log metering stub. The Worker supplies the live
317:   // ledger hook (`makeLedgerMeteringHook`, #5477) when the gateway is enabled.
318:   meteringHook?: MeteringHook
319:   // SERVED-TOKENS RECORDER (issue #6227). Records one canonical
320:   // `token_usage_events` row per SERVED completion so the public "Khala Tokens
321:   // Served" counter (GET /api/public/khala-tokens-served) reflects ALL Khala
322:   // traffic. Invoked AFTER the metering hook on every completion path
323:   // (non-streaming, buffered stream, true pass-through stream) and only when the
324:   // request produced real provider usage — so a failed / 4xx / refused call is
325:   // never recorded. It runs INDEPENDENTLY of which metering wrapper handled the
326:   // request, so a FREE-TIER completion (whose credit-ledger hook is
327:   // short-circuited in `withFreeTierKhala`) STILL records its served tokens (the
328:   // tokens were served and must count, even though no credit was debited). The
329:   // row is idempotent per request id (one served completion = one row); a
330:   // retry/replay is a no-op insert. Default undefined => no-op (the inert/test
331:   // path is byte-for-byte unchanged). The recorder never throws and never fails
332:   // the customer's already-delivered completion.
333:   recordTokensServed?: ServedTokensRecorder | undefined
334:   // Resolves the account's funding kind (card | bitcoin) for the metering hook.
335:   // Defaults to card (no Bitcoin discount).
336:   resolveFundingKind?: InferenceFundingResolver
337:   // Minimum available balance (msat) required to accept a request. Until #5477
338:   // prices per-model, any positive balance clears the gate; an account with
339:   // zero/negative available balance is rejected.
340:   minimumAvailableMsat?: number
341:   // FREE-ALLOWANCE PRE-FLIGHT (EPIC #5474 §1). Read-only mirror of the gate
342:   // inside `withFreeAllowance`. The balance gate calls this BEFORE rejecting a
343:   // zero/insufficient-balance account: if the (account, model) is free-eligible
344:   // and the resolving owner still has remaining free allowance, the request is
345:   // allowed through (the metering hook then eats the cost and accrues it), so a
346:   // genuinely-free request is never falsely 402'd. Default undefined => the gate
347:   // is unchanged (a zero-balance account is rejected). Resolution errors return
348:   // not-eligible, so the balance gate stands. Wired by the Worker to
349:   // `checkFreeAllowancePreflight` against the SAME owner-identity resolver the
350:   // metering hook uses, keeping the bypass and the accrual consistent.
351:   checkFreeAllowance?: (
352:     accountRef: string,
353:     model: string,
354:   ) => Promise<{ readonly eligible: boolean }>
355:   // OWNER BALANCE-GATE EXEMPTION (issue #6180). Read-only seam the balance gate
356:   // calls BEFORE the 402: if the (account, model) resolves to an EXEMPT verified
357:   // owner AND the model is non-premium / own-infra, the zero-balance request is
358:   // allowed through (the `withOperatorCredit` metering wrapper then records it as
359:   // `operator_credit` — zero credit debit + receipt, no referral). Lets
360:   // approved/internal keys test our OWN lanes (e.g. `openagents/khala` on the
361:   // hourly Hydralisk box) WITHOUT funding while Khala stays paid for the public.
362:   // HARD GUARDRAIL: a PREMIUM model (`claude`/`unknown`) is NEVER exempt — it
363:   // still hits the normal balance + premium gates. Default undefined => the gate
364:   // is unchanged (a zero-balance account is rejected). Resolution errors return
365:   // not-exempt, so the balance gate stands. Wired by the Worker to
366:   // `makeOperatorExemptionGate` ONLY when INFERENCE_OPERATOR_EXEMPTION_ENABLED is
367:   // on, against the SAME owner-identity resolver the metering wrapper uses so the
368:   // bypass and the zero-debit record agree.
369:   checkOperatorExemption?: (
370:     accountRef: string,
371:     model: string,
372:   ) => Promise<{ readonly exempt: boolean }>
373:   // KHALA FREE TIER bypass (issue #6228). Read-only seam the balance gate calls
374:   // BEFORE the 402: if the (account, model) is a FREE-TIER key on the free Khala
375:   // lane (own-infra / non-premium) AND still within its per-key daily quota, the
376:   // zero-balance request is allowed through (the `withFreeTierKhala` metering
377:   // wrapper then records it as a zero-debit free receipt + accrues the quota).
378:   // This is the THIRD balance-gate bypass alongside `checkFreeAllowance` (owner
379:   // Sybil pool on Gemini) and `checkOperatorExemption` (owner operator credit);
380:   // it is the self-serve one — anyone who mints a free key gets free Khala within
381:   // quota. A PREMIUM model is NEVER free; over-quota / non-free-tier / non-Khala
382:   // requests fall through to the normal 402. Default undefined => the gate is
383:   // unchanged. Wired by the Worker to `makeFreeTierGate` ONLY when
384:   // INFERENCE_FREE_TIER_ENABLED is on, against the SAME store + quota the metering
385:   // wrapper uses so the bypass and the zero-debit accrual agree.
386:   checkFreeTier?: (
387:     accountRef: string,
388:     model: string,
389:   ) => Promise<{ readonly free: boolean }>
390:   // ABUSE-CONTROL SEAMS (#5486). Both default to undefined => the gate is OPEN
391:   // (no-op), so the inert/flag-off path and the unconfigured-account path are
392:   // byte-for-byte unchanged. The decisions are computed by the pure deciders in
393:   // inference-abuse-controls.ts (`decideFairShare` / `decideSpendCap`); the
394:   // Worker wires these to per-account window counters (D1/KV) so one customer
395:   // cannot starve the shared Vertex quota (fair-share) or drain its whole balance
396:   // via a compromised key (spend cap), both DISTINCT from the raw balance gate.
397:   //
398:   // PER-CUSTOMER RATE / FAIR-SHARE GATE. Returns the fair-share decision for the
399:   // account in the current window. When `allowed` is false the route rejects with
400:   // the decision's statusCode (429) and sets RateLimit-* headers from the
401:   // bounded counters. Checked AFTER auth (so it is keyed to the account) and
402:   // BEFORE provider dispatch (so a starve-attempt never reaches a provider).
403:   checkFairShare?: (accountRef: string) => Promise<FairShareDecision>
404:   // PER-ACCOUNT SPEND-CAP GATE. Returns the spend-cap decision for the account in
405:   // the current window. When `allowed` is false the route rejects with the
406:   // decision's statusCode (402, distinct from the balance gate's
407:   // insufficient_credits). Checked after the balance gate; pre-flight it bounds
408:   // the already-spent window total (no per-request estimate exists yet).
409:   checkSpendCap?: (accountRef: string) => Promise<SpendCapDecision>
410:   // PREMIUM-MODEL OWNER-GRANT GATE (free-tier enablement §2). Returns the
411:   // premium-access decision for the (account, model) pair. Premium models
412:   // (Claude / GPT / partner-passthrough) require the account's resolved OWNER
413:   // identity to be on the owner-controlled allowlist; non-allowlisted premium
414:   // requests are DENIED (403) with a clear, actionable message. Non-premium
415:   // models (Gemini free default, Fireworks open) always pass. Default undefined
416:   // => the gate is OPEN (no-op), so the inert/flag-off path is unchanged.
417:   // Checked AFTER auth (so it is keyed to the resolved owner) and BEFORE
418:   // provider dispatch (so a denied premium request never reaches a provider).
419:   checkPremiumAccess?: (
420:     accountRef: string,
421:     model: string,
422:   ) => Promise<PremiumAccessDecision>
423:   // Deterministic id/timestamp injection for tests. `nowEpochSeconds` defaults
424:   // to the runtime-primitives clock; `newId` to a runtime-primitives random id.
425:   nowEpochSeconds?: () => number
426:   newId?: () => string
427:   // DURABLE-STREAM RANK-1 SEAM (#6058, EPIC #6056). When `durableStreamEnabled`
428:   // is true AND `durableStream` resolves a per-request `StreamStore`, a streaming
429:   // completion is teed into a durable offset log keyed by the response id, so a
430:   // client disconnect mid-generation can be resumed by offset (the durable read
431:   // route). DEFAULT OFF + UNWIRED: with the flag off or no store factory, the
432:   // streaming path is byte-for-byte today's pass-through (no persistence/resume).
433:   // Metering still settles EXACTLY ONCE on the real upstream EOF and NEVER on a
434:   // resume/replay read (the resume route has no metering hook).
435:   durableStreamEnabled?: boolean | undefined
436:   durableStream?: DurableInferenceStreamStore | undefined
437:   // PRODUCTION DURABLE SUBSTRATE (#6058). When present AND `durableStreamEnabled`
438:   // is true, the streaming completion is teed into the per-request Durable Object
439:   // (`DurableInferenceStreamObject`, keyed `getByName(responseId)`) over the
440:   // `/v1/stream/{id}` HTTP contract — the DO is the single authoritative durable
441:   // log a LATER GET resume reads. This is the live wiring; `durableStream` (the
442:   // synchronous `StreamStore` factory) is the in-memory test/contract substrate.
443:   // When both are present the DO namespace wins. Absent (e.g. the binding is
444:   // unbound on an env) => fail-safe non-durable pass-through.
445:   durableStreamNamespace?: DurableStreamNamespace | undefined
446:   // TYPED COMPONENT CHANNEL SEAM (EPIC #6123, issue #6127). The ADDITIVE,
447:   // OPT-IN `oa.component` SSE channel: a Khala turn may stream prose PLUS one or
448:   // more validated, versioned `oa.component` cards from the CLOSED v1 catalog
449:   // (khala-component-channel.ts). DEFAULT OFF: with `componentChannel` absent or
450:   // `enabled: false`, `/v1/chat/completions` is byte-for-byte today's text-only
451:   // stream — standard OpenAI clients are unaffected. When `enabled` is true the
452:   // channel is STILL only activated for a request that explicitly opts in (the
453:   // `x-oa-component-channel: on` header or an `oa_component_channel: true` body
454:   // field) AND targets a Khala model, so the default response shape never
455:   // changes. On activation, the gateway:
456:   //   - injects the closed-catalog system prompt (stable prefix),
457:   //   - assembles the completion, splits prose vs fenced `oa-component` blocks,
458:   //   - validates each card's props against the closed catalog with Effect
459:   //     Schema (ONE bounded repair turn, then drop — never ship malformed),
460:   //   - honors the SAME provider-identity redaction backstop as the prose path,
461:   //   - re-emits prose as `{content}` deltas and each card as one atomic
462:   //     `event: oa.component` frame.
463:   componentChannel?: ComponentChannelConfig | undefined
464:   // CACHE-AWARE ROUTING SEAM (book P0-2 deliverable 6 / #6084). When wired, a
465:   // same-session/codebase/account follow-up is routed to the cache-WARM lane
466:   // first (the lane that previously served this affinity hash), subject to the
467:   // lane still being viable + healthy + privacy/region-allowed. All three are
468:   // INJECTED capabilities so routing stays pure + typed (no ad-hoc string
469:   // matching). DEFAULT UNWIRED: with `cacheWarmthOracle` absent the lane plan is
470:   // unchanged (cheapest-viable order), so existing behavior is unaffected.
471:   //   - `cacheWarmthOracle` : affinity HASH → the lane that last served it.
472:   //   - `laneHealthOracle`  : lane → health posture (warm hint ignored if sick).
473:   //   - `cachePinPolicy`    : lane → may this account pin here (privacy/region).
474:   cacheWarmthOracle?: CacheWarmthOracle | undefined
475:   laneHealthOracle?: LaneHealthOracle | undefined
476:   cachePinPolicy?: CachePinPolicy | undefined
477:   // Deterministic clock (epoch ms) for the durable log's TTL/offset bookkeeping.
478:   // Defaults to a fixed value in tests; the Worker threads `currentEpochMillis`.
479:   nowEpochMillis?: (() => number) | undefined
480:   // ACCEPTANCE-DISPATCH SEAM (EPIC #6017). When a khala-code completion produces an
481:   // EXECUTABLE artifact (it passes the cheap pre-screen), the gateway enqueues an
482:   // out-of-Worker verification job: a node-side runner (Pylon / sandbox / Cloud Run)
483:   // runs the real headless acceptance suite and posts the verdict back to the
484:   // authenticated callback, which backfills the receipt (`unverified` ->
485:   // `test_passed`/`failed`). DEFAULT OFF + UNWIRED: with `acceptanceDispatch.enabled`
486:   // false or no `queue`/`artifactStore` wired, NOTHING is enqueued and the receipt
487:   // stays the honest `unverified`. The Worker wires this once a runner host is
488:   // deployed and the flag (KHALA_ACCEPTANCE_DISPATCH_ENABLED) is on. Chromium NEVER
489:   // runs in the Worker; this only enqueues a job (pure producer).
490:   acceptanceDispatch?: Readonly<{
491:     enabled: boolean
492:     queue: AcceptanceJobQueue | undefined
493:     // Persist the artifact bytes and return a dereferenceable ref the runner
494:     // resolves (an R2 key). Absent => no job is enqueued (the runner would have no
495:     // artifact to fetch). Receives the request id + the runnable HTML.
496:     storeArtifact?: (
497:       input: Readonly<{ requestId: string; html: string }>,
498:     ) => Promise<string>
499:   }>
500:   // CROSS-APP TRACE EMISSION (openagents #6214, epic #6206). When wired AND
501:   // enabled AND the request opts in, a COMPLETED non-streaming Khala chat session
502:   // is mapped to a public-safe ATIF trajectory and persisted via the shared trace
503:   // store, so the session becomes a shareable `/trace/{uuid}`. DEFAULT OFF +
504:   // UNWIRED: with `traceEmit` absent or `enabled: false`, NOTHING is emitted and
505:   // `/v1/chat/completions` is byte-for-byte today's behaviour. The emit is
506:   // fire-and-forget over the already-delivered completion (it never blocks, fails,
507:   // or alters the response). The emitted model id is the gateway projection
508:   // (`openagents/khala`), never a raw backend; the emitter reuses the SAME ingest
509:   // validation + public-safety tripwire (never bypassed). Autopilot/Pylon
510:   // emission are explicit follow-ups (#6214), not built on this path.
511:   traceEmit?: Readonly<{
512:     enabled: boolean
513:     // Persist a completed Khala chat session as an ATIF trace. The Worker wires
514:     // this to `emitKhalaChatTrace` against the shared trace store + the resolved
515:     // owner. Absent => no-op. Returns void; the call site logs nothing sensitive.
516:     emit: (
517:       input: Readonly<{
518:         accountRef: string
519:         requestedModel: string
520:         requestMessages: ReadonlyArray<InferenceMessage>
521:         result: InferenceResult
522:         responseId: string
523:         optedIn: boolean
524:       }>,
525:     ) => Promise<void>
526:   }>
527: }>

529: // REQUEST SCHEMA ----------------------------------------------------------
530: type ChatCompletionsRequestBody = Readonly<{
531:   // `model` is OPTIONAL: an unspecified model defaults to DEFAULT_CHAT_MODEL
532:   // (`openagents/khala`) in `resolveRequestedModel` below.
533:   model?: string | undefined
534:   messages: ReadonlyArray<InferenceMessage>
535:   stream?: boolean | undefined
536: }>
537: 
538: // Resolve the effective requested model: a present, non-blank `model` normalized
539: // through the single Khala alias rule, otherwise the Khala default. Centralized
540: // so the default applies uniformly to routing, premium gating, response echo,
541: // and metering.
542: export const resolveRequestedModel = (model: string | undefined): string => {
543:   const trimmed = model?.trim()
544:   return trimmed === undefined || trimmed === ''
545:     ? DEFAULT_CHAT_MODEL
546:     : normalizeKhalaModelId(trimmed)
547: }
548: 
549: const decodeOpenAiContent = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
550:   if (typeof value === 'string') {
551:     return value
552:   }
553:   if (value === null) {
554:     return ''
555:   }
556:   if (!Array.isArray(value)) {
557:     return undefined
558:   }
559:   const parts: Array<string> = []
560:   for (const item of value) {
561:     const record = recordFromUnknown(item)
562:     if (record?.['type'] !== 'text' || typeof record['text'] !== 'string') {
563:       return undefined
564:     }
565:     parts.push(record['text'])
566:   }
567:   return parts.join('\n\n')
568: }
569: 
570: const nonEmptyString = (value: unknown): string | undefined =>
571:   typeof value === 'string' && value !== '' ? value : undefined
572: 
573: const decodeMessage = (value: unknown): InferenceMessage | undefined => {
574:   const record = recordFromUnknown(value)
575:   const role = nonEmptyString(record?.['role'])
576:   if (record === undefined || role === undefined) {
577:     return undefined
578:   }
579:   const rawToolCalls = record['tool_calls']
580:   const toolCalls =
581:     rawToolCalls === undefined
582:       ? undefined
583:       : inferenceToolCallsFromUnknown(rawToolCalls)
584:   if (rawToolCalls !== undefined && toolCalls === undefined) {
585:     return undefined
586:   }
587:   const content =
588:     record['content'] === undefined && toolCalls !== undefined
589:       ? ''
590:       : decodeOpenAiContent(record['content'])
591:   if (content === undefined) {
592:     return undefined
593:   }
594:   const toolCallId = nonEmptyString(record['tool_call_id'])
595:   const name = nonEmptyString(record['name'])
596:   return {
597:     content,
598:     ...(name === undefined ? {} : { name }),
599:     role,
600:     ...(toolCallId === undefined ? {} : { toolCallId }),
601:     ...(toolCalls === undefined || toolCalls.length === 0
602:       ? {}
603:       : { toolCalls }),
604:   }
605: }
606: 
607: const decodeBody = (
608:   value: unknown,
609: ): ChatCompletionsRequestBody | undefined => {
610:   const record = recordFromUnknown(value)
611:   if (record === undefined || !Array.isArray(record['messages'])) {
612:     return undefined
613:   }
614:   const model = record['model']
615:   if (model !== undefined && typeof model !== 'string') {
616:     return undefined
617:   }
618:   const stream = record['stream']
619:   if (stream !== undefined && typeof stream !== 'boolean') {
620:     return undefined
621:   }
622:   const messages = record['messages'].map(decodeMessage)
623:   if (messages.some(message => message === undefined)) {
624:     return undefined
625:   }
626:   return {
627:     messages: messages as ReadonlyArray<InferenceMessage>,
628:     ...(model === undefined ? {} : { model }),
629:     ...(stream === undefined ? {} : { stream }),
630:   }
631: }
632: 
633: // GATEWAY-SIDE STABLE PROMPT LAYOUT (book P0-2 / #6084 deliverable 1+2). Build the
634: // outgoing messages as TAGGED blocks so the prefix-cache assembler can order them
635: // STABLE content first (acceptance contract → identity → tool schemas → stable
636: // policy) and NOVEL/volatile content last — the book's rule "novel tokens as late
637: // as possible" so the long shared prefix stays cacheable. Each gateway-injected
638: // block is tagged with its `StableBlockKind`, so ordering is deterministic and
639: // structural (classification of OUR OWN injected blocks, not a keyword match on
640: // user intent — honors the workspace semantic-routing rule).
641: //
642: // Identity injection (STEP 1, the PRIMARY identity mechanism): for every
643: // `openagents/khala-*` request the strong Khala identity system message is part of
644: // the STABLE prefix so the identity rule binds to every Khala consumer and the
645: // post-completion signature guard remains the backstop on top.
646: //
647: // Acceptance-contract injection (EPIC #6017): for a khala-code coding request whose
648: // intent maps to an executable acceptance lane, the contract guidance (the runner's
649: // `window` hooks) is a STABLE prefix block too. Both are additive; the assembler
650: // orders them canonically (acceptance contract leads, then identity) regardless of
651: // append order.
652: const buildTaggedKhalaMessages = (
653:   clientMessages: ReadonlyArray<InferenceMessage>,
654:   requestedModel: string,
655:   autopilotConcierge: AutopilotConciergeRequestConfig | undefined,
656:   // COMPONENT-CHANNEL (issue #6127): inject the closed-catalog system prompt as a
657:   // STABLE prefix block ONLY when the channel is active for this request. Absent /
658:   // false => no injection, prompt byte-identical to before (additive + opt-in).
659:   componentChannelActive?: boolean,
660: ): ReadonlyArray<TaggedPromptMessage> => {
661:   const tagged: Array<TaggedPromptMessage> = []
662: 
663:   // Acceptance-contract stable block (khala-code coding lane only).
664:   if (requestedModel === KHALA_CODE_MODEL_ID) {
665:     const guidance = acceptanceContractGuidanceForRequest({
666:       messages: clientMessages.map(message => ({
667:         content: message.content,
668:         role: message.role,
669:       })),
670:       model: requestedModel,
671:     })
672:     if (guidance !== undefined) {
673:       tagged.push({
674:         message: { content: guidance, role: 'system' },
675:         stableKind: 'acceptanceContract' satisfies StableBlockKind,
676:       })
677:     }
678:   }
679: 
680:   // Identity stable block (every khala-* model).
681:   if (isKhalaModel(requestedModel)) {
682:     tagged.push({
683:       message: { content: KHALA_IDENTITY_SYSTEM_PROMPT, role: 'system' },
684:       stableKind: 'identity' satisfies StableBlockKind,
685:     })
686:   }
687: 
688:   // Autopilot Concierge product prompt (#6148): server-owned model config,
689:   // vertical enum, component catalog metadata, and Output Spec instructions. It
690:   // is injected by the gateway for the virtual model only; callers cannot supply
691:   // arbitrary vertical/system-prompt text.
692:   if (autopilotConcierge !== undefined) {
693:     tagged.push({
694:       message: {
695:         content: buildAutopilotConciergeSystemPrompt(autopilotConcierge),
696:         role: 'system',
697:       },
698:       stableKind: 'otherSystem' satisfies StableBlockKind,
699:     })
700:   }
701: 
702:   // Component-catalog stable block (issue #6127): only when the typed component
703:   // channel is active for this Khala request. The catalog prompt lists the closed
704:   // v1 component set + prop shapes so the model surfaces a card via the fenced
705:   // `oa-component` mechanism that works across all Khala backends.
706:   if (componentChannelActive === true && isKhalaModel(requestedModel)) {
707:     tagged.push({
708:       message: { content: KHALA_COMPONENT_CATALOG_PROMPT, role: 'system' },
709:       stableKind: 'identity' satisfies StableBlockKind,
710:     })
711:   }
712: 
713:   // Client messages: their own `system` messages are stable policy/steer (the
714:   // assembler classifies role==='system' as `otherSystem`, ordered after the
715:   // known stable blocks); everything else is volatile/novel (ordered last).
716:   for (const message of clientMessages) {
717:     tagged.push({ message })
718:   }
719:   return tagged
720: }
721: 
722: const toInferenceRequest = (
723:   body: ChatCompletionsRequestBody,
724:   raw: Record<string, unknown>,
725:   requestedModel: string,
726:   // The session-affinity passthrough params derived from the request's
727:   // cache-affinity key (book P0-2 deliverable 4). MERGED into passthroughParams
728:   // (overriding any stray client copy) so the adapters pin the session to one
729:   // cache-warm replica. Empty for non-Khala / no-affinity requests.
730:   affinityParams: Readonly<Record<string, string>>,
731:   // COMPONENT-CHANNEL (issue #6127): when active, the closed-catalog system prompt
732:   // is injected as a stable prefix block. Default false => no injection.
733:   componentChannelActive?: boolean,
734:   autopilotConcierge?: AutopilotConciergeRequestConfig | undefined,
735: ): InferenceRequest => {
736:   const clientMessages: ReadonlyArray<InferenceMessage> = body.messages
737:   // For a non-Khala model, leave the messages exactly as the client sent them
738:   // (no gateway injection, no reordering) — byte-identical to prior behavior.
739:   // For a Khala model, assemble the cache-optimal stable layout.
740:   const messages = isKhalaModel(requestedModel)
741:     ? assembleStablePromptLayout(
742:         buildTaggedKhalaMessages(
743:           clientMessages,
744:           requestedModel,
745:           autopilotConcierge,
746:           componentChannelActive,
747:         ),
748:       ).messages
749:     : clientMessages
750:   const { messages: _messages, model: _model, stream: _stream, ...rest } = raw
751:   return {
752:     messages,
753:     model: requestedModel,
754:     // Gateway-derived session affinity wins over any stray client copy.
755:     passthroughParams: { ...rest, ...affinityParams },
756:     stream: body.stream === true,
757:   }
758: }
759: 
760: // Resolve the cache-affinity dimensions for a request from the authenticated
761: // account plus client-supplied session / codebase hints (book P0-2 deliverable
762: // 3). The session/codebase hints are read from the OpenAI-style `user` field and
763: // a non-standard `codebase` hint in the raw body, plus the `x-session-affinity` /
764: // `x-codebase` request headers — all OPTIONAL, all bounded fields (deterministic
765: // parse of explicit caller-supplied identifiers, never an intent parse). Only
766: // `account` is required; the others sharpen affinity when present.
767: const resolveCacheAffinity = (
768:   accountRef: string,
769:   raw: Record<string, unknown>,
770:   request: Request,
771: ): Readonly<{
772:   rawKey: string
773:   hash: string
774:   params: Readonly<Record<string, string>>
775: }> => {
776:   const stringField = (value: unknown): string | undefined =>
777:     typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '' ? value.trim() : undefined
778:   const header = (name: string): string | undefined => {
779:     const value = request.headers.get(name)
780:     return value !== null && value.trim() !== '' ? value.trim() : undefined
781:   }
782:   const session =
783:     stringField(raw['user']) ?? header('x-session-affinity') ?? undefined
784:   const codebase =
785:     stringField(raw['codebase']) ?? header('x-codebase') ?? undefined
786:   const rawKey = deriveCacheAffinityKey({
787:     account: accountRef,
788:     ...(session === undefined ? {} : { session }),
789:     ...(codebase === undefined ? {} : { codebase }),
790:   })
791:   return {
792:     hash: hashCacheAffinityKey(rawKey),
793:     params: sessionAffinityParams(deriveSessionAffinityValue(rawKey)),
794:     rawKey,
795:   }
796: }
797: 
798: const defaultId = () => compactRandomId('chatcmpl')
799: 
800: // The public OpenAgents disclosure block (M0 / #6008). Khala virtual models use
801: // it to show which concrete model/worker served one endpoint; Hydralisk raw
802: // model ids use it to disclose owned infrastructure without pretending to be a
803: // Khala-specific coordinator. No prompts, credentials, or chain-of-thought are
804: // exposed.
805: type OpenAgentsReceipt = Readonly<{
806:   requested_model: string
807:   served_model: string
808:   worker: string
809:   lane: string
810:   // Concrete priced supply lane, distinct from the legacy `lane` model class
811:   // (`open` / `gemini` / `claude`). Additive and public-safe; lets OpenAgents
812:   // expose day-zero Hydralisk routing without changing older receipt readers.
813:   supply_lane?: SupplyLane | undefined
814:   routing?:
815:     | Readonly<{
816:         provider_health_score: number | typeof NOT_MEASURED
817:         region: string | typeof NOT_MEASURED
818:         fallback_reason: string | null
819:       }>
820:     | undefined
821:   // `unverified` is the HONEST default for an executable artifact we have not actually
822:   // run yet (EPIC #6017): the regex pre-screen passed but the out-of-Worker headless
823:   // acceptance runner has not executed it, so we do NOT certify it. `test_passed` is
824:   // reserved for an EXECUTED acceptance suite that fully passed.
825:   verification: 'none' | 'test_passed' | 'unverified' | 'failed'
826:   // Whether a real headless acceptance run produced this verdict. False => the
827:   // verdict is the honest pre-screen-only downgrade, not an execution result.
828:   executed?: boolean | undefined
829:   verified?: boolean | undefined
830:   receipt?: string | undefined
831:   receipt_url?: string | undefined
832:   route?: 'coding' | undefined
833:   workers?: ReadonlyArray<string> | undefined
834:   verification_receipt?: string | undefined
835:   verification_command?: string | undefined
836:   scalar_reward?: number | undefined
837:   reward_handoff?: string | undefined
838:   rubric?:
839:     | Readonly<{
840:         ref: string
841:         passed_checks: ReadonlyArray<string>
842:         failed_checks: ReadonlyArray<string>
843:       }>
844:     | undefined
845:   // KHALA REQUEST-TELEMETRY SCORECARD (book P0-1 / Open Q #1-2). The SMALL,
846:   // immediate lifecycle summary — request class, tokens, TTFT, total wall-clock,
847:   // verification class + executed verdict + scalar reward, and a `detailRef`
848:   // pointer to the full dereferenceable record. Non-breaking additive field;
849:   // every numeric is a real measurement or the honest `not_measured` sentinel.
850:   // The full P0-1 record (time split, queue/batch wait, region, cache-affinity
851:   // hash, fallback reason, cost basis / margin bucket / settlement state /
852:   // blockers) is the depth behind the receipt detail, off this hot path.
853:   telemetry?: KhalaTelemetryBlock | undefined
854:   // Bitcoin/Spark settlement on a VERIFIED accepted outcome (#6011, EPIC #6017).
855:   // `settled` is the honest default `false` on the hot path: settlement fires
856:   // ASYNC after an out-of-Worker headless acceptance run verifies the outcome
857:   // (the verdict-callback path settles the worker + validator and flips this to
858:   // `true`), so a fresh completion is `verified:false`/`settled:false` until the
859:   // runner verifies it. The field exists so the receipt shape is stable and a
860:   // settled accepted outcome surfaces `settled:true` + the settlement receipt
861:   // refs alongside `verified`/`scalar_reward`.
862:   settled?: boolean | undefined
863:   settlement_receipts?: ReadonlyArray<string> | undefined
864:   // AUTOPILOT CONCIERGE STRUCTURED ARTIFACTS (#6148). Present ONLY for the
865:   // `openagents/autopilot-concierge` virtual model.
866:   //   - `output_spec` is the 10-section intake Output Spec reliably extracted
867:   //     from the completion (the fenced `oa-output-spec` JSON block, with a
868:   //     markdown-section fallback), so a programmatic consumer reads the
869:   //     accumulated intake state as a STRUCTURED field rather than parsing prose.
870:   //     Absent when the turn surfaced no parseable spec content.
871:   //   - `tools` is the bounded, server-declared Concierge tool set with each
872:   //     tool's review/effect posture and an honest `declared_not_executed`
873:   //     status (live execution is a deferred seam — see autopilot-concierge-tools.ts).
874:   output_spec?: AutopilotConciergeOutputSpec | undefined
875:   tools?: ReadonlyArray<ConciergeToolDeclaration> | undefined
876: }>
877: 
878: const publicInferenceReceiptUrl = (receiptRef: string): string =>
879:   `/api/public/inference/receipts/${encodeURIComponent(receiptRef)}`
880: 
881: const supplyLaneForAdapterId = (adapterId: string): SupplyLane | undefined => {
882:   switch (adapterId) {
883:     case FIREWORKS_ADAPTER_ID:
884:       return 'fireworks'
885:     case HYDRALISK_ADAPTER_ID:
886:     case HYDRALISK_GPT_OSS_120B_ADAPTER_ID:
887:       return 'hydralisk'
888:     case OPENAGENTS_NETWORK_ADAPTER_ID:
889:       return 'openagents-network'
890:     case VERTEX_ANTHROPIC_ADAPTER_ID:
891:       return 'vertex-anthropic'
892:     case VERTEX_GEMINI_ADAPTER_ID:
893:       return 'vertex-gemini'
894:     default:
895:       return undefined
896:   }
897: }
898: 
899: // What the gateway can MEASURE about this request's lifecycle on the hot path
900: // today (book P0-1). All optional: an unmeasured value becomes the honest
901: // `not_measured` sentinel in the telemetry builder — never a fabricated number.
902: type KhalaTelemetryTiming = Readonly<{
903:   // True for the streaming path. Determines the request class (interactive_stream
904:   // vs async_job/batch later) and whether TTFT/ITL are measurable at all.
905:   streamed: boolean
906:   // Total wall-clock from request accept to completion (ms), gateway-edge
907:   // measured. Undefined on a path that did not capture it => sentinel.
908:   totalWallClockMs?: number | undefined
909:   // Time to first token (ms), measurable on the streaming path only.
910:   ttftMs?: number | undefined
911:   // Generation wall-clock (first byte -> last byte, ms) used to DERIVE perceived
912:   // TPS + mean inter-token latency. Streaming only; undefined => those derived
913:   // metrics are the sentinel (we never guess them from total wall-clock).
914:   generationWallClockMs?: number | undefined
915: }>
916: 
917: // Map a khala-code verifier verdict's CLASS onto the telemetry verification
918: // class vocabulary (khala.md §6). Reuses the existing values; never invents one.
919: const telemetryVerificationClass = (
920:   verification: KhalaCodeVerificationVerdict['verification'],
921: ): KhalaVerificationClass => verification
922: 
923: // Map executed-ness + class onto the executed verdict. `not_executed` is the
924: // honest default whenever the headless run did not produce a verdict.
925: const telemetryExecutedVerdict = (
926:   verdict: KhalaCodeVerificationVerdict,
927: ): KhalaExecutedVerdict => {
928:   if (!verdict.executed) {
929:     return 'not_executed'
930:   }
931:   return verdict.verification === 'test_passed' ? 'passed' : 'failed'
932: }
933: 
934: // Derive the request class from the measured shape. Streaming => interactive
935: // stream; otherwise an async/synchronous job. (batch/verifier_run lanes set
936: // their own class once those request paths land; this is the chat path.)
937: const telemetryRequestClass = (streamed: boolean): KhalaRequestClass =>
938:   streamed ? 'interactive_stream' : 'async_job'
939: 
940: const openAgentsReceiptForResult = (
941:   input: Readonly<{
942:     adapterId: string
943:     metering: MeteringOutcome
944:     requestedModel: string
945:     responseId: string
946:     result: InferenceResult
947:     routeMetadata?: DispatchRouteMetadata | undefined
948:     // The executed-acceptance verdict from the out-of-Worker headless runner, when an
949:     // execution actually ran (EPIC #6017). PRESENT => `verified`/`scalarReward` derive
950:     // from EXECUTION. ABSENT (the hot Worker path today, which cannot launch a browser)
951:     // => HONEST DOWNGRADE to `unverified`. Full prod wiring (async sandbox dispatch of
952:     // the runner + receipt backfill) threads a real verdict in here.
953:     acceptance?: AcceptanceVerdict | undefined
954:     // What the gateway measured about this request's lifecycle (book P0-1). When
955:     // absent, every telemetry numeric is the honest `not_measured` sentinel.
956:     timing?: KhalaTelemetryTiming | undefined
957:     // The RAW cache-affinity key for this request (book P0-2 deliverable 3:
958:     // account/session/codebase). It is hashed into `cacheAffinityKeyHash` by the
959:     // telemetry builder and NEVER stored/exposed raw. Undefined → no affinity key
960:     // applied → the hash is null and a blocker records why.
961:     cacheAffinityKeyRaw?: string | undefined
962:   }>,
963: ): OpenAgentsReceipt | undefined => {
964:   const supplyLane =
965:     lookupModel(input.result.servedModel)?.lane ??
966:     supplyLaneForAdapterId(input.adapterId) ??
967:     lookupModel(input.requestedModel)?.lane
968:   const isKhala = isKhalaModel(input.requestedModel)
969:   if (!isKhala && supplyLane !== 'hydralisk') {
970:     return undefined
971:   }
972: 
973:   const base = {
974:     lane: classifyModel(input.requestedModel),
975:     requested_model: input.requestedModel,
976:     served_model: input.result.servedModel,
977:     ...(supplyLane === undefined ? {} : { supply_lane: supplyLane }),
978:     worker: input.adapterId,
979:   } satisfies Omit<OpenAgentsReceipt, 'verification'>
980:   const routing = {
981:     fallback_reason: input.routeMetadata?.fallbackReason ?? null,
982:     provider_health_score:
983:       input.routeMetadata?.providerHealthScore ?? NOT_MEASURED,
984:     region: input.routeMetadata?.region ?? NOT_MEASURED,
985:   } satisfies NonNullable<OpenAgentsReceipt['routing']>
986: 
987:   if (!isKhala) {
988:     return {
989:       ...base,
990:       routing,
991:       verification: 'none' as const,
992:     }
993:   }
994: 
995:   // Shared telemetry inputs measurable for EVERY Khala request (tokens from the
996:   // provider usage, latency from the gateway edge). The cache-affinity key (book
997:   // P0-2 deliverable 3) is now wired: when present it is hashed into the receipt
998:   // and the cached-input dimension is populated from provider usage; when absent
999:   // (no session/codebase context) the hash is null and a blocker records why.
1000:   const streamed = input.timing?.streamed ?? false
1001:   const settlementBlockers =
1002:     input.metering.receiptRef === null ? ['cost_not_measured'] : []
1003:   const cacheAffinityKeyRaw =
1004:     input.cacheAffinityKeyRaw !== undefined &&
1005:     input.cacheAffinityKeyRaw.trim() !== ''
1006:       ? input.cacheAffinityKeyRaw
1007:       : undefined
1008:   const cacheBlockers =
1009:     cacheAffinityKeyRaw === undefined ? ['cache_affinity_key_not_resolved'] : []
1010: 
1011:   if (input.requestedModel !== KHALA_CODE_MODEL_ID) {
1012:     // Non-coding Khala lane: no verifier pass (verification class `none`). Still
1013:     // carries the full lifecycle telemetry summary (tokens + latency + request
1014:     // class) so EVERY Khala response is measurable, not just khala-code.
1015:     const settlementState: KhalaSettlementState = 'not_applicable'
1016:     const telemetry = buildKhalaTelemetryBlock(
1017:       {
1018:         completionTokens: input.result.usage.completionTokens,
1019:         costBasisMsat: undefined,
1020:         executedVerdict: 'not_executed',
1021:         priceMsat: undefined,
1022:         promptTokens: input.result.usage.promptTokens,
1023:         provider: input.adapterId,
1024:         ...(input.routeMetadata?.providerHealthScore === undefined
1025:           ? {}
1026:           : { providerHealthScore: input.routeMetadata.providerHealthScore }),
1027:         requestClass: telemetryRequestClass(streamed),
1028:         requestId: input.responseId,
1029:         requestedModel: input.requestedModel,
1030:         route: classifyModel(input.requestedModel),
1031:         servedModel: input.result.servedModel,
1032:         ...(input.routeMetadata?.region === undefined
1033:           ? {}
1034:           : { region: input.routeMetadata.region }),
1035:         fallbackReason: input.routeMetadata?.fallbackReason ?? null,
1036:         ...(cacheAffinityKeyRaw === undefined ? {} : { cacheAffinityKeyRaw }),
1037:         ...(input.result.usage.cachedPromptTokens === undefined
1038:           ? {}
1039:           : { cachedInputTokens: input.result.usage.cachedPromptTokens }),
1040:         ...(input.timing?.generationWallClockMs === undefined
1041:           ? {}
1042:           : { generationWallClockMs: input.timing.generationWallClockMs }),
1043:         ...(input.timing?.totalWallClockMs === undefined
1044:           ? {}
1045:           : { totalWallClockMs: input.timing.totalWallClockMs }),
1046:         ...(input.timing?.ttftMs === undefined
1047:           ? {}
1048:           : { ttftMs: input.timing.ttftMs }),
1049:         scalarReward: undefined,
1050:         settlementState,
1051:         totalTokens: input.result.usage.totalTokens,
1052:         verificationClass: 'none',
1053:         blockerRefs: [...settlementBlockers, ...cacheBlockers],
1054:       },
1055:       input.metering.receiptRef === null
1056:         ? null
1057:         : publicInferenceReceiptUrl(input.metering.receiptRef),
1058:     )
1059:     // AUTOPILOT CONCIERGE artifacts (#6148): surface the structured Output Spec
1060:     // (reliably extracted from the completion) and the declared bounded tool set
1061:     // on the disclosure block, so a programmatic `/api/v1/chat/completions`
1062:     // consumer reads them as structured fields. Only for the concierge virtual
1063:     // model; every other Khala model is byte-identical to before.
1064:     const conciergeOutputSpec = isAutopilotConciergeModel(input.requestedModel)
1065:       ? extractConciergeOutputSpec(input.result.content)
1066:       : undefined
1067:     return {
1068:       ...base,
1069:       routing,
1070:       telemetry,
1071:       verification: 'none' as const,
1072:       ...(isAutopilotConciergeModel(input.requestedModel)
1073:         ? { tools: autopilotConciergeToolDeclarations() }
1074:         : {}),
1075:       ...(conciergeOutputSpec === undefined
1076:         ? {}
1077:         : { output_spec: conciergeOutputSpec }),
1078:     }
1079:   }
1080: 
1081:   const verdict: KhalaCodeVerificationVerdict = verifyKhalaCodeCompletion({
1082:     content: input.result.content,
1083:     meteringReceiptRef: input.metering.receiptRef,
1084:     requestId: input.responseId,
1085:     servedModel: input.result.servedModel,
1086:     worker: input.adapterId,
1087:     ...(input.acceptance === undefined ? {} : { acceptance: input.acceptance }),
1088:   })
1089:   const receipt = input.metering.receiptRef ?? verdict.receiptRef

// (EPIC #5474, #5476). This is the FOUNDATION the rest of the inference build
// plugs into:
//
//   - flag-gated INERT by default (INFERENCE_GATEWAY_ENABLED, default off)
//   - per-account API-key auth (reuses the agent bearer-token credential)
//   - read-only credit-balance gate (rejects on insufficient balance; #5477
//     owns the real decrement/top-up paths, not this route)
//   - provider-adapter seam dispatch (registry resolves model -> adapter; ships
//     wired to the stub/echo adapter so the route works end-to-end in tests)
//   - metering-hook seam (#5477 decrements credits from provider `usage`;
//     stubbed here as a no-op/log)
//
// Streaming and non-streaming shapes are both supported. The response mirrors
// the OpenAI Chat Completions contract so off-the-shelf clients work by changing
// only base URL + key. Anthropic Messages compatibility is a parallel surface;
// a clean spot is left for it (see ANTHROPIC SEAM below) but is out of scope for
// #5476.
import { Effect } from 'effect'

import { recordFromUnknown } from '../json-boundary'
import { noStoreJsonResponse } from '../http/responses'
import {
  compactRandomId,
  currentEpochMillis,
  currentEpochSeconds,
} from '../runtime-primitives'
// PURE dispatch producer only — enqueues an out-of-Worker verification job; never
// imports the headless runner (playwright) into the Worker bundle (EPIC #6017).
import {
  type AcceptanceJobQueue,
  enqueueAcceptanceJob,
} from './acceptance-dispatch'
// PURE verdict type only — the route NEVER imports the headless runner (playwright)
// into the Worker bundle. The runner executes out of the Worker; its verdict shape is
// the only thing the route consumes (EPIC #6017).
import { type AcceptanceVerdict } from './acceptance-runner/verdict'
import {
  acceptanceContractGuidanceForRequest,
  intentToAcceptanceSpec,
} from './acceptance-spec'
import {
  type AutopilotConciergeRequestConfig,
  buildAutopilotConciergeSystemPrompt,
  isAutopilotConciergeModel,
  resolveAutopilotConciergeConfig,
} from './autopilot-concierge-model'
import {
  type AutopilotConciergeOutputSpec,
  extractConciergeOutputSpec,
} from './autopilot-concierge-output-spec'
import {
  type ConciergeToolDeclaration,
  autopilotConciergeToolDeclarations,
} from './autopilot-concierge-tools'
